text.ar: التاسع : أن يدخل الحائر المقدس من الباب الشرقي على ما أمر الصادق صَلَواتُ الله وسَلامُهُ عَلَيهِ يوسف الكناسي

text.en: Tenth: Ibn Qawlawayh has reported that Imam al-Sadiq (‘a) said to al-Mufazzal ibn ‘Umar, “If you reach the tomb of al-Husayn (‘a), you may stop at the gate of the courtyard and say the following sentences, for each sentence brings you a share of Almighty Allah’s mercy:
text.ar: العاشر : عن ابن قولويه عن الصادق عليه‌السلام أنّه قال لمفضّل بن عمر يا مفضّل إذا أتيت قبر الحسين بن علي عليهما‌السلام فقف بالباب الروضة وقل هذه الكلمات فإنّ لك بكلّ كلمة منها كفلاً من رحمة الله فقلت : ما هي جعلت فداك؟ قال : تقول :

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا وَارِثَ آدَمَ صَفْوَةِ ٱللَّهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O inheritor of Adam the choice of Allah.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا وَارِثَ نُوحٍ نَبِيِّ ٱللَّهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O inheritor of Noah the prophet of Allah.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا وَارِثَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ خَلِيلِ ٱللَّهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O inheritor of Abraham the friend of Allah.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا وَارِثَ مُوسَىٰ كَلِيمِ ٱللَّهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O inheritor of Moses the spoken by Allah.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا وَارِثَ عِيسَىٰ رُوحِ ٱللَّهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O inheritor of Jesus the spirit of Allah.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا وَارِثَ مُحَمَّدٍ حَبِيبِ ٱللَّهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O inheritor of Mu¦ammad the most-beloved of Allah.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا وَارِثَ عَلِيٍّ وَصِيِّ رَسُولِ ٱللَّهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O inheritor of `Al¢ the successor of Allah’s Messenger.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا وَارِثَ ٱلحَسَنِ ٱلرَّضِيِّ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O inheritor of al-°asan the Well-pleased.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيكَ يَا وَارِثَ فَاطِمَةَ بِنْتَ رَسُولِ ٱللَّهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O inheritor of F¡§imah the daughter of Allah’s Messenger.
Peace be upon you, O martyr and veracious.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ أيُّهَا ٱلْوَصِيُّ ٱلْبَارُّ ٱلْتَّقِيُّ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O obedient and pious successor.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَىٰ ٱلأرْوَاحِ ٱلَّتي حَلَّتْ بِفِنَائِكَ وَأنَاخَتْ بِرَحْلِكَ

translation.en: Peace be upon the souls that resided in your yard and settled next to your place.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَىٰ مَلاَئِكَةِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلْمُحْدِقِينَ بِكَ

translation.en: Peace be upon the angels of Allah who keep eyes on you.
I bear witness that you performed the prayers,
defrayed the poor-rate,
enjoined the right,
forbade the evil,

segments.arabic: وَعَبَدْتَ ٱللَّهَ مُخْلِصاً حَتَّىٰ أتَاكَ ٱلْيَقِينُ

translation.en: and served Allah sincerely until death came upon you.
Peace and Allah’s mercy and blessings be upon you.

text.en: You may then walk towards the tomb. With each step you make, you will be awarded the reward of one who sacrifices his blood for the sake of Allah. When you approach the tomb, you may wipe your hand over it and say:
text.ar: ثم تمضي إلى القبر ولك بكل خطوة تخطوها أجر المتشحط بدمه في سبيل الله فإذا اقتربت من القبر فامسحه بيدك وقل :

segments.arabic: السَّلامُ عَلَيْكَ يا حُجَّةَ الله فِي أَرْضِهِ وَسَمائِهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O Allah’s argument in His lands and heavens.

text.en: You may then go for offering the prayer. For each unit of prayer that you offer near the tomb, you will have the reward of one who went on one thousand times of hajj and one thousand times of ‘umrah, manumitted one thousand slaves for the sake of Allah, and stood in the line of a missioned Prophet one thousand times.
text.ar: ثم تمضي إلى صلاتك ولك بكل ركعة ركعتها عنده كثواب من حجّ ألف حجّة واعتمر ألف عمرة وأعتق في سبيل الله ألف رقبة ، وكأنَّما وقف في سبيل الله ألف مرة مع نبيّ مرسل

text.en:
Eleventh: Abu-Sa’id al-Mada’ini is reported to have said that he, once, came to Imam al-Sadiq (‘a) and asked, “May I go for visiting the tomb of al-Husayn?” The Imam (‘a) answered, “Yes, you may. Go for visiting the tomb of al-Husayn the son of Allah’s Messenger, the most immaculate of all the immaculate ones, the most infallible of all the infallible ones, and the most munificent of all those who act munificently. After you visit him, you may repeat the glorification words of the Commander of the Faithful (Amir al-Mu’minin) one thousand times while you are at the head’s side and repeat the glorification words of the Luminous Lady (i.e. al-Zahra’) one thousand time while you are at the legs’ side. You may then offer a two-unit prayer reciting <ref=1:36>Surah Al-Yasin</ref>  and <ref=1:55>Surah Al-Rahman</ref>
 in that prayer. If you do so, you will earn a great reward.”When the reporter asked the Imam (‘a) to teach him the glorification words of the Commander of the Faithful and those of the Luminous Lady, the Imam said: The glorification words of (Imam) ‘Ali are the following:
text.ar: الحادي عشر : روي عن أبي سعيد المدائني قال : أتيت الصادق عليه‌السلام فسألته أأذهب إلى زيارة قبر الحسين عليه‌السلام فأجاب : بلى اذهب إلى زيارة قبر الحسين عليه‌السلام ابن رسول الله صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله أطيب الطيبين وأطهر الطاهرين وأحسن المحسنين. فإذا زرته فسبّح عند رأسه بتسبيح أمير المؤمنين عليه‌السلام ألف مرة وسبّح عند رجليه بتسبيح الزهراء عليها‌السلام ألف مرّة ثم صل عنده ركعتين تقرأ فيهما سورة <ref=1:36>یس</ref> و <ref=1:55>الرحمن</ref> ، فإذا فعلت ذلك كتب الله لك ثواب ذلك إن شاء الله تعالي.قلت : جعلت فداك ، علمني تسبيح علي وفاطمة عليها‌السلام قال : بلى يا أبا سعيد تسبيح علي صَلَواتُ الله وسَلامُهُ عَلَيهِ هو :

translation.en: Glory be to Him Whose reserves never run out.
Glory be to Him Whose signs are never exterminated.

segments.arabic: سُبْحَانَ ٱلَّذِي لاَ يَفْنَىٰ مَا عِنْدَهُ

translation.en: Glory be to Him Whose possessions never expire.

segments.arabic: سُبْحَانَ ٱلَّذِي لاَ يُشْرِكُ أحَداً فِي حُكْمِهِ

translation.en: Glory be to Him Who never betakes to Himself a partner in His judgments.
Glory be to Him Whose honor never vanishes.
Glory be to Him Whose duration (of existence) is never interrupted.
Glory be to Him save Whom there is no god.

text.en: The glorification words of Fatimah (‘a) are the following:
text.ar: وَتسبيح فاطمة عليها‌السلام هو :

translation.en: Glory be to the Lord of majesty and exalted greatness.
Glory be to the Lord of honor and sublime authority.
Glory be to the Lord of eternal, splendid sovereignty.
Glory be to the Lord of splendor and beauty.
Glory be to Him Who is clad in light and dignity.

segments.arabic: سُبحَانَ مَنْ يَرَىٰ أثَرَ ٱلَّنمْلِ فِي ٱلصَّفَا

translation.en: Glory be to Him Who makes out the footprints of the ant on the stone
and (the exact time and place of) the bird dipping

text.en: Twelfth: It is recommended to perform the obligatory prayers and offer the supererogatory prayers (nafilah) near the tomb of Imam al-Husayn (‘a), because prayers at that place are admissible. Sayyid Ibn Tawus further says, “Try your best to perform all your obligatory prayers and offer all your supererogatory prayers inside the Ha’ir (i.e. courtyard of Imam al-Husayn’s holy shrine), because the reward of one obligatory prayer that is performed therein is equal to the reward of going on ritual pilgrimage (hajj) and the reward of offering a supererogatory prayer therein is equal to the reward of going on recommended pilgrimage (‘umrah).”
text.ar: الثاني عشر : أن تصلي الفرائض والنوافل عند قبر الحسين عليه‌السلام فإن الصلاة عنده مقبولة. وقال السيد ابن طاووس (رض) : اجتهد في أن تؤدي صلواتك كلها فريضة كانت أو نافلة في الحائر ، فقد روي أنّ الفريضة عنده تعدل الحجّ والنافلة تعدل العمرة

text.en: Thirteenth: Be it known to you that the most important act in the pure shrine of Imam al-Husayn (‘a) is to pray Almighty Allah, because granting of requests under the supreme dome of his shrine is one of the distinctive returns that Almighty Allah has exclusively given to Imam al-Husayn (‘a) as one compensation for his martyrdom. Seizing this opportunity, a pilgrim is advised not to show any shortcoming in entreating Almighty Allah, turning to Him, repenting before Him, and providing one’s needs to Him.Through the numerous forms of ziyarah that are said during visiting Imam al-Husayn’s tomb, many supplicatory prayers, holding high meanings, have been mentioned to be said there. However, briefness has not allowed us to cite all these supplicatory prayers here. It is most advisable to say the supplications of al-Sahifah al-Sajjadiyyah, because they are the most preferable. At the end of this section and immediately after the comprehensive forms of Ziyarah, I will cite a comprehensive supplicatory prayer that is said in all of the holy shrines. Besides, another, most comprehensive, supplicatory prayer that is said in all of the holy shrines will be also cited in this book. It is therefore advised not to miss these supplicatory prayers.However, in order to avoid making this part of the book free of any supplicatory prayer, let us cite the following one, which is required to be said while raising one’s hands towards the sky:
text.ar: الثالث عشر : إعلم أن أهم الاعمال في الروضة الطاهرة للحسين عليه‌السلام هو الدعاء ، فإن إجابة الدعاء تحت قبته السامية هي مما خوّله الله الحسين عليه‌السلام عوضا عن الشهادة فعلى الزائر أن يغتنم ذلك ولايتوانى في التضرّع إلى الله والإنابة والتوبة وعرض الحوائج عليه. وقد وردت في خلال زيارته عليه‌السلام أدعية كثيرة ذات مضامين عالية لم يسمح لنا الاختصار بإيرادها هنا ، والأفضل أن يدعو بدعوات (الصحيفة الكاملة) ما وسعه الدعاء فإنها أفضل الأدعية. ونحن سنذكر دعاءً يدعى به في جميع الروضات المقدسة في أواخر هذا الباب بعد ذكر الزيارات الجامعة ، واعلم انا سنذكر في دعاءً هو أجمع الدعوات يدعى به في روضات الأئمة عليهم‌السلام فلا تغفل عنه ، واحترازاً عن خلو المقام نثبت هنا دعاءً وجيزاً ورد في خلال بعض الزيارات وهو أنه تقول في ذلك الحرم الشريف رافعاً يديك إلى السَّماء :

translation.en: O Allah, You do see my place,
hear my words,
and see you situation, imploration,
and seeking shelter with the tomb of Your Argument and Your Prophet’s son.
You, O my Master, have already known my needs
and none of my manners can be hidden from You.
I am now turning my face towards You in the name of Your Prophet’s son,
You Argument, and Your trustee.

segments.arabic: وَقَدْ أتَيْتُكَ مُتَقَرِّباً بِهِ إِلَيْكَ وَإِلىٰ رَسُولِكَ

translation.en: I have come to You seeking nearness to You in his name and in the name of Your Messenger.
So, (please), in his name, make me worthy of regard with You
in this world and in the Hereafter and (make me) of those drawn near to You.
And grant me, due to this visit of mine, what I hope,
endue me with my wish,
favor me with my desire and aspiration,
settle for me my needs,
do not reject me with disappointment,
do not cut off my hope (in You),
do not let down my prayer,

segments.arabic: وَعَرِّفْنِي ٱلإِجَابَةِ فِي جَمِيعِ مَا دَعَوْتُكَ

translation.en: show me proofs of Your response to all that which I prayed You for,
including the affairs of my religion, worldly life, and the Hereafter,
include me with Your servants
from whom You have warded off misfortunes, diseases,
seditious matters, and calamities;
those whom You make live in wellbeing,
cause to die in wellbeing,
allow to enter Paradise in wellbeing,
and save from Hellfire in wellbeing;
and, out of Your favor, lead me
to the achievement of what I hope for regarding myself,
my wife, my sons, my brethren-in-faith, my properties,
and whatever bliss You have endowed me;
O most merciful of all those who show mercy!

text.en: Fourteenth: Among the rites to be done in the holy shrine of Imam al-Husayn (‘a) is to invoke Almighty Allah’s blessings upon him. It is thus reported to stand behind the tomb at the side of the two shoulders and to invoke Almighty Allah’s blessings upon the Holy Prophet (s) and Imam al-Husayn (‘a).In his book of Misbah al-Za’ir, Sayyid Ibn Tawus has reported the following form of invocation of blessings upon Imam al-Husayn (‘a) within some forms of ziyarah:
text.ar: الرابع عشر : من أعمال حرم الحسين عليه‌السلام الصلاة عليه ، وروي أنّك تقف خلف القبر عند كتفه الشّريف وتصلّي على النبي صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله وعلى الحسين (صَلوات الله عَلَيهِ). وقد أورد السيد ابن طاووس في (مصباح الزائر) في خلال بعض الزيارات هذه الصلاة عليه :

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ

translation.en: O Allah, (please) send blessings upon Mu¦ammad and the Household of Mu¦ammad
and send blessings upon al-°usayn, the wronged, the martyred,
the victim of shed tears, and the captive of agonies
with ever-increasing, pure, and sacred blessings
the first of which ascend and the last of which never run out;
with the best manner that You have ever conferred upon any
of the descendants of the Prophets and Messengers,
O Lord of the worlds!
O Allah, send blessings upon the Imam, the martyr,
the slain, the wronged, the disappointed,
the leading master, the worshipper, the abstemious,
the successor, the vicegerent, the leader, the veracious,
the pure, the immaculate, the superb, the blessed,
the amiable, the pleased, the pious, the guide, the well-guided,
the abstemious, the defender (of Islam), the striver, the knowledgeable,

segments.arabic: إِمَامِ ٱلْهُدَىٰ سِبْطِ ٱلرَّسُولِ وَقُرَّةِ عَيْنِ ٱلْبَتُولِ

translation.en: the leader of true guidance, the grandson of the Messenger, and the delight of the eye of the Chaste Lady.
O Allah, send blessings upon my master and chief
for he acted obediently to You,
warned against disobeying You,
made all efforts to attain Your pleasure,
warmly received belief in You,
rejected all excuses against You,
called the servants to You secretly and openly,
showed them the way to You,
undertook in obedience to You the mission
of demolishing inequity with rectitude
and refreshing the traditions in the (light of the) Book.
So, he lived in fatigue for the sake of Your pleasure

segments.arabic: وَمَضَىٰ عَلَىٰ طَاعَتِكَ وَفِي أوْلِيَائِكَ مَكْدُوحاً

translation.en: and passed away in obedience to You, working hard for the sake of Your Intimate Servants.
He thus joined You missed (by the believers).

segments.arabic: لَمْ يَعْصِكَ فِي لَيْلٍ وَلاَ نَهَارٍ

translation.en: He never disobeyed You; neither at night nor in day;
rather, he, for Your sake, strove against the hypocrites and the unbelievers.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ فَٱجْزِهِ خَيْرَ جَزَاءِ ٱلصَّادِقِينَ ٱلأبْرَارِ

translation.en: So, O Allah, (please) award him with the best reward of the veracious and the righteous
and multiply the chastisement and the punishment for those who slew him.

segments.arabic: فَقَدْ قَاتَلَ كَرِيماً وَقُتِلَ مَظْلُوماً وَمَضَىٰ مَرْحُوماً

translation.en: Verily, he fought with nobility, was slain wrongly, and passed away enjoying mercy.

segments.arabic: يَقُولُ أنَا ٱبْنُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ مُحَمَّدٍ

translation.en: He said to them, “I am the son of Allah’s Messenger Mu¦ammad
and the son of him who gave alms and served Allah.
Yet, they slew him clearly intentionally.
They slew him because of his faith;
therefore, they obeyed Satan
and never cared for the All-beneficent Lord.
So, O Allah, (please) send upon my master and chief
such blessings by which You raise his eminence,
make prevalent his issue,
and hasten his victory.

segments.arabic: وَٱخْصُصْهُ بِأفْضَلِ قِسَمِ ٱلْفَضَائِلِ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَامَةِ

translation.en: And (please) endue him on the Resurrection day with the best of Your favors,
increase his honor to the highest degree of the Supreme Rank,
take him higher to the highest rank of the honored ones,
raise him in the honor of Your mercy
to the honor of the most favorable ones
in the Highest Level,
grant him the right of mediation, the loftiest position,
the superiority, the excellence,
and the abundant privilege.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ فَٱجْزِهِ عَنَّا أفْضَلَ مَا جَازَيْتَ إِمَاماً عَنْ رَعِيَّتِهِ

translation.en: O Allah, (please) award him on behalf of us with the most excellent reward that You have ever given to a leader on behalf of his subjects

segments.arabic: وَصَلِّ عَلَىٰ سَيِّدِي وَمَوْلاَيَ كُلَّمَا ذُكِرَ

translation.en: and bless my master and chief whenever he is mentioned
and whenever he is not.
O my master and chief,
include me with your party and group
and ask my and your Lord to release me as grant from Him to you,

segments.arabic: فَإِنَّ لَكَ عِنْدَ ٱللَّهِ جَاهاً وَقَدْراً

translation.en: for you verily enjoy a special esteem and value with Allah
as well as a lofty standing:
if you ask, He gives you
and if you intercede, He admits your intercession.
By Allah I ask you to regard me, your slave and servant.
Do not desert me at the time of ordeals and horrors

segments.arabic: لِسُوءِ عَمَلِي وَقَبِيحِ فِعْلِي وَعَظِيمِ جُرْمِي

translation.en: because of my evildoing, misconduct, and enormous offenses.
You are verily my hope and my wish,
I have confidence in you, I rely upon you,
and you are my means to Allah, my and your Lord.
No means that has been made to Allah
is greater in status,
more incumbent in sanctity,
or loftier in eminence
than yours, the Ahl al-Bayt, in the sight of Allah.
May Allah never cause me to lag behind you because of my sins
and may He join me with you in the perpetual abode of Paradise
that He has prepared for you and your loyalists.
Verily, He is the best of all those who may forgive and the most merciful of all those who show mercy.
O Allah, (please) convey to my master and chief
abundant greetings and salutations
and convey to us his response to our greetings.
You are verily All-magnanimous and All-generous.
And bless him whenever blessing is mentioned

segments.arabic: وَكُلَّمَا لَمْ يُذْكَرْ يَا رَبَّ ٱلْعَالَمِينَ

translation.en: and whenever it is not mentioned, O Lord of the worlds!

text.en: Fifteenth: Among the recommended acts in the shrine of Imam al-Husayn (‘a) is to say the supplicatory prayer of the oppressed against the oppressor. In other words, one who has been exposed to wronging by an oppressor is advised to pray Almighty Allah with this supplicatory prayer in the holy shrine of Imam al-Husayn (‘a). This supplicatory prayer, which has been recorded by Shaykh al-Tusi in his book of Misbah al-Mutahajjid to be one of the recommended acts on Fridays, is as follows:
text.ar: الخامس عشر : من أعمال الروضة المنورة دعاء المظلوم على الظالم ، أي ينبغي لمن بغى عليه باغٍ أن يدعو بهذا الدعاء في ذلك الحرم الشريف وهو ما أورده شيخ الطائفة (رض) في (مصباح المتهجد) في أعمال الجمعة ، قال : ويستحب أن يدعو بدعاء المظلوم عند قبر أبي عبد الله عليه‌السلام وهو :

translation.en: O Allah, I obtain my dignity from following Your religion
and I am honored because of You have guided me,
but [so-and-so] is humiliating me by his evil,
insulting me by harming me,
imputing dishonor to me because I am loyal to Your Representatives,
and charging me with false claims.
I have come to the center of supplication
and the guarantee of response.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍٍ

translation.en: O Allah, (please) send blessings upon Muhammad and his Household
and support me against him at this very hour! At this very hour!

text.en: You may then throw yourself on the grave and say,
text.ar: ثم تنكب على القبر وتقول :

translation.en: O my master and leader,
(I have been) oppressed and (I am) seeking your support against him who has oppressed me.
Grant me support! Grant me support!

text.en: The last statement may be repeated as many times as one breath may take.
text.ar: حتى ينقطع النفس

text.en: Sixteenth: Among the acts in the holy shrine of Imam al-Husayn (‘a) is to say the following supplicatory prayer, which has been reported by Ibn Fahad in his book of ‘Uddat al-Da’i:He who wants his request to be granted by Almighty Allah may stop at the side of Imam al-Husayn’s head and say the following words:
text.ar: السادس عشر : من أعمال ذلك الحرم الشريف الدعاء الذي رواه ابن فهد (رض) في (عدة الداعي) عن الصادق عليه‌السلام قال : من كان له إلى الله تعالى حاجة فليقف عند رأس الحسين عليه‌السلام ويقول :

translation.en: O Abu-`Abdullah,
I bear witness that you can now see my situation
and hear my words
and that you are alive with Your Lord and you are receiving sustenance from Him.

segments.arabic: فَٱسْألْ رَبَّكَ وَرَبِّي فِي قَضَاءِ حَوَائِجِي

translation.en: So (please) ask your and my Lord to grant me my requests.

text.en: He who says this supplication will have his requested granted by Almighty Allah, God willing.
text.ar: فإنه يقضي حاجته إن شاء الله تعالى

text.en: Seventeenth: Among the other recommended acts in Imam al-Husayn’s holy shrine is to offer a two-unit prayer at the side of the head, reciting Surah al-Fatihah and <ref=1:55>Surah Al-Rahman</ref> in the first unit and Surah al-Fatihah and <ref=1:67>Surah Al-Mulk</ref> in the second.On the word of Sayyid Ibn Tawus, one who offers this prayer will be granted the reward of having performed an admitted and acceptable ritual pilgrimage (hajj) with the Holy Prophet (s) for twenty times.
text.ar: السابع عشر : من جملة الاعمال في ذلك الحرم الشريف الصلاة عند الرأس المقدس ركعتين بسورة <ref=1:55>الرحمن</ref> وسورة <ref=1:67>الملک</ref> . روى السيِّد ابن طاووس (رض) أن من صلاها كتب الله له خمساً وعشرين حجَّةً مقبولةً مبرورة مع رسول الله صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله

text.en: Eighteenth: Istikharah (i.e. asking Almighty Allah to make one choose for the best of two or more choices through a certain process when one fails to decide on a certain choice) is one of the most recommended acts to be done under the supreme dome of Imam al-Husayn’s holy shrine. According to ‘Allamah al-Majlisi who quotes from the book of Qurb al-Isnad by al-Himyari, Imam al-Sadiq (‘a) is reported through an authentic chain of authority to have said, “If a servants repeats the process of Istikharah one hundred times next to the side of the head of Imam al-Husayn (‘a) and says:
text.ar: الثامن عشر : من الاعمال تحت تلك القبّة السامية الاستخارة ، وصفتها على ما أوردها العلامة المجلسي (رض) ومصدر الرّواية كتاب (قرب الإسناد) للحميري قال بسند صحيح عن الصادق عليه‌السلام قال : مااستخار الله عزَّ وجلَّ عبدٌ في أمر قطّ مائة مرة يقف عند رأس الحسين (صَلّوات اللهِ عليهِ) ويقول :

segments.arabic: الحَمْدُ للهِ وَلا إِلهَ إِلاّ الله وَسُبْحانَ اللهِ

translation.en: All praise be to Allah. There is no god save Allah.All glory be to Allah.

text.en: and then praises Almighty Allah, professes that there is no god save Him, all glory belong to Him alone, all magnificence is His alone, commends Him by suitable statements, and then asks Him to choose the best for him one hundred times - if he does all that, Almighty Allah shall most certainly inspire him to choose the best of the two choices.”According to another tradition, the process of Istikharah is to repeat the following statement one hundred times:
text.ar: فيحمد الله ويهلّله ويسبِّحه ويمجِّده ويثني عليه بما هو أهله ويستخيره مائة مرة ؛ إِلاّ رماه الله تبارك وتعالى بأخير الأمرين. وعلى رواية أخرى : يستخير الله مائة مرة قائلاً :

segments.arabic: أَسْتَخِيرُ الله بِرَحْمَتِهِ خِيَرَةً فِي عافِيَةٍ

translation.en: I ask Allah to guide me to decide on the best choice that achieves me wellbeing, out of His mercy.

text.en: Nineteenth: The lofty and great Shaykh Abu’l-Qasim Ja’far ibn Qawlawayh al-Qummi, may Allah have mercy upon him, has reported Imam al-Sadiq (‘a) to have said, “When you visit Abu-’Abdullah al-Husayn (‘a), you should keep silent except for good wording. The recording angels of days and nights present themselves before the angels of Imam al-Husayn’s holy shrine to shake hands with them, but the angels of Imam al-Husayn’s shrine cannot respond to their greetings because they are too busy weeping for Imam al-Husayn to have a chance to respond to these angels. The angels of Imam al-Husayn’s shrine never stop weeping and wailing except at midday and at dawn. The recording angels therefore wait for these two times to come and talk to these angels and ask them about some matters related to the heavens. Thus, the angels of Imam al-Husayn’s shrine do not stop praying and weeping all the time between these two hours (of midday and dawn).”
text.ar:
التاسع عشر : روى الشّيخ الاجلّ الكامل أبو القاسم جعفر بن قولويه القمّي (رض) عن أبي
عبدالله عليه‌السلام أنّه قال : إذا زرتم أبا عبد الله الحسين عليه‌السلام فالزموا الصمت إِلاّ من خير وإنّ ملائكة اللّيل والنّهار من الحفظة يحضرون عند الملائكة الذين هم بالحائر فتصافحهم فلا يجيبونها من شدَّة البكاء فينتظرونهم حتى تزول الشمس وحتى ينور الفجر ثم يكلمونهم ويسألونهم عن أشياء من أمر السماء فأمّا ما بين هذين الوقتين فإنّهم لا ينطقون ولا يفترون عن البكاء والدعاء.

text.en: Twentieth: Sayyid Ibn Tawus says that it is recommended for those who intend to leave the holy shrine of Imam al-Husayn (‘a) after they have accomplished visiting his tomb to throw themselves on the tomb, kiss it, and say the following words:
text.ar: العشرون : قال السيد ابن طاووس (رض) يستحب للمر إذا فرغ من زيارته عليه‌السلام وأراد الخروج من الرّوضة المقدسة أن ينكب على الضريح ويُقبِّله ويقول :

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O my master.
Peace be upon you, O Allah’s Argument (against His creatures).
Peace be upon you, O Allah’s choice.
Peace be upon you, O Allah’s select.
Peace be upon you, O victim of thirst.
Peace be upon you, O strangest of all strangers.
Peace be upon you; this is the greeting of one who has to bid farewell to you
although he is neither bored with you nor tired of you.
If I leave (you), this is not because of weariness;

segments.arabic: وَإِنْ أقِمْ فَلاَ عَنْ سُوءِ ظَنٍّ بِمَا وَعَدَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلصَّابِرِينَ

translation.en: and if I reside, this is not because of mistrusting the promise that Allah has made for the serene ones.

segments.arabic: لاََ جَعَلَهُ ٱللَّهُ آخِرَ ٱلْعَهْدِ مِنِّي لِزِيَارَتِكَ

translation.en: May Allah not decide this time of my visit to be the last.
May He endue me with more chances to return to your shrine,
to reside in your courtyard,
and to stay in your sanctuary.
To Him do I pray to make me delightful with you
and to include me with you in this world and in the Hereafter.

name: Method of Visiting of Imam Husain (as)

text.en: general forms of Ziyarah of Imam al-Husayn (as)
text.ar: في الزيارات المطلقة للحسين عليه‌السلام

text.en:
First Form of Ziyarah.In his book of al-Kafi, Shaykh al-Kulayni has reported the following through his chain of authority from al-Husayn ibn Thuwayr:

Yunus ibn ²abyan, al-Mufazzal ibn ‘Umar, Abu-Salamah al-Sarraj, and I were sitting with Abu-’Abdullah Ja’far ibn Muhammad (al-Sadiq) and Yunus, the oldest among us, was our spokesman. “May Allah accept me as ransom for you,” Yunus expressed, “I usually attend the sessions of these people (i.e. the ‘Abbasid ruling authorities). What should I say then?” The Imam (‘a) instructed, “When you attend such sessions and mention us, you should say,
text.ar: الزيارة الأولى : روى الكليني في (الكافي) بسنده عن الحسين بن ثوير قال : كنت أنا ويونس بن ظبيان والمفضل بن عمر وأبو سلمة السراج جلوساً عند أبي عبد الله جعفر بن محمد عليه‌السلام وكان المتكلم يونس وكان أكبرنا سنّا فقال له : جعلت فداك إني أحضر مجالس هؤُلاءِ القوم يعني ولد عبّاس فما أقول؟ قال : إذا حضرتهم وذكرتنا فقل :

translation.en: ‘O Allah, (please) show us luxury and pleasure.

text.en: If you say so, you will gain the reward that you wish to gain or you will be allowed to return to this worldly life at the Return.”I then spoke, “May Allah accept me as ransom for you! I very frequently mention (Imam) al-Husayn; what should I say then?” The Imam (‘a) instructed, “You should repeat the following statement three times:
text.ar: لتبلغ ما تريد من الثواب أو الرجوع عند الرجعة . فقلت : جعلت فداك إنِّي كَثِيراً ماأذكر الحسين عليه‌السلام فأي شي أقول؟ قال : تقول وتعيد ذلك ثلاثاً :

segments.arabic: صَلَّىٰ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْكَ يَا أَبَا عَبْدِ ٱللَّهِ

translation.en: ‘May Allah bless you, O Abu-`Abdullah.’

text.en: Actually, greetings that are addressed to him always reach him whether they are said near him or from a remote place.When Abu-’Abdullah (Imam al-Husayn) passed away, the seven heavens, the seven layers of the earth, whatever within them, whatever between them, all the inhabitants of Paradise and Hell from the various species of the creatures of our Lord, whatever can be seen, and whatever cannot be seen - all these wept for al-Husayn (‘a) except for three.”“May Allah accept me as ransom for you!” I asked, “What are these three things?”The Imam (‘a) answered, “The things that did not weep for him are al-Basrah, Damascus, and the family of ‘Uthman.”I then asked, “May Allah accept me as ransom for you! When I intend to visit him, what should I say and what should I do?”The Imam (‘a) instructed:When you come to Abu-’Abdullah (al-Husayn), you should bathe yourself on the bank of the River Euphrates. You should then put on (ceremonially) pure clothes and walk barefooted, because you are in the sanctuary of Almighty Allah and His Messenger, uttering repeatedly statements of professing Almighty Allah being the Greatest (i.e. allahu-akbar), there being no god save Him (i.e. la-ilaha-illa-allahu), all glory being to Him (i.e. subhaha-allahi), and all veneration being to Him, and uttering the statement of invoking His blessings on the Holy Prophet and his Household (i.e. allahumma salli ‘ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin) until you stop at the gate of the shrine. Then, you should say the following:
text.ar: فإن السلام يصل إليه من قريبٍ وبعيدٍ. ثم قال إن أبا عبد الله عليه‌السلام لما مضى بكت السماوات السبع والأَرضون السبع وما فيهن وما بينهن ومن يتقلب في الجنة والنار من خلق ربنا وما يرى ومالا يرى بكاءً على أبي عبد الله عليه‌السلام إِلاّ ثلاثة أشياء لم تبك عليه. قلت : جُعلت فداك ما هذه الثلاثة الأشياء؟ قال : لم تبك عليه البصرة ولا دمشق ولا آل عثمان. قال قلت : جعلت فداك إنِّي أريد أن أزوره فكيف أقول وكيف أصنع؟ قال : إذا أتيت أبا عبد الله عليه‌السلام فاغتسل على شاطي الفرات ثمّ البس ثيابك الطاهرة ثمَّ امش حافياً فإنك في حرم من حرم الله ورسوله بالتكبير والتهليل والتمجيد والتعظيم للهِ كَثِيراً والصلاة على محمد وأهل بيته حتى تصير إلى باب الحائر ثم قل :

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا حُجَّةَ ٱللَّهِ وَٱبْنَ حُجَّتِهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O Argument of Allah and son of His Argument.
Peace be upon you all, O Allah’s angels
and visitors of the tomb of the son of Allah’s Prophet

text.en: You should then walk for ten steps and stop again to repeat the following statement thirty times:
text.ar: ثم اخطُ عشر خُطىً ثم قف فكبر ثلاثين تكبيرة

translation.en: Allah is the Most Great.

text.en: You should then move towards the tomb from the side of the Imam’s face having your face to be to the face of the Imam and the kiblah direction to be between your shoulders. You should then say the following:
text.ar: ثم امش إلى القبر من قبل وجهه واستقبل وجهك بوجهه واجعل القبلة بين كتفيك ثم قل :

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا حُجَّةَ ٱللَّهِ وَٱبْنَ حُجَّتِهِ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya hujjata allahi wabna hujjatihi

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O Allah’s Argument (on His creatures) and son of His Argument.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا قَتِيلَ ٱللَّهِ وَٱبْنَ قَتِيلِهِ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya qatila allahi wabna qatilihi

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O killed for Allah’s sake and son of the killed for His sake.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا ثَارَ ٱللَّهِ وَٱبْنَ ثَارِهِ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya thara allahi wabna tharihi

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O vengeance of Allah and son of His vengeance.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا وِتْرَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلْمَوْتُورَ فِي ٱلسَّمَاوَاتِ وَٱلأَرْضِ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya witra allahi almawtura fi alssamawati wal-ardi

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O he whose blood-claimer is Allah and whose blood is also claimed by the heavens and the earth.

segments.arabic: أَشْهَدُ أَنَّ دَمَكَ سَكَنَ فِي ٱلْخُلْدِ
segments.transliteration: ashhadu anna damaka sakana fi alkhuldi

translation.en: I bear witness that your blood has resided in the Eternity,
the shades of the Divine Throne have shivered for it,
and all creatures have wept for it,

segments.arabic: وَبَكَتْ لَهُ ٱلسَّمَاوَاتُ ٱلسَّبْعُ وَٱلأَرَضُونَ ٱلسَّبْعُ
segments.transliteration: wa bakat lahu alssamawatu alssab`u wal-araduna alssab`u

translation.en: including the seven heavens, the seven layers of the earth,

segments.transliteration: wa ma fihinna wa ma baynahunna

translation.en: whatever is within them, whatever is between them,

segments.arabic: وَمَنْ يَتَقَلَّبُ فِي ٱلْجَنَّةِ وَٱلنَّارِ مِنْ خَلْقِ رَبِّنَا
segments.transliteration: wa man yataqallabu fi aljannati walnnari min khalqi rabbina

translation.en: all the inhabitants of Paradise and Hellfire from the creatures of our Lord,

segments.transliteration: wa ma yura wa ma la yura

translation.en: whatever can be seen, and whatever cannot be seen.

segments.arabic: أَشْهَدُ أَنَّكَ حُجَّةُ ٱللَّهِ وَٱبْنُ حُجَّتِهِ
segments.transliteration: ashhadu annaka hujjatu allahi wabnu hujjatihi

translation.en: I bear witness that you are Allah’s Argument and son of His Argument.

segments.arabic: وَأَشْهَدُ أَنَّكَ قَتِيلُ ٱللَّهِ وَٱبنُ قَتِيلِهِ
segments.transliteration: wa ashhadu annaka qatilu allahi wabnu qatilihi

translation.en: I bear witness that you were killed for Allah’s sake and you are the son of him who was killed for the same.

segments.arabic: وأَشْهَدُ أَنَّكَ ثَارُ ٱللَّهِ وَٱبْنُ ثَارِهِ
segments.transliteration: wa ashhadu annaka tharu allahi wabnu tharihi

translation.en: I bear witness that you are Allah’s vengeance and the son of His vengeance.

segments.arabic: وَأَشْهَدُ أَنَّك وِتْرُ ٱللَّهِ ٱلْمَوْتورُ فِي ٱلسَّمَاوَاتِ وَٱلأَرْضِ
segments.transliteration: wa ashhadu annaka witru allahi almawturu fi alssamawati wal-ardi

translation.en: I bear witness that Allah is your blood-claimer and your blood is also claimed by the heavens and the earth.

segments.transliteration: wa ashhadu annaka qad ballaghta wa nasahta

translation.en: I bear witness that you conveyed (the message), acted sincerely,
carried out (your duties), fulfilled (your pledges),
strove for Allah’s sake,

segments.arabic: وَمَضَيْتُ لِلَّذِي كُنْتَ عَلَيْهِ شهَيداً وَمُسْتَشْهِداً
segments.transliteration: wa madayta lilladhi kunta `alayhi shahidan wa mustashhidan

translation.en: you advanced to that on which you were witness and that which you were asked to witness,
and you were observer and you were watched.
I am the slave of Allah, the servant of you,
the obedient to you, and the visitor of you.
I seek for a perfect position with Allah,

segments.transliteration: wa thabata alqadami fi alhijrati ilayka

translation.en: I seek steadfastness through my pilgrimage to you,
and I seek the path that cannot be passed without you

segments.arabic: مِنَ ٱلدُّخُولِ فِي كِفَالَتِكَ ٱلَّتِي أُمِرْتَ بِهَا
segments.transliteration: min alddukhuli fi kifalatika allati umirta biha

translation.en: so that I will be allowed to enter in your guarantee that you have been ordered to give.
He who seeks Allah must begin with you,
because only through you has Allah disclosed forgery.

segments.transliteration: wa bikum yuba`idu allahu alzzamana alkaliba

translation.en: Through you only does Allah throw away difficult days.
For your sake only has Allah started (creation).
With you only will Allah seal.

segments.transliteration: wa bikum yamhu ma yasha'u wa yuthbitu

translation.en: Through you only does He erase whatever He wills and establishes (whatever He wills).

segments.transliteration: wa bikum yafukku aldhdhulla min riqabina

translation.en: Through you only does He release us from humility.

segments.arabic: وَبِكُمْ يُدْرِكُ ٱللَّهُ تِرَةَ كُلِّ مُؤْمِنٍ يُطْلَبُ بِهَا
segments.transliteration: wa bikum yudriku allahu tirata kulli mu'minin yutlabu biha

translation.en: Through you only will Allah avenge every faithful believer.
Through you only does the soil grow its trees.
Through you only does the earth produce its fruits.

segments.transliteration: wa bikum tunzilu alssama'u qatraha wa rizqaha

translation.en: Through you only does the sky pour down its drops and sustenance.
Through you only does Allah relieve from agonies.
Through you only does Allah cause the rain to come down.

segments.arabic: وَبِكُمْ تُسَبِّحُ ٱلأَرْضُ ٱلَّتِي تَحْمِلُ أَبْدَانَكُمْ
segments.transliteration: wa bikum tusabbihu al-ardu allati tahmilu abdanakum

translation.en: Through you only does the earth that carries your bodies glorify (the Lord)
and are the mountains made firm.

segments.arabic: إِرَادَةُ ٱلرَّبِّ فِي مَقَادِيرِ أُمُورِهِ تَهْبِطُ إِلَيْكُمْ
segments.transliteration: iradatu alrrabbi fi maqadiri umurihi tahbitu ilaykum

translation.en: The will of the Lord regarding the estimations of His affairs come down to you
and are issued from your houses.

segments.arabic: وَٱلصَّادِرُ عَمَّا فُصِّلَ مِنْ أَحْكَامِ ٱلْعِبَادِ

translation.en: So do the details of the decrees to the servants.
Cursed be the people who killed you.
Cursed be the people who dissent from you,
the people who deny your (Divinely commissioned) leadership,
the people who supported each other against you,

segments.transliteration: wa ummatun shahidat wa lam tustashhad

translation.en: and the people who witnessed you but they shunned being martyred for you.

segments.arabic: اَلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِي جَعَلَ ٱلنَّارَ مَأْوَاهُمْ
segments.transliteration: alhamdu lillahi alladhi ja`ala alnnara ma'wahum

translation.en: All praise be to Allah Who has decided Hellfire to be the dwelling of these peoples.
Woeful indeed will be the place to which they shall be led
and woeful indeed will be the place to which they shall be brought.
All praise be to Allah, the Lord of the worlds.

text.en: You should then repeat the following words three times:
text.ar: وقل ثلاث مرات :

segments.arabic: وَصَلّى الله عَلَيْكَ يا أبا عَبْدِ اللهِ.

translation.en: And may Allah bless you, O Abu-`Abdullah

text.en: You should then repeat the following words three times, too:
text.ar: وقل ثلاث مرات :

segments.arabic: أَنَا إِلَىٰ ٱللَّهِ مِمَّنْ خَالَفَكَ بَريءٌ

translation.en: I disavow, in the presence of Allah, all those who dissented you.

text.en: You should then stand up and direct towards his son, ‘Ali, who is buried to the side of the Imam’s feet, and say the following words:
text.ar: ثم تقوم فتاتي ابنه علياً وهو عند رجله فتقول :

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا بْنَ رَسُولِ ٱللَّهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O son of Allah’s Messenger.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا بْنَ أَمِيرِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O son of the Commander of the Faithful.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا بْنَ ٱلْحَسَنِ وَٱلْحُسَيْنِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O son of al-Hasan and al-Husayn.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا بْنَ خَدِيجَةَ وَفَاطِمَةَ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O son of Khadijah and Fatimah.
May Allah bless you.
May Allah bless you.
May Allah bless you.
May Allah curse those who killed you.
May Allah curse those who killed you.
May Allah curse those who killed you.
I disavow all of them in the presence of Allah.

text.en: You should then stand up and point to the martyrs with your hand, may Allah be pleased with them, and say the following words:
text.ar: ثمّ تقوم فتومي بيدك إلى الشّهداء رضي‌الله‌عنهم وتقول :

translation.en: Peace be upon you.
Peace be upon you.
Peace be upon you.
You, by Allah, have achieved success.
You, by Allah, have achieved success.
You, by Allah, have achieved success.

segments.arabic: فَلَيْتَ أَنِّي مَعَكُمْ فَأَفُوزَ فَوْزاً عَظيماً

translation.en: Would that I were with you, then should I achieve a great success.

text.en: You should then turn around and make the tomb of Abu-’Abdullah in front of you (i.e. stop behind the tomb) and offer a six-unit prayer. Thus, you have accomplished the visitation of Imam al-Husayn’s tomb. You may then leave, if you wish. (Here end the instructive words of Imam al-Sadiq)This form of Ziyarah has been also reported by Shaykh al-Tusi, in Tahdhib al-Ahkam, and Shaykh al-Saduq, in man-la-yahzuruhul-faqih. Commenting on it, Shaykh al-Saduq says, “Although I have mentioned other forms of Ziyarah in my two books of al-Mazar and al-Maqtal, I have chosen this very form for this book, because it is the most authentically reported form; therefore, it covers what we need and meets the required terms.”
text.ar: ثم تدور فتجعل قبر أبي عبد الله عليه‌السلام بين يديك أي تقف خلف القبر المطهر فتصلي ست ركعات وقد تمت زيارتك فإن شئت فانصرف . أقول : قد روى أيضاً هذه الزيارة الشيخ الطوسي في التهذيب والصدوق في كتاب (من لا يحضره الفقيه). وقال الصدوق إنِّي قد ذكرت في كتابي (المزار) و (المقتل) أنواعاً من الزيارات وانتخبت هذه الزيارة لهذا الكتاب فإنّها أصحُّ الزيارات عندي روايةً وهي تكفينا وتفي بالمقصود انتهى.

text.en:
Second Form of Ziyarah.Shaykh al-Kulayni reports Imam ‘Ali al-Naqi (al-Hadi) to have said:
When you are at al-Husayn’s tomb, you should say the following words:
text.ar: الزيارة الثانية : روى الشيخ الكليني عن الإمام علي النقي عليه‌السلام قال : تقول عند الحسين عليه‌السلام :

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا أَبَا عَبْدِ ٱللَّهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O Abu-`Abdullah.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا حُجَّةَ ٱللَّهِ فِي أَرْضِهِ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya hujjata allahi fi ardihi

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O Allah’s Argument in His lands
and His witness on His creatures.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا بْنَ رَسُولِ ٱللَّهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O so of Allah’s Messenger.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا بْنَ عَليٍّ ٱلْمُرْتَضَىٰ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O son of `Ali the well-pleased.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا بْنَ فَاطِمَةَ ٱلزَّهْرَاءِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O son of Fatimah the Luminous.

segments.arabic: أَشْهَدُ أَنَّكَ قَدْ أَقَمْتَ ٱلصَّلاَةَ وَآتَيْتَ ٱلزَّكَاةَ
segments.transliteration: ashhadu annaka qad aqamta alssalata wa atayta alzzakata

translation.en: I bear witness that you performed the prayers, defrayed the poor-rate,

segments.transliteration: wa amarta bilma`rufi wa nahayta `an almunkari

translation.en: enjoined the right, forbade the wrong,

segments.arabic: وَجَاهَدْتَ فِي سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ حَتَّىٰ أَتَاكَ ٱلْيَقِينُ
segments.transliteration: wa jahadta fi sabili allahi hatta ataka alyaqinu

translation.en: and strove for Allah’s sake until death came upon you.
So, Allah may bless you in your lifetime and after your death.

text.en: You may then stick your right cheek to the tomb and say these words:
text.ar: ثم تضع خدّك الأيمن على القبر وتقول :

segments.arabic: أَشْهَدُ أَنَّكَ عَلَىٰ بَيِّنَةٍ مِنْ رَبِّكَ

translation.en: I bear witness that you are verily relying on clear proof from your Lord.
I have come confessing of my sins
so that you may intercede for me before your Lord.
O son of Allah’s Messenger.

text.en: You should then mention the Holy Imams by names respectively and then say the following words:
text.ar: ثم تذكر الأئمة عليهم‌السلام بأسمائهم واحداً واحداً وقل :

translation.en: I bear witness that you are verily Allah’s Arguments (against His creatures).
Please, write down for me a covenant and a pledge with you.
I have come to you to refresh my covenant;
so, testify this to me before your Lord.
You are verily the Witness.

text.en: Third Form of Ziyarah.This form of Ziyarah has been reported by Ibn Tawus in his book of al-Mazar. Reporting a great reward for those who visit Imam al-Husayn with this form of Ziyarah, Ibn Tawus, having overlooked the chain of authority, reported that Jabir al-Ju’fi said that Imam al-Sadiq (‘a) asked him, “What is the distance between your house and the tomb of al-Husayn?”Jabir answered, “May Allah accept my father and mother as ransoms for you! It is only a day and half a day (walking).”“Do you usually visit him?” the Imam (‘a) asked.“Yes, I do,” answered Jabir.The Imam (‘a) then said to him, “May I convey to you good tidings? May I delight you by informing you some of the rewards of visiting his tomb?”“Yes, you may,” answered Jabir.The Imam (‘a) thus said, “Once you provide yourselves with supplies of travel and prepare yourselves for visiting Imam al-Husayn (‘a), the inhabitants of the heavens convey this glad tiding to each other. As soon as you leave the door of your houses and ride or walk, Almighty Allah appoints four thousand angels to bless you until you arrive at Imam al-Husayn’s tomb. When you arrive at the tomb of al-Husayn ibn ‘Ali (‘a), you may stop at the gate and say these words, because you shall be granted a portion of Allah’s mercy for each word you utter... These are the words:
text.ar: الزيارة الثالثة : هي ما رواها ابن طاووس في (المزار) ، وروى لها فضلاً كَثِيراً قال بحذف الاسناد عن جابر الجعفي قال : قال الصادق عليه‌السلام لجابر : كم بينك وبين قبر الحسين عليه‌السلام : قال قلت : بأبي أنت وأُمِّي ، يوم وبعض آخر. قال : فقال لي : أتزوره؟ قلت : نعم. قال : فقال : أفلا أفرحّك؟ ألا اُبشّرك بثوابهم؟ قال : قلت : بلى ، جعلت فداك. قال : إن الرجل منكم ليأخذ في جهازه ويتهيّأ لزيارته فيتباشر به أهل السماء فإذا خرج من باب منزله راكباً أو ماشياً وكَّل الله به أربعة آلاف من الملائكة يصلُّون عليه حتى يوافي الحسين عليه‌السلام ، يا مفضل إن أتيت قبر الحسين بن علي عليه‌السلام فقف بالباب وقل هذه الكلمات فإن لك بكل كلمة كِفلاً من رحمة اللهِ. فقلت : ما هي جُعلت فداك؟ قال : تقول :

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا وَارِثَ آدَمَ صَفْوَةِ ٱللَّهِ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya waritha adama safwati allahi

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O inheritor of Adam the chosen by Allah.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا وَارِثَ نُوحٍ نَبِيِّ ٱللَّهِ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya waritha nuhin nabiyyi allahi

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O inheritor of Noah the prophet of Allah.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا وَارِثَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ خَلِيلِ ٱللَّهِ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya waritha ibrahima khalili allahi

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O inheritor of Abraham the friend of Allah.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا وَارِثَ مُوسَىٰ كَلِيمِ ٱللَّهِ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya waritha musa kalimi allahi

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O inheritor of Moses the spoken by Allah.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا وَارِثَ عِيسَىٰ رُوحِ ٱللَّهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O inheritor of Jesus the spirit of Allah.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا وَارِثَ مُحَمَّدٍ سَيِّدِ رُسُلِ ٱللَّهِ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya waritha muhammadin sayyidi rusuli allahi

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O inheritor of Muhammad the chief of Allah’s Messengers.
Peace be upon you, O inheritor of `Ali
the commander of the faithful and the best of the Prophets’ successors.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا وَارِثَ ٱلْحَسَنِ ٱلرَّضِيِّ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O inheritor of al-Hasan the amicable,
the immaculate, the content, and the pleased.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ أَيُّهَا ٱلْوَصِيُّ ٱلْبَرُّ ٱلتَّقِيُّ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O dutiful and pious successor.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ وَعَلَىٰ ٱلأَرْوَاحِ ٱلَّتِي حَلَّتْ بِفِنَائِكَ وَأَنَاخَتْ بِرَحْلِكَ

translation.en: Peace be upon you and upon these souls who gathered before your yard and resided next to your residence.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ وَعَلَىٰ ٱلْمَلاَئِكَةِ ٱلْحَافِّينَ بِكَ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka wa `ala al-arwahi allati hallat bifina'ika wa anakhat birahlika

translation.en: Peace be upon you and upon the angels who are encompassing you.

segments.arabic: أَشْهَدُ أَنَّكَ قَدْ أَقَمْتَ ٱلصَّلاَةَ وَآتَيْتَ ٱلزَّكَاةَ

translation.en: I bear witness that you performed the prayers, defrayed the poor-rate,

segments.transliteration: ashhadu annaka qad aqamta alssalata wa atayta alzzakata

translation.en: enjoined the right, forbade the wrong,

segments.arabic: وَجَاهَدْتَ ٱلْمُلْحِدينَ وَعَبَدْتَ ٱللَّهَ حَتَّىٰ أَتَاكَ ٱلْيَقِينُ
segments.transliteration: wa amarta bilma`rufi wa nahayta `an almunkari

translation.en: strove against the atheists, and served Allah until death came upon you.

segments.transliteration: wa jahadta almulhidina wa `abadta allaha hatta ataka alyaqinu

translation.en: Peace and Allah’s mercy and blessings be upon you.

text.en: You may then walk towards the tomb. With each step you make, you will be awarded the reward of one who sacrifices his blood for the sake of Allah. When you approach the tomb, you may wipe your hand over it and say these words:
text.ar: ثمّ تسعى إلى القبر فلك بكل قدمٍ رفعتها أو وضعتها كثواب المتشحط بدمه في سبيل الله فإذا وصلت إلى القبر ووقفت عنده فأمرر عليه يدك وقل :

segments.arabic: السَّلامُ عَلَيْكَ يا حُجَّةَ الله فِي أَرْضِهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O Allah’s Argument in His lands.

text.en: You may then go for offering the prayer. For each unit of prayer that you offer near the tomb, you will have the reward of one who went one thousand times on hajj and one thousand times on ‘umrah, manumitted one thousand slaves for the sake of Allah, and stood in the line of a missioned Prophet one thousand times.”This tradition has been previously mentioned within the etiquettes of visiting Imam al-Husayn’s tomb, yet with little difference in paragraphs and in reporter, who has been al-Mufazzal ibn ‘Umar.
text.ar: ثم تمضي إلى صلاتك ولك بكل ركعة ركعتها عنده كثواب من حجّ ألف حجة واعتمر ألف عمرة وأعتق ألف رقبةٍ وكأنّما وقف في سبيل الله ألف مرّة مع نبي مرسل. الخبر. وقد مرّت هذه الرواية مع اختلافٍ يسير في آداب زيارة الحسين عليه‌السلام على رواية مفضل بن عمر

text.en: Fourth Form of Ziyarah.Mu’awiyah ibn ‘Ammar has reported that he asked Imam al-Sadiq (‘a) what to do when visiting Imam al-Husayn’s tomb. The Imam (‘a) taught him to say the following words:
text.ar: الزيارة الرابعة : عن معاوية بن عمّار قال : قلت لأبي عبد الله عليه‌السلام ما أقول إذا أتيت قبر الحسين عليه‌السلام؟ قال : قل :

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا أَبَا عَبْدِ ٱللَّهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O Abu-`Abdullah.

segments.arabic: صَلَّىٰ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْكَ يَا أَبَا عَبْدِ ٱللَّهِ

translation.en: May Allah bless you, O Abu-`Abdullah.

segments.arabic: رَحِمَكَ ٱللَّهُ يَا أَبَا عَبْدِ ٱللَّهِ

translation.en: May Allah have mercy upon you, O Abu-`Abdullah.
May Allah curse those who slew you.

segments.arabic: وَلَعَنَ ٱللَّهُ مَنْ شَرِكَ فِي دَمِكَ
segments.transliteration: wa la`ana allahu man sharika fi damika

translation.en: May Allah curse those who took part in shedding your blood.

segments.arabic: وَلَعَنَ ٱللَّهُ مَنْ بَلَغَهُ ذٰلِكَ فَرَضِيَ بِهِ
segments.transliteration: wa la`ana allahu man balaghahu dhalika faradiya bihi

translation.en: May Allah curse those who are pleased when they knew about it.

segments.arabic: أَنَا إِلَىٰ ٱللَّهِ مَنْ ذٰلِكَ بَريءٌ
segments.transliteration: ana ila allahi min dhalika bari'un

translation.en: I disavow all those in the presence of Allah.

text.en: Fifth Form of Ziyarah.Through a valid chain of authority, Imam al-Kazim (‘a) is reported to have asked Ibrahim ibn Abi’l-Ballad, “What do you say when you visit al-Husayn (‘a)?”The man answered: I say the following words:
text.ar: الزيارة الخامسة : بسند معتبر عن الكاظم عليه‌السلام أنه قال لإبراهيم بن أبي البلاد : ماذا تقول إذا زرت الحسين عليه‌السلام؟ فأجاب : أقول :

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا أَبَا عَبْدِ ٱللَّهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O Abu-`Abdullah.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا بْنَ رَسُولِ ٱللَّهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O son of Allah’s Messenger.
I bear witness that you performed the prayers,
defrayed the poor-rate,
enjoining the right,
forbade the evil,
and called to the way of your Lord
with wisdom and excellent exhortation.

segments.arabic: وَأَشْهَدُ أَنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ سَفَكُوٱ دَمَكَ وَٱسْتَحَلُّوٱ حُرْمَتَكَ
segments.transliteration: wa ashhadu anna alladhina safaku damaka wastahallu hurmataka

translation.en: I also bear witness that those who shed your blood and violated your sanctity

segments.arabic: مَلْعُونُونَ مُعَذَّبُونَ عَلَىٰ لِسَانِ دَاوُدَ وَعيسَىٰ بْنِ مَرْيَمَ
segments.transliteration: mal`ununa mu`adhdhabuna `ala lisani dawuda wa `isa bni maryama

translation.en: have been accursed, and will be tortured, by the tongue of David and Jesus the son of Mary.
This was because they disobeyed and used to exceed the limit.

text.en: The Imam ('a) commented, “Yes, this is accurate.”
text.ar: فقال عليه‌السلام : نعم هو هكذا

text.en: Sixth Form of Ziyarah. ‘Ammar has reported Imam al-Sadiq (‘a) as saying that when you stop at the grave of Imam al-Husayn (‘a), you may say the following words:
text.ar: الزيارة السادسة : عن عمّار عن الصادق عليه‌السلام قال : تقول إذا انتهيت إلى قبره عليه‌السلام :

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا بْنَ رَسُولِ ٱللَّهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O son of Allah’s Messenger.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا بْنَ أَمِيرِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O son of the commander of the faithful.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا أَبَا عَبْدِ ٱللَّهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O Abu-`Abdullah.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا سَيِّدَ شَبَابِ أَهْلِ ٱلْجَنَّةِ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya sayyida shababi ahli aljannati

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O chief of the youth of Paradise.
Allah’s mercy and blessings be upon you.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا مَنْ رِضَاهُ مِنْ رِضَا ٱلرَّحْمٰنِ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya man ridahu min rida alrrahmani

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O he whose pleasure is part of the pleasure of the All-beneficent Lord
and whose displeasure is part of the displeasure of the All-beneficent Lord.
Peace be upon you, O trustee of Allah,

segments.transliteration: wa hujjata allahi wa baba allahi

translation.en: Argument of Allah, door to Allah,

segments.arabic: وَٱلدَّلِيلَ عَلَىٰ ٱللَّهِ وَٱلدَاعِيَ إِلَىٰ ٱللَّهِ

translation.en: guide to Allah, and caller to Allah.

segments.arabic: أَشْهَدُ أَنَّكَ قَدْ حَلَّلْتَ حَلاَلَ ٱللَّهِ
segments.transliteration: ashhadu annaka qad hallata halala allahi

translation.en: I bear witness that you deemed lawful all that which Allah has deemed lawful,
deemed unlawful all that which Allah has deemed unlawful,
performed the prayers,
defrayed the poor-rate,
enjoined the right,
forbade the evil,
and called to the way of your Lord
with wisdom and excellent exhortation.

segments.transliteration: wa ashhadu annaka wa man qutila ma`aka

translation.en: I also bear witness that you, as well as those whom were slain with you,
are martyrs, alive, and provided sustenance from your Lord.

segments.transliteration: wa ashhadu anna qatilaka fi alnnari

translation.en: I also bear witness that your slayer shall be in Hellfire.
Before Allah, I profess disavowal of those who slew you,
who fought against you, who supported each other against you,
who rallied against you,

segments.transliteration: wa mimman sami`a sawtaka wa lam yu`inka

translation.en: and who heard your call but refrained from helping you.

segments.arabic: يَا لَيْتَنِي كُنْتُ مَعَكُمْ فَأَفُوزَ فَوْزاً عَظيماً
segments.transliteration: ya laytani kuntu ma`akum fa'afuza fawzan `aziman

translation.en: Would that I were with you, then should I achieve a great success.

name: Ziyarats of Imam (atfs) at Samarra Sirdab

text.en: Etiquettes at the Holy Sardab
text.ar: في آداب السرداب الطاهر وصفة زيارة حجة الله على العباد وبقية الله في البلاد الإمام المهدي الحجة بن الحسن صاحب الزمان صلوات الله عليه وعلى آبائه.

text.en: In addition to the etiquettes at the Holy Vault, this section comprises the details of visiting Imam al-Mahdi the Argument of Allah against His the servants, the left by Allah in His lands, the well-guided leader, al-Hujjah the son of al-Hasan, and the Patron of the Age. Allah’s blessings be upon him and his fathers.
text.ar: وعلينا أن نصدِّر المقصد بالتنبيه على أمر تحدّثنا عنه في كتاب الهديّة نقلاً عن كتاب التحيّة وهو

text.en: Before indulging into this topic, it seems necessary to draw attentions to a matter which I have quoted in my book of Hadiyyat al-Za’ir from the book of al-Tahiyyah. This holy vault is part of the house of Imam al-Hadi and Imam al-’Askari (‘a). Before the construction of the new building (i.e. the courtyard, shrine, and dome), the entrance to the vault was situated behind the tomb of the holy Imams near the tomb of Lady Narjis (Narjis Khatun), which may now be in the portico. In the past, this entrance to the vault descended to a long dark way ending at a door that lied in the center of the Occultation Vault. In the present day, this vault is decorated with mirrors. To the side of the kiblah direction, there is a window taking to the courtyard of the shrine of the two holy Imams. The place of the former door is marked by the picture of the niche, which is decorated with Qashani bricks. Visiting of the three Imams were made in one shrine; therefore, we can notice that al-Shahid al-Awwal, in his book of al-Mazar, follows up the ziyarah of Imam al-Hadi and Imam al-’Askari with the ziyarah of the holy vault and then refers to the ziyarah of Lady Narjis. A hundred and a few years ago, Ahmad Khan al-Dunbuli, the supported and guided by God, prepared himself for reconstructing the entire building. He therefore spent a huge amount of money on separating the courtyard of the holy shrine of the two Imams from the holy vault and then constructed the building, portico, and lofty dome of the vault. For the holy vault, he established a private courtyard, hall, entrance, and vestibule. He also built another vault specially for women, as is seen in the present day. As a result, the features of whatever was before the entrance, scale, and door were effaced and all the traces of that place were obliterated [Except the place that is currently known as Bayt al-Akhbariyyin.] and the place of some traditional etiquettes was also obliterated. However, the origin of the holy vault, which is the place of a set of forms of ziyarah, has been kept unchanged.
text.ar: أن هذا السرداب الطاهر هو قسم من دارهما عليهما‌السلام وقبلما يشيد هذا البناء الحديث (الصحن والحرم والقبّة) كان المدخل إلى السرداب خلف القبر عند مرقد السيدة نرجس (نرجس خاتون) ولعله الان واقع في الرواق فكان ينحدر إلى مسلك مظلم طويل ينتهي بباب يفتح وسط سرداب الغيبة. والسرداب في عصرنا الحاضر مزخرف بالمرايا وله في جانب القبلة نافذة إلى صحن العسكريين عليهما‌السلام ، وموضع الباب السابق معلَّم بصورة المحراب منقوشة بالقاشاني فكانت الزيارات وغيرها لهؤُلاءِ الأئمة الثلاث تؤدى كلها من حرم واحد ولذلك نجد الشهيد الأول في (المزار) يعقب زيارة العسكريين عليهما‌السلام بزيارة السرداب ثم يذكر زيارة السيدة نرجس ومنذ مائة وبضعة سنين تأهب للبناء المؤيد المسدد أحمد خان الدّنبلي وأفرز بما أنفقه من المبلغ الخطير صحن الإمامين عليهما‌السلام كما هو الان وشيد الروضة والرواق والقبّة الشامخة وأسّس للسرداب الطاهر الصحن الخاص والإيوان والمدخل والدِّهليز كما شيد للنساء سرداباً خاصاً كما هو قائم الان فطمست معالم ما كان من قبل المدخل والدّرج والباب وانمحى جميع آثاره فزال بذلك بعض الآداب المأثورة ولكن أصل السرداب الشريف وهو موضع جملة من الزيارات باقٍ لم يتغير.

text.en: However, the etiquette of asking permission of entering the holy vault is not canceled due to the blocking of the former entrance, because, generally, every visit to any holy shrine must be preceded by a form of asking permission of entrance, as is concluded from narrations. Moreover, we can observe that master scholars have declared that it is necessary to ask permission before entering any gate of a shrine of one of the Holy Imams that people used to take in entering that place.
text.ar: وأما الاستئذان لدخول السرداب فلم يسقط‌ بانسداد المدخل السابق ، فلكل زيارة استئذان كما دلّ عليه الاستقراء ونجد العلماء كذلك يصرّحون بلزوم الاستئذان تأدباً للدخول من أي باب اعتيد الدخول منه إلى حرم إمام من الأئمة عليهم‌السلام والان نبدأ في صفة الزيارة.

text.en: Form of Ziyarah: According to traditions, the form of asking permission to enter the holy vault is the ziyarah form that will be mentioned soon. It begins with the following statement:
text.ar: إعلم أنّ الاستئذان الخاص المأثور لدخول السرداب هو الزيارة الآتية التي مفتتحها :

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O vicegerent of Allah
and vicegerent of your well-guided fathers…

text.en: This form of ziyarah ends with a form of asking permission of entrance to the holy vault. It is said at the door of the vault before coming down there.Sayyid Ibn Tawus has mentioned another form of asking permission of entrance (i.e. isti'dhan), whose statements are similar to the first general form of asking permission, which has been mentioned in Chapter Two of this section of Forms of Ziyarah.As to `Allamah al-Majlisi, he has mentioned another form, which he reports from an ancient book on forms of ziyarah. This form begins with the following statement:
text.ar: وتنتهي بالاستئذان ويزار بها على باب السرداب قبل النزول إليه وقد أورد السيد ابن طاووس (رض) استئذانا آخر يقرب من الاستئذان العام الأول الذي أوردناه في الفصل الثاني من باب الزيارات. وأورد العلامة المجلسي (رض) استئذانا آخر حكاه عن نسخة قديمة وأوّلها :

translation.en: O Allah, this is verily an area that You have purified
and a courtyard that You have honored…

text.en: This form has been also previously mentioned immediately after the first general form of asking permission. You may refer to and say it before coming down to the holy vault.You may then come down to the holy vault and visit Imam al-Mahdi (`a) with the forumula of ziyarah that is reported from him according to the report of the sublime Shaykh Ahmad ibn Abi-Talib al-Tabrisi in his book of al-Ihtijaj, as follows:The following document was issued from the Sacred Side after replying to some questions:
text.ar: وهو ماعقبنا به الاستئذان العام المذكور فارجع إليه واستأذن به ثم انزل إلى السرداب وزره عليه‌السلام بما روي عنه نفسه الشريفة كما عن الشيخ الجليل أحمد بن أبي طالب الطبرسي في كتاب الاحتجاج أنّه خرج من الناحية المقدسة إلى محمد الحميري بعد الجواب عن المسائل التي سألها :

translation.en: In the Name of Allah; the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.
You neither understand His decree
nor accept from His Vicegerents.
(This is) consummate wisdom; but warnings do not avail
people who do not believe.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْنَا وَعَلَىٰ عِبَادِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلصَّالِحِينَ

translation.en: Peace be upon us and upon the righteous servants of Allah.

segments.arabic: إِذَا أَرَدْتُمُ ٱلتَّوَجُّهَ بِنَا إِلَىٰ ٱللَّهِ تَعَالَىٰ وَإِلَيْنَا

translation.en: When you intend to turn your faces towards Allah and towards us by means of us,
you should say just as Allah the All-exalted has said:
Peace be upon the Household of Yasin.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا دَاعِيَ ٱللَّهِ وَرَبَّانِيَّ آيَاتِهِ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya da`iya allahi wa rabbaniyya ayatihi

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O caller to Allah and interpreter of His Verses.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا بَابَ ٱللَّهِ وَدَيَّانَ دِينِهِ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya baba allahi wa dayyana dinihi

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O door to Allah and applier of His religion.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا خَلِيفَةَ ٱللَّهِ وَنَاصِرَ حَقِّهِ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya khalifata allahi wa nasira haqqihi

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O viceroy of Allah and backer of His right.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا حُجَّةَ ٱللَّهِ وَدَلِيلَ إِِرَادَتِهِ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya hujjata allahi wa dalila iradatihi

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O Argument of Allah and sign of His volition.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا تَالِيَ كِتَابِ ٱللَّهِ وَتَرْجُمَانَهُ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya taliya kitabi allahi wa tarjumanihi

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O reciter of the Book of Allah and its interpreter.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ فِي آنَاءِ لَيْلِكَ وَأَطْرَافِ نَهَارِكَ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka fi ana'i laylika wa atrafi naharika

translation.en: Peace be upon you at the hours of Your night and the two ends of Your day.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا بَقِيَّةَ ٱللَّهِ فِي أَرْضِهِ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya baqiyyata allahi fi ardihi

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O herald of Allah on His lands.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا مِيثَاقَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلَّذِي أَخَذَهُ وَوَكَّدَهُ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya mithaqa allahi alladhi akhadhahu wa wakkadahu

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O covenant of Allah that He has made and confirmed.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلَّذِي ضَمِنَهُ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya wa`da allahi alladhi daminahu

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O Promise of Allah that He has assured.
Peace be upon you, O appointed pennon,
poured knowledge,
aid, and expansive mercy.
This is verily a promise that shall never be belied.
Peace be upon you whenever you rise (to undertake the mission).
Peace be upon you whenever you sit.
Peace be upon you whenever you recite and elucidate.
Peace be upon you whenever you offer prayer and supplicate.
Peace be upon you whenever you genuflect and prostrate (yourself for Allah).
Peace be upon you whenever you profess Allah’s Godhead and profess His All-greatness.
Peace be upon you whenever you praise Allah and implore for His forgiveness.
Peace be upon you whenever you begin and end your day.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ فِي ٱللَّيْلِ إِِذَا يَغْشَىٰ

translation.en: Peace be upon you in the night when it draws a veil
and the day when it shines in brightness.
Peace be upon you, O entrusted leader.
Peace be upon you, O favored and expected.
Peace be upon you to the ultimate meaning of peace.
I solicit you, O my master,

segments.arabic: أَنِّي أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ ٱللَّهُ
segments.transliteration: anni ashhadu an la ilaha illa allahu

translation.en: to testify to me that I bear witness that there is no god but Allah;
One and Only Lord and having no associate,
and that Muhammad is His servant and messenger.

segments.transliteration: la habiba illa huwa wa ahluhu

translation.en: There is no one more beloved by Allah than him and his household.
And I solicit you, O my master, to testify to me that I bear witness
that `Ali the Commander of the Faithful is His argument,
al-Hasan is His argument,
al-Husayn is His argument,
`Ali the son of al-Husayn is His argument,
Muhammad the son of `Ali is His argument,
Ja`far the son of Muhammad is His argument,
Musa the son of Ja`far is His argument,
`Ali the son of Musa is His argument,
Muhammad the son of `Ali is His argument,
`Ali the son of Muhammad is His argument,
and al-Hasan the son of `Ali is His argument.
I also bear witness that you are verily the argument of Allah.
You all are the first and the last.

segments.arabic: وَأَنَّ رَجْعَتَكُمْ حَقٌّ لاَ رَيْبَ فِيهَا
segments.transliteration: wa anna raj`atakum haqqun la rayba fiha

translation.en: And (I bear witness) that your (promised) Return is undoubtedly true
“On the day when its belief avails not a soul
which theretofore believed not,
nor in its belief earned good by works,”
death is true,

segments.transliteration: wa anna nakiran wa nakiran haqqun

translation.en: and Nakir and Nakir are true.
And I bear witness that the Raising for death is true,
the Resurrection is true,
the Discriminating Bridge is true,
the Watchtower is true,
the Balance (of deeds) is true,
the Gathering is true,
the Reckoning is true,
Paradise and Hell are true,
and the promise of Paradise and the threat with Hell are true.
O My master! Certainly wretched is he who opposes you
and certainly happy is he who obeys you.
So, testify to me all that which I have besought you to testify.
I am verily an ally of you
and denouncing your enemy.
The truth is only that which you accept,
the wrong is only that which you disallow,
the good is only that which you enjoin,
and the evil is only that which you forbid.
My soul is thus believing in Allah;
One and Only Lord and having no associate,
and also in His Messenger, in the Commander of the Faithful,

segments.transliteration: wa bikum ya mawlaya awwalikum wa akhirikum

translation.en: and in you all, O my master, from the beginning to the end.
I have prepared myself to back you
and my love is sincerely dedicated to you.
Respond! Respond!

text.en: Supplication Following this Form of Ziyarah
text.ar: الدعاء عقيب هذا القول:

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: allahumma inni as'aluka an tusalliya `ala muhammadin

translation.en: O Allah, I beseech You to send blessings upon Muhammad
the Prophet of Your mercy and the Word of Your Light,

segments.transliteration: wa an tamla'a qalbi nura alyaqini

translation.en: and to fill in my heart with the illumination of certitude,
my chest with the illumination of faith,
my intellect with the illumination of honest intentions,
my determination with the illumination of knowledge,
my strength with the illumination of work,
my tongue with the illumination of honesty,
my religion with the illumination of clear evidence from You,
my sight with the illumination of brightness,
my hearing with the illumination of wisdom,

segments.transliteration: wa mawaddati nura almuwalati limuhammadin wa alihi

translation.en: and my faculty of love with the illumination of sincere loyalty to Muhammad and his Household,
peace be upon them,

segments.arabic: حَتَّىٰ أَلْقَاكَ وَقَدْ وَفَيْتُ بِعَهْدِكَ وَمِيثَاقِكَ
segments.transliteration: hatta alqaka wa qad wafaytu bi`ahdika wa mithaqika

translation.en: so that I, when I meet You, will have fulfilled the pledge and covenant that I made with You
and then Your mercy will encompass me.
O Protector! O Worthy of all praise!
O Allah, bless Muhammad (al-Mahdi)
Your argument in Your land,
Your viceroy in Your countries,
the caller to Your course,
the establisher of Your justice,
the revolting by Your command,
the authority of the believers,
the ruiner of the unbelievers,
the remover of the murk,
the illuminator of the truth,
the speaker with wisdom and honesty,
the perfect Word of You in Your lands,
the vigilant and fearful,
the well-wishing saint,
the ark of salvation,
the sign of guidance,
the light of the peoples’ sights,

segments.transliteration: wa khayri man taqammasa wa irtada

translation.en: the best of all those who put clothes on their bodies,
and the unveiler of blindness,
who will fill the lands with justice and equity
as it would be filled up with wrong and unfairness.
You verily have power over all things.
O Allah, bless Your vicegerent
and the son of Your vicegerents the obedience to whom You have made incumbent upon us
and the observance of the right of whom You have made obligatory
and from whom You have removed filth
and whom You have purified with thorough purifying.
O Allah, give him victory and make him the supporter of Your religion
and through him, give victory to Your and his adherents,
followers, and backers.
Include us with them.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ أَعِذْهُ مِنْ شَرِّ كُلِّ بَاغٍ وَطَاغٍ
segments.transliteration: allahumma a`idhhu min sharri kulli baghin wa taghin

translation.en: O Allah, protect him against the evil of every transgressor and tyrant
and against the evils of all Your creatures.

segments.arabic: وَٱحْفَظْهُ مِنْ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ وَمِنْ خَلْفِهِ
segments.transliteration: wahfazhu min bayni yadayhi wa min khalfihi

translation.en: Guard him from ahead of him, behind him,
his right side, and his left side.

segments.arabic: وَٱحْرُسْهُ وَٱمْنَعْهُ مِنْ أَنْ يوُصَلَ إِِلَيْهِ بِسُوءٍ
segments.transliteration: wahrus-hu wamna`hu min an yusala ilayhi bisu'in

translation.en: Watch him and defend him against anything that may cause evil to him.

segments.transliteration: wahfaz fihi rasulaka wa ala rasulika

translation.en: Save through him Your Messenger and the family of Your Messenger.
Manifest justice through him.
Aid him with victory.
Help his backers.
Frustrate those who intend to disappoint him.
Terminate those who intend to eradicate him.
And, through him, terminate the tyrants of unbelief,
kill the disbelievers, the hypocrites,
and all atheists,

segments.arabic: حَيْثُ كَانُوٱ مِنْ مَشَارِقِ ٱلأَرْضِ وَمَغَارِبِهَا
segments.transliteration: haythu kanu min mashariqi al-ardi wa magharibiha

translation.en: wherever they are; in the east or the west of the earth
on the lands or in the oceans,
and, through him, fill in the earth with justice,
manifest the religion of Your Prophet,

segments.transliteration: salla allahu `alayhi wa alihi wa sallama

translation.en: blessings and peace of You be upon him and his Household,

segments.transliteration: waj`alni allahumma min ansarihi wa a`wanihi

translation.en: make me, O Allah, of his supporters, helpers,
followers, and adherents;

segments.arabic: وَأَرِنِي فِي آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلسَّلاَمُ مَا يَأْمُلُونَ
segments.transliteration: wa arini fi ali muhammadin `alayhim alssalamu ma ya'muluna

translation.en: allow me to see the hopes of the family of Muhammad, peace be upon them, come true,
and the hazards of their enemies come true.
O God of Truth, respond.
O Lord of Glory and Honor!
O most merciful of those who show mercy!

text.en: Another Form of Ziyarah. The following form of ziyarah is quoted from the highly esteemed reference books: You may stop at the gate of the holy vault and say the following words:
text.ar: زيارة أخرى منقولة عَنْ الكتب المعتبرة : قف على باب حرمه الشريف وَقل :

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O vicegerent of Allah
and vicegerent of your well-guided fathers.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا وَصِيَّ ٱلأَوْصِيَاءِ ٱلْمَاضِينَ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O successor of the past successors (of the Prophets).

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا حَافِظَ أَسْرَارِ رَبِّ ٱلْعَالَمِينَ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya hafiza asrari rabbi al`alamina

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O keeper of the secrets of the Lord of the worlds.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا بَقِيَّةَ ٱللَّهِ مِنَ ٱلصَّفْوَةِ ٱلْمُنْتَجَبِينَ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya baqiyyata allahi min alssafwati almuntajabina

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O Allah’s remainder from among the elite, select ones.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا بْنَ ٱلأَنْوَارِ ٱلزَّاهِرَةِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O son of the luminous lights.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا بْنَ ٱلأَعْلاَمِ ٱلْبَاهِرَةِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O son of the splendid signs.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا بْنَ ٱلْعِتْرَةِ ٱلطَّاهِرَةِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O son of the immaculate progeny.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا مَعْدِنَ ٱلْعُلُومِ ٱلنَّبَوِيَّةِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O essence of the Prophetic knowledge.
Peace be upon you, O Allah’s gate;
the only gate from which one can enter to Him.
Peace be upon you, O Allah’s course
that perdition will be the result of anyone who takes other than it.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا نَاظِرَ شَجَرَةِ طُوبَىٰ وَسِدْرَةِ ٱلْمُنْتَهَىٰ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya nazira shajarati tuba wa sidrati almuntaha

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O observer of the Tree of Bliss (tuba) and the Farthest Lot-tree.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا نُورَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلَّذِي لاََ يُطْفَىٰ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya nura allahi alladhi la yutfa

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O Allah’s light that never extinguishes.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا حُجَّةَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلَّتِي لاََ تَخْفَىٰ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka ya hujjata allahi allati la takhfa

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O Allah’s decisive argument that is never hidden.
Peace be upon you, O Allah’s decisive argument
against all those on the earth and in the heavens.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ سَلاَمَ مَنْ عَرَفَكَ بِمَا عَرَّفَكَ بِهِ ٱللَّهُ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka salama man `arafaka bima `arrafaka bihi allahu

translation.en: Peace be upon you; a salutation of one who recognizes you as exactly as Allah has introduced you
and one who mentions only some of your characteristics
that you are worthy of and even more.

segments.arabic: أَشْهَدُ أَنَّكَ ٱلْحُجَّةُ عَلَىٰ مَنْ مَضَىٰ وَمَنْ بَقِيَ
segments.transliteration: ashhadu annaka alhujjatu `ala man mada wa man baqiya

translation.en: I bear witness that you are the argument against the past and the coming generations,
your party are the true triumphant,
your friends are the true winners,
your enemies are truly losers,
you are the keeper of the entire knowledge,
you are the opener of all closed things,
you are the establisher of all truths,
and you are the eradicator of all wrong things.

segments.transliteration: radituka ya mawlaya imaman wa hadiyan

translation.en: O my master, I accept you as my leader, guide,
master, and adviser.
I never seek any alternative

segments.transliteration: wa la attakhidhu min dunika waliyyan

translation.en: and I never betake myself any master save You.
I bear witness that you are verily the unchanging truth
that is free of any defect

segments.transliteration: wa anna wa`da allahi fika haqqun

translation.en: and that Allah’s promise concerning you is so true

segments.arabic: لاََ أَرْتَابُ لِطُولِ ٱلغَيْبَةِ وَبُعْدِ ٱلأَمَدِ
segments.transliteration: la artabu lituli alghaybati wa bu`di al-amadi

translation.en: that I never doubt no matter how long the occultation will take and the time (of your advent) will be far

segments.arabic: وَلاَ أَتَحَيَّرُ مَعَ مَنْ جَهِلَكَ وَجَهِلَ بِكَ
segments.transliteration: wa la atahayyaru ma`a man jahilaka wa jahila bika

translation.en: and I will never have misgivings like those who ignore you and ignore the truth that you represent
and I am waiting and expecting your age,

segments.transliteration: wa anta alshshafi`u alladhi la tunaza`u

translation.en: for you are the unmatched intercessor
and the irrefutable master.
Allah has saved you for the support of the religion,
for the fortification of the believers,
and for retaliating upon the deniers and the apostates.
I bear witness that through the loyalty to you, deeds are approved,
acts are purified,
rewards are doubled,
and punishments for wrongdoings are erased.
Therefore, he who bears loyalty to you
and recognizes your Imamate
will have his deeds approved,
his sayings credited,
his rewards doubled,
and his wrongdoings erased;
while if he rejects loyalty to you,
ignores the recognition of you,
and follows anyone else,

segments.arabic: كَبَّهُ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ مَنْخَرِهِ فِي ٱلنَّارِ

translation.en: Allah shall throw him down in Hellfire on the nose,
shall never approve of any of his acts,

segments.arabic: وَلَمْ يُقِمْ لَهُ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَامَةِ وَزْناً
segments.transliteration: wa lam yuqim lahu yawma alqiyamati waznan

translation.en: and shall never set up a balance for him on the Day of Resurrection.
I ask Allah and His angels
and you, my master, to testify to this for me;
the outward of this declaration is as same as its inward,
and its secret is as same as its overtness;
and you are the witness on this declaration,
and this is my pledge with you
and my covenant to you
since you are verily the system of the religion,
the leader of the pious ones,
and the dignity of the monotheists.
This is the command of the Lord of the Worlds to me.
Even if times take longer periods
and ages extend,
this will increase in me nothing but more belief in you,
love for you,
reliance on and trust in you,

segments.transliteration: wa lizuhurika illa mutawaqqi`an wa muntaziran

translation.en: anticipation and expectation of your advent,
and waiting to strive hard in your presence.
So, I sacrifice myself, my fortune,
