my sons, my family members,

segments.arabic: وَجَمِيعَ مَا خَوَّلَنِي رَبِّي بَيْنَ يَدَيْكَ
segments.transliteration: wa jami`a ma khawwalani rabbi bayna yadayka

translation.en: and all that which my Lord has granted me for your sake
and for obeying your orders and refraining from that which you order me not to do.
O my master, If I catch your thriving days
and see your splendid signs,
then, here I, a servant of you,
am yielding to your orders and abstaining from that which you order me not to do.
Through all that, I hope for martyrdom in your presence
and success in your view.

segments.arabic: مَوْلاَيَ فَإِنْ أَدْرَكَنِيَ ٱلْمَوْتُ قَبْلَ ظُهُورِكَ
segments.transliteration: mawlaya fa'in adrakani almawtu qabla zuhurika

translation.en: O my master, but if death comes upon me before your advent,
then I beseech you

segments.transliteration: wa bi'aba'ika alttahirina ila allahi ta`ala

translation.en: and your immaculate fathers to mediate for me before Allah the All-exalted

segments.arabic: وَأَسْأَلُهُ أَنْ يُصَلِّيَ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: wa as'aluhu an yusalliya `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin

translation.en: Whom I pray to send blessings upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad

segments.arabic: وَأَنْ يَجْعَلَ لِي كَرَّةً فِي ظُهُورِكَ
segments.transliteration: wa an yaj`ala li karratan fi zuhurika

translation.en: and to decide for me a return after your advent
and a second life during your days
so that I will attain obedience to you, which is my desire,
and to heal my heart from your enemies.
O my master, while I am visiting you, I am standing
in the position of the repentant wrongdoers,
who fear the chastisement of the Lord of the Worlds
and I am, meanwhile, depending upon your intercession for me
and I, through my loyalty to you and your intercession for me, wish for
eradication of my sins,
covering of my defects,
and forgiveness of my slips.
So, O my master, (please) help me—your servant—
to achieve my hope
and (please) ask Allah to forgive my slips.
Verily, I am clinging to your rope,
holding on my loyalty to you,
and declaring my disavowal of your enemies.
O Allah, (please) bless Muhammad and his Household
and fulfill Your promise to Your Vicegerent.
O Allah, (please) make prevalent his word,
elevate his mission,
and support him against Your and his enemies,
O Lord of the Worlds!

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍٍ
segments.transliteration: allahumma salli `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin

translation.en: O Allah, (please) send blessings upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad
and allow Your Perfect Word,
Your concealed one in Your lands,
the afraid, and the vigilant, to reappear.
O Allah, (please) support him with powerful support
and give him a nigh victory.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ وَأَعِزَّ بِهِ ٱلدِّينَ بَعْدَ ٱلْخُمُولِ
segments.transliteration: allahumma wa a`izza bihi alddina ba`da alkhumuli

translation.en: O Allah, (please) fortify the religion through him after it has been sluggish,

segments.transliteration: wa atli` bihi alhaqqa ba`da al-ufuli

translation.en: demonstrate the truth through him after it has declined,
remove darkness through him,
and relieve the grief through him.
O Allah, (please) spread security over the lands through him
and guide the servants (of You) through him.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ ٱمْلَأْ بِهِ ٱلأَرْضَ عَدْلاًَ وَقِسْطاً
segments.transliteration: allahumma imla bihi al-arda `adlan wa qistan

translation.en: O Allah, (please) fill the earth with justice and equality through him
as it is filled with injustice and wrong.
Verily, You are All-hearing and Responding.
Peace be upon you, O Allah’s vicegerent.

segments.arabic: إِئْذَنْ لِوَلِيِّكَ فِي ٱلدُّخُولِ إِلَىٰ حَرَمِكَ
segments.transliteration: i'dhan liwaliyyika fi alddukhuli ila haramika

translation.en: (please) permit me, your servant, to enter your sanctuary.
May Allah’s blessings be upon you
and upon your immaculate fathers.
Allah’s mercy and benedictions be upon you.

text.en: You may then come to the Occultation Vault and stop between the two doors, holding the door’s side with your hand. You may then hem and haw, as if you are seeking permission of entrance, and say these words:
text.ar: ثم ائت سرداب الغيبة وقف بين البابين ماسكا جانب الباب بيدك ثم تنحنح كالمستأذن وقل :

translation.en: In the Name of Allah; the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.

text.en: You may now come down with tranquility and present-heartedness. Offer a two-unit prayer in the yard of the vault and say the following words:
text.ar: وانزل بسكينة وحضور قلب وصل ركعتين في عرصة السرداب وقل :

segments.arabic: اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ ٱللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ ٱللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ
segments.transliteration: allahu akbaru allahu akbaru allahu akbaru

translation.en: Allah is the Most Great. Allah is the Most Great. Allah is the Most Great.
There is no god save Allah.
Allah is the Most Great and to Allah be all praise.
All praise be to Allah Who has guided us to this,
made us discriminate between His friends and enemies,
granted us success to visit our Imams,

segments.transliteration: wa lam yaj`alna min almu`anidina alnnasibina

translation.en: and has not made us of the obdurate antagonists (of the truth),
nor of the immoderate indeterminists,
nor of the suspicious and negligent.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَىٰ وَلِيِّ ٱللَّهِ وَٱبْنِ أَوْلِيَائِهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon Allah’s vicegerent and son of His vicegerents.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَىٰ ٱلْمُدَّخَرِ لِكَرَامَةِ أَوْلِيَاءِ ٱللَّهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon the one saved for achieving dignity of Allah’s intimate servants
and for ruining Allah’s enemies.
Peace be upon the light
that the people of atheism intend to extinguish;

segments.arabic: فَأَبَىٰ ٱللَّهُ إِِلاَّ أَنْ يُتِمَّ نُورَهُ بِكُرْهِهِمْ
segments.transliteration: fa'aba allahu illa an yutimma nurahu bikurhihim

translation.en: but Allah would not allow but that His light should be perfected, despite their contempt.
He thus supported him with survival

segments.arabic: حَتَّىٰ يُظْهِرَ عَلَىٰ يَدِهِ ٱلْحَقَّ بِرَغْمِهِمْ

translation.en: so that He shall make prevalent the truth at his hands against their will.
I bear witness that Allah has chosen you since you were little
and perfected His knowledge for you when you grew up;
and that you are alive and you will not die
before you efface all idols and false deities.
O Allah, (please) bless him

segments.arabic: وَعَلَىٰ خُدَّامِهِ وَأَعْوَانِهِ عَلَىٰ غَيْبَتِهِ وَنَأْيِهِ
segments.transliteration: wa `ala khuddamihi wa a`wanihi `ala ghaybatihi wa na'yihi

translation.en: and his servants and those who helped him to occult and hide from sights,
cover him with invulnerable covering,
arrange for him an invincible haven,
increase, O Allah, Your intensity on those who resist him obdurately,
and guard his loyalists and visitors.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ كَمَا جَعَلْتَ قَلْبِي بِذِكْرِهِ مَعْمُوراً
segments.transliteration: allahumma kama ja`alta qalbi bidhikrihi ma`muran

translation.en: O Allah, just as You have made my heart full of reference to him,
so also (please) make my weapon prepared in support of him.

segments.transliteration: wa in hala bayni wa bayna liqa'ihi

translation.en: If death that You have made inescapable on Your servants

segments.arabic: ٱلْمَوْتُ ٱلَّذِي جَعَلْتَهُ عَلَىٰ عِبَادِكَ حَتْماً

translation.en: and You have made inevitably predestined on Your creatures
impedes me from meeting him,
then (please) restore me to life at his advent,
coming out from my hole (i.e. grave),
and using my shroud as my garment
so that I will fight strivingly in his presence

segments.arabic: فِي ٱلصَّفِّ ٱلَّذِي أَثْنَيْتَ عَلَىٰ أَهْلِهِ

translation.en: in the line whose people have been praised by You
in You Book where You say,
“…as if they were a firm and compact wall.”
O Allah, too long has been the expectation,
the wicked are gloating at our misfortune,
and victory has been difficult for us.
O Allah, (please) show us the blessed face of Your vicegerent
in our lifetimes and after death.
O Allah, I profess of the Return as my faith
in the presence of the owner of this area.
[I beseech for] Relief, relief, relief.
O Patron of the Age!
I have ruptured my relations with many friends in order to build good relations with you,
I have deserted my homeland in order to visit you,
and I have concealed my intention from the peoples of the towns
so that you may be my intercessor with your and my Lord
and with your fathers and my masters,
seeking excellence of success,
conferral of bounties,
and making charitable acts run towards me.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍٍ
segments.transliteration: allahumma salli `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin

translation.en: O Allah, (please) send blessings upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad
the rightful ones
and the leaders of the creatures,
and (please) respond to me in what I have besought You for

segments.arabic: وَأَعْطِنِي مَا لَمْ أَنْطِقْ بِهِ فِي دُعَائِي
segments.transliteration: wa a`tini ma lam antiq bihi fi du`a'i

translation.en: and grant me all that which I have not mentioned in my prayer,
such as the uprightness of my faith and my worldly affairs.
You are verily worthy of praise and worthy of glory.

segments.arabic: وَصَلِّىٰ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِهِ ٱلطَّاهِرِينَ
segments.transliteration: wa salla allahu `ala muhammadin wa alihi alttahirina

translation.en: May Allah bless Muhammad and his Immaculate Household.

text.en: You may then enter the hall, offer a two-unit prayer, and say the following words:
text.ar: ثم ادخل الصّفة فصل ركعتين وقل :

translation.en: O Allah, I—Your visiting servant—
am in the yard of Your visited vicegerent

segments.arabic: ٱلَّذِي فَرَضْتَ طَاعَتَهُ عَلَىٰ ٱلْعَبِيدِ وَٱلأَحْرَارِ

translation.en: the obedience to whom has been made obligatory by You on the slaves and the free

segments.arabic: وَأَنْقَذْتَ بِهِ أَوْلِيَاءَكَ مِنْ عَذَابِ ٱلنَّارِ
segments.transliteration: wa anqadhta bihi awliya'aka min `adhabi alnnari

translation.en: and through whom You shall save Your intimate servants from the chastisement of Hellfire.
O Allah, (please) decide it to be an admitted visitation
of a granted prayer
from one who gives full credence to Your vicegerent and who never suspects him.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ لاََ تَجْعَلْهُ آخِرَ ٱلْعَهْدِ بِهِ وَلاَ بِزِيَارَتِهِ
segments.transliteration: allahumma la taj`alhu akhira al`ahdi bihi wa la biziyaratihi

translation.en: O Allah, (please) do not decide this to be the last of my presence and visitation to him

segments.transliteration: wa la taqta` athari min mashhadihi

translation.en: and do not cut off my traces in his shrine
and in visitation to his father and grandfather.
O Allah, (please) compensate for me my expenditure,
make me benefit from the sustenance that You have provided me
in my worldly life and in my Next Life,
and do the same to my brothers, parents,
and all of my offspring.
I entrust you with Allah, O Leader
through whom the believers will win
and at whose hands the belying unbelievers will perish.

segments.arabic: يَا مَوْلاَيَ يَا بْنَ ٱلْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ

translation.en: O my master! O son of al-Hasan the son of `Ali,

segments.transliteration: ji'tuka za'iran laka wa li'abika wa jaddika

translation.en: I have come to you visiting you, your father, and your grandfather,
being certain of winning through you,
and having full faith in your Imamate.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ ٱكْتُبْ هٰذِهِ ٱلشَّهَادَةَ وَٱلزِّيَارَةَ لِي
segments.transliteration: allahumma uktub hadhihi alshshahadata walzziyarata li

translation.en: O Allah, (please) write down this testimony and visitation to me
with You in the Most Elevated Place,
make me attain that which is attained by the righteous ones,
and make me benefit by my love for them, O Lord of the Worlds.

text.en: Another Form of Ziyarah reported by Sayyid Ibn Tawus:
text.ar: زيارة أخرى : وهي مارواها السيّد ابن طاووس ، تقول :

translation.en: Peace be upon the fresh truth
and the knowledgeable whose knowledge never expires.
Peace be upon the reviver of the believers
and the terminator of the unbelievers.
Peace be upon the restorer of all nations
and the combiner of all creeds.
Peace be upon the successor of the ancestors
and the owner of honor.
Peace be upon the argument of the Worshipped (Lord)
and the Word of the Praised (Lord).
Peace be upon the consolidator of the intimate servants (of God)
and the humiliator of the enemies.
Peace be upon the inheritor of the Prophets
and the seal of the (Prophets’) successors.
Peace be upon the Rising (Imam), the awaited,
and the famed establisher of justice.
Peace be upon the unsheathed sword,
the brilliant moon,
and the dazzling light.
Peace be upon the sun of darkness
and the absolutely full moon.
Peace be upon the springtime of humanity
and the bloom of days.
Peace be upon the holder of sharp sword
and the splitter of heads.
Peace be upon the long-established faith
and the scripture inscribed.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَىٰ بَقِيَّةِ ٱللَّهِ فِي بِلاَدِهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon the left by Allah in His lands
and the argument of Him against His servants
to whom the inheritances of the Prophets end
and with whom the traditions of the elite ones are kept.
Peace be upon the one entrusted with the secret
and the one who shall undertake the mission.

segments.transliteration: alssalamu `ala almahdiyyi alladhi wa`ada allahu `azza wa jalla bihi al-umama

translation.en: Peace be upon al-Mahdi (the rightly guided)

segments.arabic: ٱلَّذِي وَعَدَ ٱللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ بِهِ ٱلأُمَمَ

translation.en: through whom Allah the Almighty and All-majestic promised the nations
to combine all decrees,

segments.transliteration: wa yamla'a bihi al-arda qistan wa `adlan

translation.en: to bring together all scattered matters,
to fill the earth with fairness and justice,
to make possible for him (to win over the all),
and to fulfill His promise to the believers.
I bear witness, O my master,
that you and the Imams, your fathers,

segments.transliteration: fi alhayati alddunya wa yawma yaqumu al-ashhadu

translation.en: are my leaders and masters

segments.arabic: فِي ٱلْحَيَاةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَيَوْمَ يَقُومُ ٱلأَشْهَادُ

translation.en: in this worldly life and on the day when the Witnesses will rise (to witness).

segments.transliteration: an tas'ala allaha tabaraka wa ta`ala

translation.en: I thus ask you, O my master,
to ask Allah the All-blessed and All-exalted
to set aright all my affairs,
to grant me all my needs,
to forgive my sins,

segments.transliteration: fi dini wa dunyaya wa akhirati

translation.en: and to lead me rightly

segments.transliteration: li wa li'ikhwani wa akhawati almu'minina walmu'minati kaffatan

translation.en: in the affairs of my faith, worldly life, and the Next Life,

segments.arabic: لِي وَلإِخْوَانِي وَأَخَوَاتِي ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنَاتِ كَافَّةً

translation.en: as well as the affairs of my all believing brothers and sisters.
Verily, He is All-forgiving, All-merciful.

text.en: You may then offer the twelve-unit prayer of ziyarah, separating between each two units with a Taslim statement (i.e. the last part of prayers). You may then utter the (famous) Tasbih al-Zahra' litany and provide all that as present to the Imam. Upon accomplishment, you may say the following supplicatory prayer:
text.ar: ثم صلّ صلاة الزيارة بما قدمناه ، أي اثنتي عشرة ركعة ، تسلّم بعد كل ركعتين منها ، وتسبح تسبيح الزهراء عليها‌السلام ، واهدها إليه عليه‌السلام فإذا فرغت من صلاة الزيارة فقل :

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَىٰ حُجَّتِكَ فِي أَرْضِكَ

translation.en: O Allah, (please) bless Your Argument in Your land,
Your representative in Your countries,
the caller to Your way,
the establisher of Your justice,
the winner of Your decree,
the guardian of the believers,
the terminator of the unbelievers,
the eliminator of darkness,
the illuminator of the truth,
the expounder with wisdom,
excellent exhortation, and veracity,
Your Word, the carrier of Your secrets,
Your Eye in Your lands,
the vigilant, the afraid,
the sincere vicegerent,
the ark of salvation,
the pennon of right guidance,
the light of the eyes of all creatures,
the best of all those who dress shirts and garments,
the unique whose folks have not been avenged yet,
the reliever of agonies,
the remover of distress,
and the mitigator of ordeals.
Allah’s blessings be incessantly upon him
and upon his fathers, the guiding Imams
and the blessed leaders
whenever stars of early dawn rise,
trees are furnished with leaves,
fruits are yielded,
night and day follow each other,
and birds warble.
O Allah, (please) make us benefit by our love for him
and gather us in his group
and under his pennon.
O God of Truth, respond.
O Lord of the Worlds!

text.en: Invocation of Blessings upon Imam al-Mahdi
text.ar: الصلاة عليه عليه‌السلام :

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَأَهْلِ بَيْتِهِ
segments.transliteration: allahumma salli `ala muhammadin wa ahli baytihi

translation.en: O Allah, (please) send blessings upon Muhammad and his Household
and send blessings upon al-Hasan’s vicegerent,
successor, and inheritor;
the establisher of Your decrees,
the absent among Your creatures,
and the awaiting for Your permission.
O Allah, (please) bless him,
make imminent his remoteness,
fulfill Your promise to him,
keep Your pledge to him,
reveal the screen of occultation against his power,
manifest the pages of ordeal through his advent,
bring the horror before him,
make firm the heart through him,
wage the war through him,

segments.transliteration: wa ayyid-hu bijundin min almala'ikati musawwimina

translation.en: aid him with marked troops from the heavens,
give him full dominance over all the enemies of Your religion,

segments.arabic: وَأَلْهِمْهُ أَنْ لاََ يَدَعَ مِنْهُمْ رُكْناً إِلاَّ هَدَّهُ
segments.transliteration: wa alhimhu an la yada`a minhum ruknan illa haddahu

translation.en: and inspire him not to leave any corner of them but that he demolishes it,
any head but that he splits it,
any trick but that he frustrates it,
any sinful one but that he exposes him to punishment,
any tyrant but that he annihilates him,
any screen but that he exposes it,
any pennon but that he lowers it,
any authority but that he seizes it,
any lance but that he smashes it,
any spear but that he shatters it,
any troops but that he takes them apart,
any pulpit but that he burns it,
any sword but that he breaks it,
any idol but that he bruises it,
any blood but that he sheds it,
any persecution but that he annihilates it,
any fortress but that he destroys it,
any door but that he fills it up,
any castle but that he destroys it,
any house but that he scans it,
any plain but that he treads on it,
any mountain but that he climbs it,
and any treasure but that he extracts it;
in the name of Your mercy, O most merciful of all those who show mercy!

text.en: When Shaykh al-Mufid mentioned the aforesaid form of ziyarah that begins with the following words:
text.ar: أورد المفيد الزيارة السالفة التي أولها :

segments.arabic: اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ ٱللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ ٱللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ
segments.transliteration: allahu akbaru allahu akbaru allahu akbaru

translation.en: Allah is the Most Great. Allah is the Most Great. Allah is the Most Great.
There is no god save Allah…

text.en: he (i.e. Shaykh al-Mufid), commenting, said the following:According to another way of narration, you may say the following words while descending to the holy vault:
text.ar: ثم قال : روي بطريق آخر تقول عند نزول السرداب :

translation.en: Peace be upon the fresh truth
and the knowledgeable whose knowledge never expires…

text.en: So, he referred to the previously mentioned form of ziyarah up to the end. He then said:
text.ar: فأورد الزيارة إلى موضع صلاتها ثم قال :

text.en: You may now offer the twelve-unit prayer of ziyarah, separating between each two units with a Taslim statement.
text.ar: ثم تصلي صلاة الزيارة اثنتي عشرة ركعة كل ركعتين بتسليمة

text.en: You may then say the following supplicatory prayer, which is reported from Imam al-Mahdi (`a) himself:
text.ar: ثم تدعو بعدها بالدعاء المروي عنه عليه‌السلام وهو :

translation.en: O my God, ordeal has become immeasurable.
The hidden has become exposed.
The covered has been disclosed.
The earth has become narrow (despite its vastness).
The sky has been prevented (from giving its blessings).
And to You, O my Lord, is the complaint.
Upon You is the reliance in hardships and leisure.
O Allah, (please do) send blessings upon Muhammad and his Household
the obedience to whom has been made obligatory upon us by You,
and whose (special) rank has been thus made known for us by You,
(please do) relieve our ordeals in the name of them
with a relief that is as immediate
as the twinkling of an eye or even closer.
O Muhammad! O `Ali!
O `Ali! O Muhammad!
(Please) support me, for you both do support me,
and save me, for you both do save.
O my master! O Patron of the Age!
(I beseech you for) relief! (I beseech you for) relief! (I beseech you for) relief!
(Please do) come to my aid! (Please do) come to my aid! (Please do) come to my aid!

text.en: This supplicatory prayer is of a high esteem; it is therefore advisable to repeat it in the holy sanctuary of Imam al-Mahdi (`a) as well as other places. I have previously mentioned it in the first section of the book, yet with little difference is statements.
text.ar: هذا دعاء شريف وينبغي أن يكرر الدّعاء به في ذلك الحرم الشريف وفي غيره من الأماكن. ونحن قد أثبتناه في الباب الأول باختلاف يسير.

text.en: Another Form of Ziyarah of Imam al-Mahdi. Sayyid Ibn Tawus has instructed the following: You may offer a two-unit prayer and then say the following words:
text.ar: الزيارة الاُخِرى. ما رواه السيد ابن طاووس قال : صلّ ركعتين وقل بعدها :

translation.en: Peace of Allah—perfect, complete,
comprehensive, and thorough…

text.en: This form of ziyarah can be found in the first section of this book (pp. 458, Vol. 1) under the title: Seeking Aid From Imam al-Mahdi (Al-Istighathah bi’l-Hujjah.) as quoted from the book of al-Kalim al-Tayyib.
text.ar: ونحن قد أثبتناها في الفصل السابع من الباب الأول تحت عنوان الاستغاثة به عليه‌السلام نقلاً عن كتاب الكلم الطيب فراجعها هناك ص ١٧٦.

text.en: Other Rites at the Holy Vault
text.ar: اعمال اخری في السرداب

text.en: In his book of Misbah al-Za'ir, Sayyid Ibn Tawus has dedicated a chapter to the devotional acts at the holy vault where he has mentioned six forms of ziyarah. He then said: Attached to this chapter are the Supplication of Lamentation <ref=3:1305>Dua Al-Nudba</ref>
text.ar: أفرد السيد ابن طاووس في كتاب مصباح الزائر فصلاً لاعمال السرداب المقدس فأثبت فيه ست زيارات ثم قال : ويلحق بهذا الفصل <ref=3:1305>دعاء النُّدبة</ref>

text.en: the ziyarah form that is said every day after the Fajr (dawn) Prayer, which is the seventh form of ziyarah
text.ar: وما يزار به مولانا صاحب الأمر عليه‌السلام في كل يوم بعد فريضة الفجر وهي السابعة من الزيارات

text.en: , the supplication known as Du`a' al-`Ahd (Supplication of the Covenant) which we have been ordered to say in the Occultation Age, and the prayer that is said when we intend to leave that holy sanctuary.
text.ar: ودعاء العهد الذي أُمرنا بتلاوته في زمان الغيبة ومايدعى به عند إرادة الخروج من ذلك الحرم الشّريف.

text.en: Morning/Evening Dua
text.ar: الدعاء عند طلوع الصبح وغروب الشمس

text.en: Seventh:It is narrated in Kafi, through a chain of narrators, from Furaat Ibn al-Ahnaf, who narrates from Imam Sadiq (a.s.) that he (a.s.) said, You may neglect to do anything, but never neglect saying the following prayer in mornings and evenings. Ref Al-Kaafi, vol. 2, p. 529, “The Book of Supplications”
text.ar: وعنه عليه‌السلام أيضاً قال : مهما تركت من شي فلا تترك أن تقول في كل صباح ومساء :

translation.en: O Allah, I implore for You forgiveness
in this morning
and on this day
for purpose of winning Your mercy.

segments.transliteration: wa abra'u ilayka min ahli la`natika

translation.en: I disavow in Your presence the people cursed by You.
O Allah, I disavow in Your presence
on this day
and in this morning

segments.arabic: مِمَّنْ نَحْنُ بَيْنَ ظَهْرَانيْهِمْ مِنَ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ
segments.transliteration: mimman nahnu bayna zahranihim min almushrikina

translation.en: the polytheists we live among them
and (disavow) what they are worshipping.
Truly, they were a people given to evil, a rebellious people.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ ٱجْعَلْ مَا أَنْزَلْتَ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَاءِ إِلَىٰ ٱلأَرْضِ
segments.transliteration: allahumma ij`al ma anzalta min alssama'i ila al-ardi

translation.en: O Allah, (please) cause what You have brought down from the heavens to the earth
in this morning
and on this day
to be blessing for Your saints
and punishment poured on Your enemies.
O Allah, (please) accede to those who are loyal to You
and feud with those who act hostilely towards You.
O Allah, (please) seal my affairs with security and faith
whenever the sun rises or sets.
O Allah, (please) forgive me and my parents
and have mercy upon them as they have cherished me in my childhood.
O Allah, (please) forgive the believing men and women
and the Muslim men and women,
both the alive and the dead.

segments.transliteration: allahumma innaka ta`lamu munqalabahum wa mathwahum

translation.en: O Allah, You do know their place of returning and their place of rest.
O Allah, (please) safeguard the leader of Muslims
with the protection of faith,
help him with powerful help,
grant him easy victory,

segments.arabic: وَٱجْعَلْ لَهُ وَلَنَا مِنْ لَدُنْكَ سُلْطَاناً نَصِيراً
segments.transliteration: waj`al lahu wa lana min ladunka sultanan nasiran

translation.en: and grant him and us from Your presence a sustaining power.
O Allah, (please) curse so-and-so and so-and-so­
as well as the factions that have dissented from Your Messenger,
from the men of authority after Your Messenger,
from the leaders who came after him, and from their partisans.
I also beseech You from more favor,

segments.arabic: وَٱلإِقْرَارَ بِمَا جَاءَ بِهِ مِنْ عِنْدِكَ

translation.en: acknowledgement of what has come from You,
submission to Your decree,
and steadfastness in what You have ordered of.
I neither intend to find another alternative than it

segments.transliteration: wa la ashtari bihi thamanan qalilan

translation.en: nor do I take a mean price in exchange for it.
O Allah, (please) guide me among those whom You guide
and save me from the evil of what You have decided,
for You decree and no one can decree against Your decree
and no humiliation will ever afflict one whom You support.
Blessed by You and exalted by You.
Glory be to You, O Lord of the House.
Accept my prayer.

segments.arabic: وَمَا تَقَرَّبْتُ بِهِ إِلَيْكَ مِنْ خَيْرٍ
segments.transliteration: wa ma taqarrabtu bihi ilayka min khayrin

translation.en: As for whatever virtuous item through which I have sought nearness to You,
(please) double it for me many folds.
Grant us from Your Presence a great reward.
O my Lord, how excellent Your favors to me are!
How great Your donations to me are!
How lasting the wellbeing with which You have endued me is!
Too many times have You covered my faults!
So, all praise be to You, O my God;
such praise that is pleasant, blessed,
as full as the heavens,
as full as the earth,

segments.transliteration: wa mil'a ma sha'a rabbi wa radiya

translation.en: as full as what my Lord wills and pleases,
and as it fits the Face of my Lord,
the Lord of majesty and honoring.

text.en: Supplicating Every Morning and Evening:
The supplicant adorns his days and nights with beautiful deeds
slaves and servants present themselves before their masters and lords every morning and evening, expressing their willingness to serve and gratitude for the bounties.

Then surely, we are more worthy of this because we know that all that Allah, Mighty and Glorified be He, has bestowed upon us of the abundant bounties and innumerable obligations, it is only due to the holy blessings of our master, Imam-e-Zamaan (a.t.f.s.).
So, it is essential that you present yourself with all your limbs and total existence, every morn and eve, in his presence.

You should know that you are being watched and heard as he (a.t.f.s.) sees you although you don’t see him (a.t.f.s.) as he make preparations for the one whom he loves and cares for. As he (a.t.f.s.) wrote to Shaykh Mufeed (a.r.),
text.ar: اِنَّا غَيْرُ مُهْمِلِيْنَ لِمُرَاعَاتِكُمْ وَ لاَ نَاسِيْنَ لِذِكْرِكُمْ، وَ لَوْلاَ ذٰلِكَ لَنَزَلَ بِكُمُ الْلأَوَاء وَ اصْطَلَمَتْكُمُ اْلأَعْدَاءُ...

text.en: In his book of al-Najm al-Thaqib, Shaykh al-Nuri, may Allah have mercy upon him, has quoted the story of building the mosque of Jamkaran, a town about five kilometers away from the holy city of Qum, by the command of the Patron of the Age (a.s.). He has thus quoted Imam al-Mahdi(a.s.) as saying to Hasan al-Mathulah of Jamkaran, “Advice people to visit frequently and venerably this place and to offer four units of prayer therein; two units as greeting of the mosque in each unit they should recite Surah al-Faatehah once and repeat Surah al-Tawheed (or al-Ikhlaas) seven times. In the genuflection and prostration of this prayer, they may repeat the doxologies of glorifying God seven times. As for the other two-unit prayer, they may dedicate it to Imam al-Mahdi(a.s.). In the first unit, they may recite Surah al-Faatehah, but when they reach this holy verse, they should repeat it one hundred times:
text.ar: وهو يبعد عن بلدة قم الطيبة مسافة فرسخ واحد وقد حكى الشيخ رض في كتاب (النجم الثاقب) حديث بناء هذا الجامع بأمر من صاحب العصر عليه‌السلام وذلك في الحكاية الأولى من الباب السابع من الكتاب وقد أتى في ذلك الحديث أنه (صلوات الله وسلامه عليه) قال لحسن المثلة الجمكراني : قل للناس ليرغبوا في هذا الموضع وليغيروه وليصلوا فيه أربع ركعتان منها لتحية المسجد يقرأ في كل ركعة منها الحمد مرة و (قل هو الله أحد) سبع مرات ويسبح سبعا في كل ركوع وسجود ، وركعتان منها صلاة الحجة عليه‌السلام يقرأ المصلّي في الأولى سورة الفاتحة فإذا بلغ الآية :

translation.en: Ref Al-Kaafi, vol. 2, p. 529, “The Book of Supplications”

text.en: They may then continue reciting this Surah. The same thing may be done in the second unit. The doxologies of glorifying God that are said in the genuflection and prostrations of the prayers may be repeated seven times in this prayer. Upon accomplishment of the prayer, they may repeat the tahlil statement (i.e. la-ilaha illa’llahu) one hundred times and then utter the Tasbih al-Zahra’ litany. Immediately after that, they may prostrate themselves and repeat the invocation of blessings upon the Holy Prophet and his Household one hundred times.” Imam al-Mahdi(a.s.) added, “Whoever offers this prayer will be awarded the reward of offering prayer in the Holy Ka’bah.”
text.ar: كرّرها مائة مرة ثم أتّم الفاتحة ويفعل مثل ذلك في الركعة الثانية ويسبّح سبعا في كل ركوع وسجود فإذا أتمّ الصلاة هلل وسبّح تسبيح الزهراء (صلوات الله وسلامه عليها) فإذا فرغ من التسبيح سجد وصلّى على النبي وآله مائة مرة وهذه الكلمة مروية بنصها عنه عليه‌السلام قال : فمن صلاهما فكأنّما صلّى في البيت العتيق ، أي الكعبة

text.en: Salutation on Imam Hussain(as) by Imam Mahdi(ajtfs). Of the several prescribed Ziyarat of Imam al-Husain (as), one was recited by Imam al-Mahdi(ATFS) and reached us through one of his four special deputies. For that reason, it is known as Ziyarat al-Nahiya al-Muqaddas. One of the most important features of this Ziyarat is that in different phrases, Imam al-Mahdi (ATFS) graphically describes the events of Ashura and the agony that Imam al-Husain (as) and his family faced on that horrific and ominous day.
text.ar: قال الشيخ المفيد قدس الله روحه في كتاب المزار بعد إيراد الزيارة التي نقلناها من المصباح ما هذا لفظه:  زيارة أخرى في يوم عاشورا برواية أخرى، إذا أردت زيارته بها في هذا اليوم فقف عليه صلى الله عليه وآله وقل:

text.en: “But as I have been hindered by the course of time, and (Allah’s) decree has prevented me from helping you, and as I could not fight those who fought you, and was not able to show hostility to those who showed hostility to you, I will, therefore, lament you morning and evening, and will weep blood in place of tears, out of my anguish for you and my sorrow for all that befell you…” —Imam al-Mahdi (ajtfs)

Of the several prescribed Ziyarat of Imam al-Husain (as), one was recited by Imam al-Mahdi (PBUH) and reached us through one of his four special deputies. For that reason, it is known as al-Muqaddasa. One of the most important features of this Ziyarat is that in different phrases, Imam al-Mahdi (as) graphically describes the events of Ashura and the agony that Imam al-Husain (as) and his family faced on that horrific and ominous day.

The text of the Ziyarat al-Nahiya is found in some early Ziyarat collections such as al-Mazar al-Kabir, by Muhammad Ibn Ja’far al-Mash’hadi, pp. 496-513. It is also reported in al-Mazar, by al-Mufid as mentioned in Bihar al-Anwar, vol. 98, pp. 318-329.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى آدَمَ صِفْوَةِ اللهِ مِنْ خَليقَتِهِ،

translation.en: Peace be upon Adam, the chosen one of Allah from among His creation.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى شَيْثٍ وَلِيِّ اللهِ وَ خِيَرَتِهِ،

translation.en: Peace be upon Seth (Shaith), the friend of Allah and His elite.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى إدْريسَ الْقائِمِ للهِِ بِحُجَّتِهِ،

translation.en: Peace be upon Enoch (Idris), who established (religion) on behalf of Allah by His authority.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى نُوحٍ الْمُجابِ في دَعْوَتِهِ،

translation.en: Peace be upon Noah (Nuh), whose invocation (for punishment) was answered.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى هُودٍ الْمَمْدُودِ مِنَ اللهِ بِمَعُونَتِهِ،
segments.transliteration: assalāmu alā hūdinil mamdūdi minal-lāhi bi-maūnatih,

translation.en: Peace be upon Hud, who was assisted through Allah’s aid.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى صالِحِ الَّذي تَوَّجَهُ اللهُ بِكَرامَتِهِ،

translation.en: Peace be upon Salih, whom Allah crowned with His generosity.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى إبْراهيمَ الَّذي حَباهُ اللهُ بِخُلَّتِهِ،

translation.en: Peace be upon Abraham (Ibrahim), whom Allah endowed with His friendship.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى إسْماعيلَ الَّذي فَداهُ اللهُ بِذِبْحٍ عَظيمٍ مِنْ جَنَّتِهِ،
segments.transliteration: assalāmu alā 'ismaīlal-ladhī fadāhul-lāhu bi-dhibħin ađīmin min jannatih

translation.en: Peace be upon Ishmael (Isma’il), whom Allah ransomed with a great sacrifice from His Heaven.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى إسْحاقَ الَّذي جَعَلَ اللهُ النُّبُوَّةَ في ذُرِّيَّتِهِ،

translation.en: Peace be upon Isaac (Is’haq), in whose progeny Allah placed prophethood.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى يَعْقُوبَ الَّذي رَدَّ اللهُ عَلَيْهِ بَصَرَهُ بِرَحْمَتِهِ،
segments.transliteration: assalāmu alā yaqūbal-ladhī raddal-lāhu alayhi bašarahu bi-raħmatih,

translation.en: Peace be upon Jacob (Ya’qub), for whom Allah restored his sight by His mercy.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى يُوسُفَ الَّذي نَجّاهُ اللهُ مِنَ الْجُبِّ بِعَظَمَتِهِ،
segments.transliteration: assalāmu alā yūsufal-ladhī najjāhul-lāhu minal jubbi bi-ađamatih,

translation.en: Peace be upon Joseph (Yusuf), whom Allah rescued from the well by His majesty.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى مُوسَى الَّذي فَلَقَ اللهُ الْبَحْرَ لَهُ بِقُدْرَتِهِ،
segments.transliteration: assalāmu alā mūsal-ladhī falaqal-lāhul baħra lahu bi-qudratih,

translation.en: Peace be upon Moses (Musa), the one for whom Allah split the sea with His Power.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى هارُونَ الَّذي خَصَّهُ اللهُ بِنُبُوَّتِهِ،

translation.en: Peace be upon Aaron (Harun), whom Allah distinguished with his prophethood.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى شُعَيْبِ الَّذي نَصَرَهُ اللهُ عَلى اُمَّتِهِ،

translation.en: Peace be upon Jethro (Shu’aib), whom Allah made victorious over his people.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى داوُدَ الَّذي تابَ اللهُ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ خَطيئَتِهِ،
segments.transliteration: assalāmu alā dāwūdal-ladhī tābal-lāhu alayhi min khaţī'atih,

translation.en: Peace be upon David (Dawud), to whom Allah turned (in mercy) after his mistake.[1]

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى سُلَيْمانَ الَّذي ذَلَّتْ لَهُ الْجِنُّ بِعِزَّتِهِ،
segments.transliteration: assalāmu alā sulaymānal-ladhīdhallat lahul jinnu bi-izzatih,

translation.en: Peace be upon Solomon (Sulaiman), for whom Allah made the Jinn subservient by His majesty.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى أيُّوبَ الَّذي شَفاهُ اللهُ مِنْ عِلَّتِهِ،

translation.en: Peace be upon Job (Ayyub), whom Allah cured after his (prolonged) illness.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى يُونُسَ الَّذي أنْجَزَ اللهُ لَهُ مَضْمُونَ عِدَتِهِ،
segments.transliteration: assalāmu alā yūnusal-ladhī 'anjazal-lāhu lahu mađhmūna idatih,

translation.en: Peace be upon Jonah (Yunus), for whom Allah fulfilled the purport of His promise.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى عُزَيْرِ الَّذي أحْياهُ اللهُ بَعْدَ ميتَتِهِ،

translation.en: Peace be upon Ezra (Uzair), whom Allah brought to life after his death.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى زَكَرِيّا الصّابِرِ في مِحْنَتِهِ،

translation.en: Peace be upon Zechariah (Zakariyya), who remained patient in his tribulations.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى يَحْيَى الَّذي أزْلَفَهُ اللهُ بِشَهادَتِهِ،

translation.en: Peace be upon John (Yahya), whom Allah drew near (his rank) by his martyrdom.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى عيسى رُوحِ اللهِ وَ كَلِمَتِهِ،

translation.en: Peace be upon Jesus (Isa), the spirit of Allah and His word.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ حَبيبِ اللهِ وَ صِفْوَتِهِ،
segments.transliteration: assalāmu alā muħammaddin ħabībil-lāhi wa šifwatih,

translation.en: Peace be upon Muhammad, the beloved of Allah and His elite.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى أميرِالْمُؤْمِنينَ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أبي طالِبٍ الْمَخْصُوصِ بِاُخُوَّتِهِ،
segments.transliteration: assalāmu alā 'amīril mu'minīna aliyy-ibni 'abī ţālibinil-makhšūši bi-'ukhuwwatih,

translation.en: Peace be upon the Leader of the Faithful, Ali Ibn Abi Talib, who was exclusively selected for brotherhood to him (the Prophet (PBUH&HF)).
Peace be upon Fatima al-Zahra, his daughter.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى أبي مُحَمَّدٍ الْحَسَنِ وَصِيِّ أبيهِ وَ خَليفَتِهِ،
segments.transliteration: assalāmu alā 'abi muħammadinil-ħasani wašiyyi 'abīhi wa khalīfatih,

translation.en: Peace be upon Abu Muhammad al-Hasan, the executor of (the will of) his father, and his successor.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلَى الْحُسَيْنِ الَّذي سَمَحَتْ نَفْسُهُ بِمُهْجَتِهِ،

translation.en: Peace be upon al-Husain, who sacrificed himself up to the last drops of the blood of his heart.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى مَنْ أطاعَ اللهَ في سِرِّهِ وَ عَلانِيَتِهِ،
segments.transliteration: assalāmu alā man 'aţāal-lāha fī sirrihi wa alāniyatih,

translation.en: Peace be upon him, who obeyed Allah secretly and openly.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى مَنْ جَعَلَ اللهُ الشِّفاءَ في تُرْبَتِهِ،
segments.transliteration: assalāmu alā man jaalal-lāhush-shifā'a fī turbatih,

translation.en: Peace be upon whom Allah placed a cure in the soil of his place (of martyrdom).

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى مَنِ الإجابَةُ تَحْتَ قُبَّتِهِ،

translation.en: Peace be upon the one under whose dome answer (to supplications) is guaranteed.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى مَنِ الأئِمَّةُ مِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِهِ،

translation.en: Peace be upon the one in whose descendants are the Imams (after him).
Peace be upon the son of the seal of the prophets.
Peace be upon the son of the chief of the executors.
Peace be upon the son of Fatima, the radiant.
Peace be upon the son of Khadija, the great.
Peace be upon the son of the lote-tree in the outermost boundary (of Heaven).[1]
Peace be upon the son of the Garden of refuge.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلَى ابْنِ زَمْزَمَ وَ الصَّفا،

translation.en: Peace be upon the son of Zamzam and al-Safaa.
Peace be upon him, who was saturated in (his) blood.
Peace be upon him, whose tents were violated.
Peace be upon the fifth of the People of the Cloak.
Peace be upon the loneliest of the lonely.
Peace be upon the (greatest) martyr of all martyrs.
Peace be upon him, who was slain by the individuals of illegitimate birth.
Peace be upon the one who is at rest in Karbala.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى مَنْ بَكَتْهُ مَلائِكَةُ السَّماءِ،

translation.en: Peace be upon the one for whom the heavenly Angels wept.
Peace be upon the one whose descendants are the pure.
Peace be upon the chief of the religion.
Peace be upon the places of the (divine) proofs.
Peace be upon the Imams, the masters (of mankind).
Peace be upon the bloodstained chests.
Peace be upon the parched lips.
Peace be upon the plucked souls.
Peace be upon the snatched spirits.
Peace be upon the stripped corpses.
Peace be upon the pallid bodies.
Peace be upon the gushing bloods.
Peace be upon the dismembered limbs.
Peace be upon the heads raised upon lances.
Peace be upon the women (forcibly) exposed.
Peace be upon the Proof of the Lord of the worlds.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلَيْكَ وَ عَلى آبائِكَ الطّاهِرينَ،

translation.en: Peace be upon you and upon your pure ancestors.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلَيْكَ وَ عَلى أبْنائِكَ الْمُسْتَشْهَدينَ،

translation.en: Peace be upon you and upon your martyred sons.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلَيْكَ وَ عَلى ذُرِّيَّتِكَ النّاصِرينَ،

translation.en: Peace be upon you and upon your children who aided (you).

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلَيْكَ وَ عَلَى الْمَلائِكَةِ الْمُضاجِعينَ،
segments.transliteration: assalāmu alayka wa alal malā'ikatil muđhājiīn,

translation.en: Peace be upon you and upon the accompanying Angels.
Peace be upon the slain and the oppressed one.
Peace be upon his poisoned brother (Imam al-Hasan).
Peace be upon Ali, the elder (Ali al-Akbar).
Peace be upon the suckling infant (Ali al-Asghar).
Peace be upon the plundered bodies.
Peace be upon the family and children who were nearby (the place of martyrdom) [who where without support among strangers].
Peace be upon the mangled corpses (left) in the desert.
Peace be upon those who were left far from their homeland.
Peace be upon those who were buried without shrouds.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلَى الرُّؤُوسِ الْمُفَرَّقَةِ عَنِ الأبْدانِ،

translation.en: Peace be upon the heads severed from the bodies.
Peace be upon the bereaved and the patient one.
Peace be upon the oppressed one who was without a helper.
Peace be upon the inhabitant of the purified soil.
Peace be upon the possessor of the lofty dome.
Peace be upon him, whom the Almighty purified.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى مَنِ افْتَخَرَ بِهِ جَبْرَئيلُ،

translation.en: Peace be upon him, of whom Gabriel (Jabra’il) was proud.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى مَنْ ناغاهُ فِي الْمَهْدِ ميكائيلُ،
segments.transliteration: assalāmu alā man nāghāhu fil mahdi mīkā'īl,

translation.en: Peace be upon the one to whom Michael (Mika’il) spoke tenderly in the cradle.
Peace be upon the one whose pact was broken.
Peace be upon the one whose rights and dignity were violated.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى مَنْ اُريقَ بِالظُّلْمِ دَمُهُ،

translation.en: Peace be upon the one whose blood was shed unjustly.
Peace be upon the one who was bathed in the blood of his wounds.
Peace be upon the one who tasted the spears raining down over his body.
Peace be upon the one against whom people came together and made lawful the shedding of his blood.
Peace be upon the one slaughtered in public.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلى مَنْ دَفَنَهُ أهْلُ الْقُرى،

translation.en: Peace be upon the one who was buried by the strangers from (nearby) villages.
Peace be upon the one whose aorta was severed.
Peace be upon the defender who had no helper.
Peace be upon the gray hair that was dyed (with blood).
Peace be upon the cheek that struck the dust.
Peace be upon the butchered body.
Peace be upon the front teeth that were beaten with a rod.
Peace be upon the head raised (upon a lance).

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلَى الأجْسامِ الْعارِيَةِ فِي الْفَلَواتِ، تَنْهَشُهَا الذِّئابُ الْعادِياتُ، وَ تَخْتَلِفُ إلَيْهَا السِّباعُ الضّارِياتُ،
segments.transliteration: assalāmu alal 'ajsāmil āriyati fil falawāt, tanhashuhadh-dhi'ābul ādiyāt, wa takhtalifu 'ilayhas-sibāuđh-đhāriyāt,

translation.en: Peace be upon the unclothed corpses in the desert, bitten by wild wolves[1] and around whom the beasts of prey prowled.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلَيْكَ يا مَوْلايَ وَ عَلَى الْمَلائِكَةِ الْمُرَفْرَفينَ حَوْلَ قُبَّتِكَ،
segments.transliteration: assalāmu alayka yā mawlāy wa alal malā'ikatil murafrafīna ħawla qubbatik,

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O my master, and the Angels who flutter around your dome,

segments.arabic: الْحافّينَ بِتُرْبَتِكَ، الطّائِفينَ بِعَرْصَتِكَ، الْوارِدينَ لِزِيارَتِكَ،
segments.transliteration: al-ħāffīna biturrbatik, aţ-ţā'ifīna biaršatik, al-wāridīna liziyāratik,

translation.en: surround your grave, circumambulate your courtyard, and come for your visitation.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلامُ عَلَيْكَ فَإنّي قَصَدْتُ إلَيْكَ، وَ رَجَوْتُ الْفَوْزَ لَدَيْكَ،
segments.transliteration: assalāmu alayka fa'innī qašadtu 'ilayk, wa rajawtul fawza ladayk,

translation.en: Peace be upon you! Indeed, I intended your visitation, and I am hopeful of achieving the prosperity that is with you.
Salutations to you,

segments.arabic: سَلامَ الْعارِفِ بِحُرْمَتِكَ، الْمُخْلِصِ في وِلايَتِكَ، الْمُتَقَرِّبِ إلَى اللهِ بِمَحَبَّتِكَ، الْبَريءِ مِنْ أعْدائِكَ،
segments.transliteration: salāmal ārifi biħurmatik, al-mukhliši fī wilāyatik, al-mutaqqarribi 'ilal-lāhi bimaħabbatik, al-barī'i min 'adā'ik,

translation.en: Salutations from he who recognizes your sanctity, is a sincere (believer) in your guardianship, seeks nearness to Allah through your love, and is aloof from your enemies,

segments.arabic: سَلامَ مَنْ قَلْبُهُ بِمُصابِكَ مَقْرُوحٌ، وَ دَمْعُهُ عِنْدَ ذِكْرِكَ مَسْفُوحٌ،
segments.transliteration: salāma man qalbuhu bimušābika maqrūħ, wa damuhu inda dhikrika masfūħ,

translation.en: Salutations from the one whose heart is wounded due to the tribulations you have suffered, and whose tears flow in your remembrance,
Salutations from the one who is distressed, grief-stricken, distracted, and yielding,

segments.arabic: سَلامَ مَنْ لَوْ كانَ مَعَكَ بِالطُّفُوفِ، لَوَقاكَ بِنَفْسِهِ حَدَّ السُّيُوفِ، وَ بَذَلَ حُشاشَتَهُ دُونَكَ لِلْحُتُوفِ،
segments.transliteration: salāma man law kāna maaka biţ-ţufūf, lawaqāka binafsihi ħaddas-suyūf, wa badhala ħushāshatahu dūnaka lil ħutūf,

translation.en: Salutations from the one, who, had he been present with you in that plain, would have shielded you from the sharpness of the swords with his body and sacrificed his last breath for you,

segments.arabic: وَ جاهَدَ بَيْنَ يَدَيْكَ، وَ نَصَرَكَ عَلى مَنْ بَغى عَلَيْكَ، وَ فَداكَ بِرُوحِهِ وَ جَسَدِهِ وَ مالِهِ وَ وَلَدِهِ،
segments.transliteration: wa jāhada bayna yadayk, wa našaraka alā man baghā alayk, wa fadāka birūħihi wa jasadihi wa mālihi wa waladih,

translation.en: would have struggled beside you, helped you against the aggressors, and redeemed you with his soul, body, wealth, and children,
(Salutations from the one) whose soul is a sacrifice for yours,
and whose family is a shield for yours.

segments.arabic: فَلَئِنْ أخَّرَتْنِي الدُّهُورُ، وَ عاقَني عَنْ نَصْرِكَ الْمَقْدُورُ،
segments.transliteration: fala'in 'akh-kharatnid-duhūr, wa āqanī an našrikal maqdūr

translation.en: But as I have been hindered by the course of time and as (Allah’s) decree has prevented me from helping you,

segments.arabic: وَ لَمْ أكُنْ لِمَنْ حارَبَكَ مُحارِباً، وَ لِمَنْ نَصَبَ لَكَ الْعَداوَةَ مُناصِباً،
segments.transliteration: wa lam 'akun liman ħārabaka muħāribā, wa liman našaba lakal adāwata munāšibā,

translation.en: and as I could not fight those who fought you, and was not able to show hostility to those who showed hostility to you,

segments.arabic: فَلَأَنْدُبَنَّكَ صَباحاً وَ مَساءً وَ لَأَبْكِيَنَّ لَكَ بَدَلَ الدُّمُوعِ دَماً، حَسْرَةً عَلَيْكَ وَ تَأسُّفاً عَلى ما دَهاكَ وَ تَلَهُّفاً،
segments.transliteration: Fala'andubannaka šabāħan wa masā'ā, wa la'abkiyanna laka badalad-dumūi damā, ħasratan alayka wa ta'assufan alā mā dahāka wa talahhufā,

translation.en: I will, therefore, lament you morning and evening, and will weep blood in place of tears, out of my anguish for you and my sorrow for all that befell you,

segments.arabic: حَتّى أمُوتَ بِلَوْعَةِ الْمُصابِ، وَ غُصَّةِ الإكْتِيابِ،

translation.en: until I meet death from the pain of the catastrophe and the choking grief.

segments.arabic: أشْهَدُ أ نَّكَ قَدْ أقَمْتَ الصَّلوةَ، وَ آتَيْتَ الزَّكوةَ،

translation.en: I bear witness that you certainly established prayer, gave alms,

segments.arabic: وَ أمَرْتَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ، وَ نَهَيْتَ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ وَ الْعُدْوانِ،
segments.transliteration: wa 'amarta bil marūf, wa nahayta anil munkari wal udwān,

translation.en: enjoined good, forbade evil and transgression,

segments.arabic: وَ أطَعْتَ اللهَ وَ ما عَصَيْتَهُ، وَ تَمَسَّكْتَ بِهِ وَ بِحَبْلِهِ
segments.transliteration: wa 'aţatal-lāha wa mā ašaytah, wa tamassakta bihi wa bi ħablih,

translation.en: obeyed Allah, never disobeyed Him, and held fast to Him and to His rope.

segments.arabic: فَأرْضَيْتَهُ وَ خَشيتَهُ وَ راقَبْتَهُ وَ اسْتَجَبْتَهُ،
segments.transliteration: fa 'arđhaytahu wa khashaytahu wa rāqabtahu wastajabtah,

translation.en: Then, you pleased Him, held Him in awe, were attentive towards Him, and were responsive to Him,

segments.arabic: وَ سَنَنْتَ السُّنَنَ، وَ أطْفَأْتَ الْفِتَنَ،

translation.en: established the customs (of the Prophet (PBUH&HF)), extinguished turmoil (in religion),

segments.arabic: وَ دَعَوْتَ إلَى الرَّشادِ، وَ أوْضَحْتَ سُبُلَ السَّدادِ، وَ جاهَدْتَ فِي اللهِ حَقَّ الْجِهادِ،
segments.transliteration: wa daawta 'ilar-rashād, wa 'awđhaħta subulas-sadād, wa jāhadta fil-lāhi ħaqqal jihād,

translation.en: invited people to rectitude, clarified the ways of righteousness, and truly strove in the way of Allah.
You were an obedient one to Allah,

segments.arabic: وَ لِجَدِّكَ مُحَمَّدٍ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ تابِعاً،
segments.transliteration: wali jaddika muħammadin šallal-lāhu alayhi wa ālihi tābiā,

translation.en: a follower of your grandfather, Muhammad, peace be upon him and his family,
heedful of the saying of your father,
quick to execute the will of your brother,

segments.arabic: وَ لِعِمادِ الدّينِ رافِعاً، وَ لِلطُّغْيانِ قامِعاً،
segments.transliteration: wali imādid-dīni rāfiā, wa liţ-ţughyāni qāmiā,

translation.en: an erector of the pillars of the religion, a suppressor of tyranny,

segments.arabic: وَ لِلطُّغاةِ مُقارِعاً، وَ لِلأُمَّةِ ناصِحاً،
segments.transliteration: wa liţ-ţughāti muqāriā, wa lil 'ummati nāšiħā,

translation.en: an advancer on the transgressors, and a sincere exhorter for the nation,
a traveler into mortal throes,

segments.arabic: وَ لِلْفُسّاقِ مُكافِحاً، وَ بِحُجَجِ اللهِ قائِماً،
segments.transliteration: wa lil fussāqi mukāfiħā, wa bi-ħujajil-lāhi qā'imā,

translation.en: a warrior against the wretched, and a maintainer of Allah’s proofs (on earth),
compassionate towards Islam and Muslims,

segments.arabic: وَ لِلْحَقِّ ناصِراً، وَ عِنْدَ الْبَلاءِ صابِراً،
segments.transliteration: wa lil ħaqqi nāširā, wa indal balā'i šābirā,

translation.en: a champion of truth and most patient in adversity,

segments.arabic: وَ لِلدّينِ كالِئاً، وَ عَنْ حَوْزَتِهِ مُرامِياً،
segments.transliteration: wa liddīni kāli'ā, wa an ħawzatihi murāmiyā,

translation.en: a protector for the religion, and a defender of its domain.
You safeguarded the right path and supported it,
spread justice and promoted it,
advocated the faith and manifested it,
restrained and reproached the frivolous,
took back (the rights) of the lowly from the privileged,

segments.arabic: وَ تُساوي فِي الْحُكْمِ بَيْنَ الْقَوِيِّ وَ الضَّعيفِ،
segments.transliteration: wa tusāwī fil ħukmi baynal qawiyyi wađh-đhaīf,

translation.en: and were equitable in your arbitrations between the weak and the strong.

segments.arabic: كُنْتَ رَبيعَ الأيْتامِ، وَ عِصْمَةَ الأنامِ، وَ عِزَّ الإسْلامِ،
segments.transliteration: kunta rabīal 'aytām, wa išmatal 'anām, wa izzal 'islām,

translation.en: You were the springtime of the orphans, the protection of humanity, the glory of Islam,

segments.arabic: وَ مَعْدِنَ الأحْكامِ، وَ حَليفَ الإنْعامِ،

translation.en: the treasure of divine laws, and a relentless ally of benevolence.

segments.arabic: سالِكاً طَرائِقَ [في طَريقَةِ] جَدِّكَ وَ أبيكَ،

translation.en: You pursued the path of your grandfather and your father,
resembled your brother in will,
were loyal to your obligations, possessed pleasant manners,
embodied generosity, and spent the darkness (of night) in prayer.

segments.arabic: قَويمَ الطَّرائِقِ، كَريمَ الْخَلائِقِ، عَظيمَ السَّوابِقِ،

translation.en: You were the straightest path, the most generous of the creation, and had the brightest record.

segments.arabic: شَريفَ النَّسَبِ، مُنيفَ الْحَسَبِ، رَفيعَ الرُّتَبِ،

translation.en: You were of great ancestry, noble descent, and lofty rank.

segments.arabic: كَثيرَ الْمَناقِبِ، مَحْمُودَ الضَّرائِبِ، جَزيلَ الْمَواهِبِ،

translation.en: You possessed plentiful merits, praiseworthy manners, and were abundant in endowments.

segments.arabic: حَليمٌ رَشيدٌ مُنيبٌ، جَوادٌ عَليمٌ شَديدٌ،
segments.transliteration: ħalīmun rashīdun munīb, jawādun alīmun shadīd,

translation.en: You were forbearing, upright, always turning (to Allah), generous, knowledgeable, strong,

segments.arabic: إمامٌ شَهيدٌ، أوّاهٌ مُنيبٌ، حَبيبٌ مَهيبٌ،

translation.en: a martyred Imam, grieved, repentant with earnest prayers (to Allah), dearly loved, and awesome.

segments.arabic: كُنْتَ لِلرَّسُولِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ وَلَداً،
segments.transliteration: kunta lir-rasūli šallal-lāhu alayhi wa ālihi waladā,

translation.en: You were to the Messenger, peace be upon him and his family, a son,
for the Quran, an authority [a savior],
and for the nation, a support.
You were diligent in obedience,
a protector of the covenant and oath,
keeping away from the paths of the debauched,
sparing no effort (in fulfilling the duties),
performing prolonged bowing and prostrations,

segments.arabic: زاهِداً فِي الدُّنْيا زُهْدَ الرّاحِلِ عَنْها،

translation.en: abstaining from the world, like one who is departing from it,
looking upon it (the world) through the eyes of one estranged (from it).
Your desires from it (the world) were abstentious,
your efforts were far-removed from its embellishments,
your glances removed from its joys,
and your desire for the hereafter was well-known,
even when tyranny became wide-spread,
injustice removed its veil,
wickedness called upon its followers,

segments.arabic: وَ أنْتَ في حَرَمِ جَدِّكَ قاطِنٌ،

translation.en: and you were residing in the sanctuary of your grandfather,
detached from the oppressors,
sitting in the house and the prayer niche,
unattached to (worldly) pleasures and carnal desires,

segments.arabic: تُنْكِرُ الْمُنْكَرَ بِقَلْبِكَ وَ لِسانِكَ، عَلى حَسَبِ طاقَتِكَ وَ إمْكانِكَ،
segments.transliteration: tunkirul munkara biqalbika wa lisānik, alā ħasabi ţāqatika wa 'imkānik,

translation.en: and renouncing evil in your heart and on your tongue to the extent of your strength and ability.
Then the knowledge demanded you for disavowal (of falsehood),
and made it incumbent on you to struggle against the deviant.

segments.arabic: فَسِرْتَ في أوْلادِكَ وَ أهاليكَ، وَ شيعَتِكَ وَ مَواليكَ
segments.transliteration: fasirta fī 'awlādika wa 'ahālīk, wa shīatika wa mawālīk,

translation.en: Therefore, you set out in company of your children, kinsfolk, followers, and supporters,

segments.transliteration: wa šadata bil ħaqqi wal bayyinah,

translation.en: disclosed the truth and clear proofs,

segments.arabic: وَ دَعَوْتَ إلَى اللهِ بِالْحِكْمَةِ وَ الْمَوْعِظَةِ الْحَسَنَةِ،
segments.transliteration: wa daawta 'ilal-lāhi bil ħikmakti wal mawiđatil ħasanah,

translation.en: summoned people towards Allah with wisdom and fine exhortation,

segments.arabic: وَ أمَرْتَ بِإقامَةِ الْحُدُودِ، وَ الطّاعَةِ لِلْمَعْبُودِ،
segments.transliteration: wa 'amarta bi'iqāmatil ħudūd, waţ-ţāati lil mabūd,

translation.en: ordered the establishment of the limits of divine law, and the obedience to the One Who should be worshipped,

segments.arabic: وَ نَهَيْتَ عَنِ الْخَبائِثِ وَ الطُّغْيانِ،

translation.en: and forbade (people) from wickedness and oppression.
But, they confronted you with injustice and aggression.

segments.arabic: فَجاهَدْتَهُمْ بَعْدَ الإيعازِ لَهُمْ [الإيعادِ إلَيْهِمْ] وَ تَأْكيدِ الْحُجَّةِ عَلَيْهِمْ،
segments.transliteration: fa jāhadtahum badal 'īāzi lahum [al-īādi 'ilayhim] wa ta'kīdil ħujjati alayhim,

translation.en: Therefore, you resisted them after advising them and stressing over (divine) proofs against them.
However, they violated your (divine) rights and oath.
angered your Lord and your grandfather,
and initiated battle against you.
Hence, you stood firm to spear and strike,

segments.arabic: وَ طَحَنْتَ جُنُودَ الْفُجّارِ، وَ اقْتَحَمْتَ قَسْطَلَ الْغُبارِ،
segments.transliteration: wa ţaħanta junūdal fujjār, waqtaħamta qasţalal ghubār,

translation.en: pulverized the soldiers of the transgressors, and stormed (courageously) into the dust of the battle,

segments.arabic: مُجالِداً بِذِى الْفَقارِ، كَأنَّكَ عَلِيٌّ الْمُخْتارُ،
segments.transliteration: mujālidan bidhil faqār, ka'annaka aliyunil mukhtār,

translation.en: fighting with Thulfaqar[1] as if you were Ali, the chosen one.

segments.arabic: فَلَمّا رَأوْكَ ثابِتَ الْجاشِ، غَيْرَ خائِفٍ وَ لا خاشٍ،
segments.transliteration: fallammā ra'awka thābital jāsh, ghayra khā'ifin wa lā khāsh,

translation.en: So when they saw you firm, fearless, and courageous,

segments.arabic: نَصَبُوا لَكَ غَوائِلَ مَكْرِهِمْ، وَ قاتَلُوكَ بِكَيْدِهِمْ وَ شَرِّهِمْ،
segments.transliteration: našabū laka ghawā'ila makrihim, wa qātaluka bikaydihim wa sharrihim,

translation.en: they set up their most malicious deceptions against you, and fought you with their deceit and viciousness.

segments.arabic: وَ أمَرَ اللَّعينُ جُنُودَهُ، فَمَنَعُوكَ الْماءَ وَ وُرُودَهُ،
segments.transliteration: wa 'amaral-laīnu junūdahu, famanaūkal mā'a wa wurūdah,

translation.en: The accursed one (Umar Ibn Sa’d) commanded his soldiers, and thus, they prevented you from reaching or receiving water.

segments.arabic: وَ ناجَزُوكَ الْقِتالَ، وَ عاجَلُوكَ النِّزالَ،

translation.en: They rushed to engage you in combat, descended swiftly upon you,
showering you with arrows and stones,
and moving towards you with uprooting hands.

segments.arabic: وَ لَمْ يَرْعَوْا لَكَ ذِماماً، وَ لا راقَبُوا فيكَ أثاماً، في قَتْلِهِمْ أوْلِياءَكَ، وَ نَهْبِهِمْ رِحالَكَ،
segments.transliteration: wa lam yaraw laka dhimāmā, wa lā rāqabū fīka 'athāmā, fī qatlihim 'awliyā'ak, wa nahbihim riħālak,

translation.en: Neither they respected your rights, nor were they mindful of retribution for slaying you and your companions, and plundering your belongings.

segments.arabic: وَ أنْتَ مُقَدَّمٌ فِي الْهَبَواتِ، وَ مُحْتَمِلٌ لِلأذِيّاتِ،
segments.transliteration: wa 'anta muqqaddamun fil habawāt, wa muħtamilun lil adhiyyāt,

translation.en: You were in the front line of the storm (of battle), enduring afflictions.

segments.arabic: قَدْ عَجِبَتْ مِنْ صَبْرِكَ مَلائِكَةُ السَّماواتِ،

translation.en: Indeed, the angels of the heavens were astonished by your patience.
The enemy then surrounded you from all sides,
weakened you by inflicting wounds,

segments.arabic: وَ حالُوا بَيْنَكَ وَ بَيْنَ الرَّواحِ،

translation.en: prevented you from taking any repose,
and you had no helper remaining.
You were bereaved yet patient,
defending your women and children,
until they caused you to fall from your horse.
You fell to the ground, wounded,
horses trampled you with their hooves,
tyrants raised their swords against you,
the sweat of death appeared on your forehead,

segments.arabic: وَ اخْتَلَفَتْ بِالاِنْقِباضِ وَ الإنْبِساطِ شِمالُكَ وَ يَمينُكَ،
segments.transliteration: wakhtalafat bil inqibāđhi wal inbisāţi shimāluka wa yamīnuk,

translation.en: and you continually clenched and unclenched your hands,

segments.arabic: تُديرُ طَرْفاً خَفِيّاً إلى رَحْلِكَ وَ بَيْتِكَ،
segments.transliteration: tudīru ţarfan khafiyyan 'ilā raħlika wa baytik,

translation.en: secretly gazing upon your caravan and tents,

segments.arabic: وَ قَدْ شُغِلْتَ بِنَفْسِكَ عَنْ وُلْدِكَ وَ أهاليكَ،
segments.transliteration: wa qad shughilta binafsika an wuldika wa 'ahālīk,

translation.en: while trapped by yourself away from your children and family.

segments.arabic: وَ أسْرَعَ فَرَسُكَ شارِداً، إلى خِيامِكَ قاصِداً، مُحَمْحِماً باكِياً،
segments.transliteration: wa 'asraa farasuka shāridan, 'ilā khiyāmika qāšidan, muħamħiman bākiyā,

translation.en: (At that time,) your horse distractedly galloped towards your camp, neighing and crying.
When the women saw your horse distraught,
and observed your saddle contorted,
they came from the tents,
disheveling their hair,
striking their now unveiled cheeks,
calling you by lamenting and wailing,
being humiliated after being honored,
hastening to where you lay wounded.
At that time Shimr (L) was sitting on your chest,
quenching his sword with (the blood of) your throat,
grabbing your beard with his hand,
as he slew you with his sword.
Your faculties faded,
your breath became shallow and ceased,
and your head was raised onto a spear.
Your family were captured like slaves,

segments.arabic: وَ صُفِّدُوا فِي الْحَديدِ فَوْقَ أقْتابِ الْمَطِيّاتِ،
segments.transliteration: wa šuffidū fil ħadīdi fawqa 'aqtābil maţiyyāt,

translation.en: bound with iron chains atop camels,
with midday heat scorching their faces.
They were driven across deserts and wastelands,
with their hands chained to their necks,
and were paraded around the markets.
Woe be unto the wicked transgressors!
Certainly, by killing you, they have killed Islam,[1]
disrupted (the truth of) prayer and fasting,
revoked the (prophetic) customs and the (divine) laws,
destroyed the pillars of faith,
distorted the verses of the Quran,

segments.arabic: وَ هَمْلَجُوا فِي الْبَغْيِ وَ الْعُدْوانِ،
segments.transliteration: wa hamlajū fil baghyi wal udwān,

translation.en: and brutally rushed into tyranny and aggression.

segments.arabic: لَقَدْ أصْبَحَ رَسُولُ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ مَوْتُوراً،
segments.transliteration: laqad 'ašbaħa rasūlul-lāhi šallal-lāhu alayhi wa ālihi mawtūrā,

translation.en: Certainly, (by this event,) the Messenger of Allah (PBUH&HF)was wronged, left alone, and denied vengeance,

segments.arabic: وَ عادَ كِتابُ اللهِ عَزَّوَجَلَّ مَهْجُوراً،
segments.transliteration: wa āda kitābul-lāhi azza wa jalla mahjūrā,

translation.en: the Book of Allah, the mighty and the majestic, was again abandoned,

segments.arabic: وَ غُودِرَ الْحَقُّ إذْ قُهِرْتَ مَقْهُوراً،

translation.en: truth was betrayed when you were forcibly overcome,

segments.arabic: وَ فُقِدَ بِفَقْدِكَ التَّكْبيرُ وَ التَّهْليلُ،

translation.en: And with your loss, call for Allah’s glorification and His Unity,

segments.arabic: وَالتَّحْريمُ وَ التَّحْليلُ، وَ التَّنْزيلُ وَ التَّأْويلُ،

translation.en: His prohibitions, sanctions, revelation, and interpretation were lost.

segments.arabic: وَ ظَهَرَ بَعْدَكَ التَّغْييرُ وَ التَّبْديلُ، وَ الإلْحادُ وَ التَّعْطيلُ، وَ الأهْواءُ وَ الأضاليلُ، وَ الْفِتَنُ وَ الأباطيلُ،
segments.transliteration: wa đahara badakat-taghyīru wat-tabdīl, wal 'ilħādu wat-taţīl, wal 'ahwā'u wal 'ađhālīl, wal fitanu wal 'abāţīl,

translation.en: After you, alteration, distortion, infidelity, abandonment (of the Islamic laws), vagary, misguidance, turmoil and falsehood became prevalent.

segments.arabic: فَقامَ ناعيكَ عِنْدَ قَبْرِ جَدِّكَ الرَّسُولِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ،
segments.transliteration: faqāma nāīka inda qabri jaddikar-rasūli, šallal-lāhu layhi wa ālih,

translation.en: The announcer of your martyrdom came near the grave of your grandfather, the Messenger (PBUH&HF),
gave the news to him with tears flowing, saying:

segments.arabic: يا رَسُولَ اللهِ، قُتِلَ سِبْطُكَ وَ فَتاكَ،
segments.transliteration: ya rasūlal-lāh, qutila sibţuka wa fatāk,

translation.en: 0 the Messenger of Allah! Your brave grandson was slain,
and abuse of your family and supporters were deemed lawful.
After you, your progeny were captured,

segments.arabic: وَ وَقَعَ الْمَحْذُورُ بِعِتْرَتِكَ وَ ذَويكَ،
segments.transliteration: wa waqaal maħdhūru biitratika wa dhawīk,

translation.en: and adversity befell your family and your offspring.

segments.arabic: فَانْزَعَجَ الرَّسُولُ، وَ بَكى قَلْبُهُ الْمَهُولُ،

translation.en: Indeed, the Messenger (PBUH&HF) became distressed and his depressed heart wept,

segments.arabic: وَ عَزّاهُ بِكَ الْمَلائِكَةُ وَ الأنْبِياءُ،

translation.en: The Angels and the prophets offered their condolences to him for your martyrdom,
Your mother, al-Zahra, became distressed and bereft of you,

segments.arabic: وَ اخْتَلَفَتْ جُنُودُ الْمَلائِكَةِ الْمُقَرَّبينَ، تُعَزّي أباكَ أميرَالْمُؤْمِنينَ،
segments.transliteration: wakhtalafat junūdul malā'ikatil muqarrabīn, tuazzī 'abāka 'amīral mu'minīn,

translation.en: Legions of favored Angels came in waves to offer their condolences to your father, the Leader of the Faithful,

segments.arabic: وَ اُقيمَتْ لَكَ الْمَآتِمُ في أعْلا عِلِّيّينَ،

translation.en: Mourning commemorations were held for you in the utmost exalted place Heaven,
and the dark-eyed Maidens (of Paradise) hit their own heads and faces in grief,
The skies and their inhabitants wept,
as did Paradise and its keepers,
the mountains and their surroundings,
the oceans and their fishes,
the heavens and their servants,
the House (Ka’ba), and the Station (of Abraham),
the Sacred Monument,
and Mecca and its sanctuary.

text.en: Dua

translation.en: O Allah! By the sanctity of this exalted place (the tomb of Imam al-Husain (PBUH)),

segments.arabic: صَلِّ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ وَ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ،
segments.transliteration: šalli alā muħammadin wa āli muħammad,

translation.en: bestow blessings upon Muhammad and the family of Muhammad,
assemble me in their company,
and admit me to Paradise by their intercession.

segments.arabic: اَللّهُمَّ إنّي أتَوَسَّلُ إلَيْكَ يا أسْرَعَ الْحاسِبينَ،

translation.en: My Allah! I implore You, O He who is the quickest of the reckoners!

segments.arabic: وَ ياأكْرَمَ الأكْرَمينَ، وَ ياأحْكَمَ الْحاكِمينَ،

translation.en: O the most generous of the generous and the wisest of judges!

segments.arabic: بِمُحَمَّدٍ خاتَمِ النَّبِيّينَ، رَسُولِكَ إلَى الْعالَمينَ أجْمَعينَ،

translation.en: By Muhammad, the seal of the prophets, Your Messenger to all the worlds,

segments.arabic: وَ بِأخيهِ وَ ابْنِ عَمِّهِ الأنْزَعِ الْبَطينِ، الْعالِمِ الْمَكينِ، عَلِيٍّ أميرِ الْمُؤْمِنينَ،
segments.transliteration: wa bi 'akhīhi wabni ammihil 'anzail baţīn, al-ālimil makīn, aliyyin 'amīril mu'minīn,

translation.en: By his brother and cousin, the uprooter of hidden polytheism, the distinguished and learned, Ali, the Prince of the Faithful,

segments.transliteration: wa bi fāţimata sayyidati nisā'il ālamīn,

translation.en: By Fatima, the chief of women of the worlds,
By al-Hasan, the purified one and the protection of the pious,

segments.arabic: وَ بِأبي عَبْدِاللهِ الْحُسَيْنِ أكْرَمِ الْمُسْتَشْهَدينَ،

translation.en: By Abi Abdillah, al-Husain, the most honored martyr,

segments.arabic: وَ بِأوْلادِهِ الْمَقْتُولينَ، وَ بِعِتْرَتِهِ الْمَظْلُومينَ،
segments.transliteration: wa bi-'awlādihil maqtūlīn wa bi itratihil mađlūmīn,

translation.en: By his slain children and oppressed family,

segments.arabic: وَ بِعَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ زَيْنِ الْعابِدينَ،
segments.transliteration: wa bi aliyyibnil ħusayni zaynil ābidīn,

translation.en: By Ali Ibn al-Husain, the ornament of the worshippers,

segments.arabic: وَ بِمُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ قِبْلَةِ الأوّابينَ،

translation.en: By Muhammad Ibn Ali, the direction of those who turn to Allah,

segments.arabic: وَ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ أصْدَقِ الصّادِقينَ،

translation.en: By Ja’far Ibn Muhammad, the most truthful,

segments.arabic: وَ مُوسَى بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ مُظْهِرِ الْبَراهينَ،

translation.en: By Musa Ibn Ja’far, the discloser of the proofs,

segments.arabic: وَ عَلِيِّ بْنِ مُوسى ناصِرِ الدّينِ،

translation.en: By Ali Ibn Musa, the helper of the religion,

segments.arabic: وَ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ قُدْوَةِ الْمُهْتَدينَ،

translation.en: By Muhammad Ibn Ali, the exemplar of those who accepted guidance,

segments.arabic: وَ عَلِيِّ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ أزْهَدِ الزّاهِدينَ،

translation.en: By Ali Ibn Muhammad, the most ascetic,

segments.arabic: وَ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ وارِثِ الْمُسْتَخْلَفينَ،

translation.en: By Hasan Ibn Ali, the inheritor of the appointed ones,
By the Proof upon all creation (al-Mahdi),

segments.arabic: أنْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ وَ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ،
segments.transliteration: 'an tušalliya alā muħammadin wa āli muħammad,

translation.en: Bless Muhammad and the family of Muhammad,

segments.arabic: الصّادِقينَ الأبَرّينَ، آلِ طه وَ يس،

translation.en: the most truthful and devoted ones (who are) the family ofTaha and Yasin,[1]

segments.arabic: وَ أنْ تَجْعَلَني فِي الْقِيامَةِ مِنَ الآمِنينَ الْمُطْمَئِنّينَ،
segments.transliteration: wa 'an tajalani fil qiyāmati minal āminīnal muţma'innīn,

translation.en: and place me on the Day of Judgment among those who are safe, confident,
triumphant, happy, and felicitous.
O Allah! Destine me to be amongst the submitters,
Join me with the righteous,[1]

segments.arabic: وَاجْعَلْ لي لِسانَ صِدْقٍ فِي الآخِرينَ،
segments.transliteration: wajal lī lisāna šidqin fil ākhirīn,

translation.en: Ordain for me (offspring with) truthful tongue among the last generation,[2]
Make me victorious over the transgressors,
Suffice me the deception of the envious,
Turn away from me the evil plans of the schemers,
Hold back from me the hands of the oppressors,

segments.arabic: وَاجْمَعْ بَيْني وَ بَيْنَ السّادَةِ الْمَيامينِ في أعْلا عِلِّيّينَ،
segments.transliteration: wajma baynī wa baynas-sādatil mayāmīni fī 'alā illiyyīn,

translation.en: Gather me with the blessed masters in the utmost exalted place of Heaven,

segments.arabic: مَعَ الَّذينَ أنْعَمْتَ عَلَيْهِمْ مِنَ النَّبِيّينَ وَ الصِّدّيقينَ وَ الشُّهَداءِ وَ الصّالِحينَ،
segments.transliteration: maal-ladhīna 'anamta alayhim minal nabiyyīna waš-šiddīqīna wash-shuhadā'i waš-šāliħīn,

translation.en: along with whom You have bestowed favor, from among the prophets, the truthful, the martyrs, and the righteous,[]
By Your mercy, O the most merciful of the merciful.

text.en: The dua continues

segments.arabic: اَللّهُمَّ إنّي اُقْسِمُ عَلَيْكَ بِنَبِيِّكَ الْمَعْصُومِ،

translation.en: O Allah! I implore You by Your infallible Prophet,

segments.arabic: وَ بِحُكْمِكَ الْمَحْتُومِ، وَ نُهْيَكَ [نَهْيِكَ] الْمَكْتُومِ،
segments.transliteration: wa biħukmikal maħtūm, wa nuhyakal maktūm,

translation.en: by Your definite judgment, and Your concealed preventive wisdom,

segments.arabic: وَ بِهذَا الْقَبْرِ الْمَلْمُومِ، الْمُوَسَّدِ في كَنَفِهِ الإمامُ الْمَعْصُومُ، الْمَقْتُولُ الْمَظْلُومُ،
segments.transliteration: wa bihādhal qabril malmūm, al-muwassadi fī kanafihil 'imāmul mašūm, al-maqtūlul mađlūm,

translation.en: by this grave which is the place of congregation and in which lies the infallible Imam, the slaughtered, and the oppressed,

segments.arabic: أنْ تَكْشِفَ ما بي مِنَ الْغُمُومِ،

translation.en: that You dispel from me all that grieves me,

segments.arabic: وَ تَصْرِفَ عَنّي شَرَّ الْقَدَرِ الْمَحْتُومِ،
segments.transliteration: wa tašrifa annī sharral qadaril maħtūm,

translation.en: divert from me the harm of the decisive foreordained plan,

segments.arabic: وَ تُجيرَني مِنَ النّارِ ذاتِ السَّمُومِ،

translation.en: and give me refuge from the Hellfire with scorching winds.
My Allah! Honor me with Your bounties,
Make me content with Your apportionment,
Encompass me with Your munificence and generosity,

segments.arabic: وَ باعِدْني مِنْ مَكْرِكَ وَ نِقْمَتِكَ،
segments.transliteration: wa bāidnī min makrika wa niqmatik,

translation.en: And keep me far off from Your requital scheme and Your wrath.
O Allah! Protect me from errors,

segments.arabic: وَ سدِّدْني فِي الْقَوْلِ وَ الْعَمَلِ،
segments.transliteration: wa saddidnī fil qawli wal amal,

translation.en: Make me firm in speech and action,
Extend for me the period (of life),

segments.arabic: وَ اعْفِني مِنَ الأوْجاعِ وَ الْعِلَلِ،

translation.en: Relieve me from pain and ailments,

segments.arabic: وَ بَلِّغْني بِمَوالِيَّ وَ بِفَضْلِكَ أفْضَلَ الأَمَلِ،

translation.en: Make me achieve, through my masters and Your grace, the best of wishes.

segments.arabic: اَللّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ وَ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ وَ اقْبَلْ تَوْبَتي،
segments.transliteration: allāhumma šalli alā muħammadin wa āli muħammad waqbal tawbati,

translation.en: O Allah! Bless Muhammad and the family of Muhammad and accept my repentance and my return,
Have mercy upon my weeping,
Lessen my lapses,
Relieve my distress,
Forgive me my mistake,
And improve my (righteousness) through my children.

segments.arabic: اَللّهُمَّ لا تَدَعْ لي في هذَا الْمَشْهَدِ الْمُعَظَّمِ وَ الْمَحَلِّ الْمُكَرَّمِ ذَنْباً إلاّ غَفَرْتَهُ،
segments.transliteration: allāhumma lā tada lī fī hādhal mash-hadil muađđami wal maħallil mukkarrami dhanban 'illā ghafartah,

translation.en: My Allah! Do not leave for me, in this exalted and honored place of martyrdom, any sin but that You forgive,
Nor any defect but that You conceal,
Nor any grief but that You remove,
Nor any sustenance but that You extend,
Nor any (spiritual) status but that You cause to prosper,
Nor any corruption but that which You correct,
Nor any wish but that You fulfill,
Nor any supplication but that You answer,
Nor any pressure but that You relieve,
Nor any dispersed (believers) but that You reunify,
Nor any matter but that You complete,
Nor any wealth but that You increase,
Nor any character but that You improve,
Nor any charity but that You repay,
Nor any condition but that You cause to improve,
Nor any envious (one) but that You suppress,
Nor any enemy but that You destroy,
Nor any evil but that You suffice,
Nor any ailment but that You cure,
Nor any distant (one) but that You bring near,
Nor any scattering but that You reunite,
Nor any request but that You grant.
O Allah! I ask You for the goodness of this transitory world,
and the reward of the hereafter.
O Allah! Suffice me with what You made lawful from the unlawful,
and with Your grace from all other creatures.
My Allah! I ask You for beneficial knowledge,
a humble heart,
unequivocal certitude,
pure action,
beautiful patience,
and a bountiful reward.
O Allah! Grant me gratitude of Your blessing upon me,

segments.arabic: وَ زِدْ في إحْسانِكَ وَ كَرَمِكَ إلَىَّ،

translation.en: Increase Your favor and munificence on me,

segments.arabic: وَ اجْعَلْ قَوْلي فِي النّاسِ مَسْمُوعاً،

translation.en: Make my speech amongst people effective,
my deeds elevated and worthy of being delivered to You,
my righteous works followed (by others),
and my enemy quelled,

segments.arabic: اَللّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ وَ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ الأخْيارِ، في آناءِ اللَّيْلِ وَ أطْرافِ النَّهارِ،
segments.transliteration: allāhumma šalli alā muħammadin wa āli muħammadinil 'akhyār, fī ānā'il-layli wa 'aţrāfin-nahār,

translation.en: O Allah! Send blessings upon Muhammad and the family of Muhammad, the best of the creations, day and night,
Spare me from the evil of the wicked,

segments.arabic: وَ طَهِّرْني مِنَ الذُّنُوبِ وَ الأوْزارِ،

translation.en: Purify me from sins and burdens,
Give me refuge from Hell-fire,
Settle me in the House of rest (Paradise),

segments.arabic: وَ اغْفِرْ لي وَ لِجَميعِ إخْواني فيكَ وَ أخَواتِيَ الْمُؤْمِنينَ وَ الْمُؤْمِناتِ،
segments.transliteration: waghfir lī wa lijamīi 'ikhwāni fīka wa 'akhawātiyal mu'minīna wal mu'mināt,

translation.en: And forgive me and all my faithful brothers and sisters,
By Your mercy, O the most merciful of the merciful!

text.en:
Then stand beside the upper side of the grave, face towards the Qibla and perform a two-unit Ziyarat prayer. Recite <ref=1:21>Al-Anbiya</ref>
 in the first unit and <ref=1:59>Al-Hashr</ref>
 in the second unit of the prayer, and recite the following
text.ar:
ثم توجه إلى القبلة وصل ركعتين واقرأ في الأولى سورة <ref=1:21>الأنبیاء</ref>
 وفي الثانية <ref=1:59>الحشر</ref>

text.en: Duaa after ziyarat - in Qunoot of the prayer of Ziyarat
text.ar: واقنت وقل:

translation.en: There is no deity but Allah, the forbearing, the generous.
There is no deity but Allah, the supreme, the great.

segments.arabic: لاإلهَ إلاَّاللهُ رَبُّ السَّماواتِ السَّبْعِ وَ الأرَضينَ السَّبْعِ،

translation.en: There is no deity but Allah, the Lord of the seven heavens and the seven earths,

segments.arabic: وَ ما فيهِنَّ وَ ما بَيْنَهُنَّ،
segments.transliteration: wa mā fīhinna wa mā baynahunn,

translation.en: and whatever is in them and whatever is between them.
(By this, I am) opposing (the belief of) His enemies,
disavowing whomever has turned away from Him,
confessing His Lordship,
and surrendering to His majesty.
He is the first other than being the first (in number).
He is the last without having an end.
He is dominant (Zahir) over everything by His power.

segments.arabic: الْباطِنُ دُونَ كُلِّ شَىْء بِعِلْمِهِ وَ لُطْفِهِ،
segments.transliteration: al-bāţinu dūna kulli shay'in biilmihi wa luţfih,

translation.en: He is hidden (Batin) as He knows underneath of every thing by His knowledge and His subtlety.

segments.arabic: لا تَقِفُ الْعُقُولُ عَلى كُنْهِ عَظَمَتِهِ،
segments.transliteration: lā taqiful uqūlu alā kunhi ađamatih,

translation.en: Intellects cannot comprehend the depth of His grandeur.

segments.arabic: وَ لا تُدْرِكُ الأوْهامُ حَقيقَةَ ماهِيَّتِهِ،

translation.en: Thoughts cannot grasp the reality of His Essence.

segments.arabic: وَ لا تَتَصَوَّرُ الأنْفُسُ مَعانِيَ كَيْفِيَّتِهِ،

translation.en: Souls cannot imagine the meanings of His (created) qualities.
He knows well the innermost thoughts (of His servants),
is cognizant of the secrets,

segments.arabic: يَعْلَمُ خائِنَةَ الأعْيُنِ وَ ما تُخْفِى الصُّدُورُ،

translation.en: and knows the treachery of the eyes, and all that the hearts (of men) conceal.[1]

segments.arabic: اَللّهُمَّ إنّي اُشْهِدُكَ عَلى تَصْديقي رَسُولَكَ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ وَ إيماني بِهِ،
segments.transliteration: allāhumma 'innī 'ush-hiduka alā tašdīqī rasūlaka šallal-lāhu layhi wa ālih wa 'īmānī bih,

translation.en: My Allah! I bear You as witness of my acknowledgement of Your Messenger (PBUH&HF) and my faith in him,
and my recognition of his status.

segments.arabic: وَ إنّي أشْهَدُ أنَّهُ النَّبِيُّ الَّذي نَطَقَتِ الْحِكْمَةُ بِفَضْلِهِ،

translation.en: I bear witness that he was the Prophet by whose favor wisdom was given voice,
of whom (previous) prophets gave glad tidings,

segments.arabic: وَ دَعَتْ إلَى الإقْرارِ بِما جاءَ بِهِ،

translation.en: called people to admit what he  brought (from the Lord),
and urged people to acknowledge him,

segments.arabic: بِقَوْلِهِ تَعالى: «اَلَّذي يَجِدُونَهُ مَكْتُوباً عِنْدَهُمْ فِي التَّوْريةِ وَ الإنْجيلِ يَأْمُرُهُمْ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَ يَنْهيهُمْ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ وَ يُحِلُّ لَهُمُ الطَّيِّباتِ وَ يُحَرِّمُ عَلَيْهِمُ الْخَبائِثَ وَ يَضَعُ عَنْهُمْ إصْرَهُمْ وَ الأغْلالَ الَّتي كانَتْ عَلَيْهِمْ»،
segments.transliteration: biqawlihi ta'ālā: alladhīna yajidūnahu maktūban indahum fit-tawrāti wal 'injīli ya'muruhum bil marūfi wa yanhāhum anil munkari wa yuħillu lahumuţ-ţayyibāti wa yuħarrimu alayhimul khabā'itha wa yađhau anhum 'išrahum wal

translation.en: 'aghlālal-lati kānat

text.en: according to His saying, the most high, “… he, whom they find mentioned in their Torah and the Gospels, who commands them virtuous acts and forbids them evil, allows them as lawful the good (and pure) things, prohibits them from impurities, and releases them from their heavy burdens and from the yokes that are upon them ...” [2]
