segments.arabic: فَصَلِّ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ رَسُولِكَ إلَى الثَّقَلَيْنِ،

translation.en: Thus, bless Muhammad, Your messenger to both masses (of human and Jinn),
the master of the chosen prophets,

segments.arabic: وَ عَلى أخيهِ وَ ابْنِ عَمِّهِ،

translation.en: and (bless) his brother and his cousin,

segments.arabic: اللَّذَيْنِ لَمْ يُشْرِكا بِكَ طَرْفَةَ عَيْن أبَداً،
segments.transliteration: alladhīna lam yushrikā bika ţarfata aynin 'abadā,

translation.en: neither of whom ever took associates with You (in worship) even for one moment,

segments.arabic: وَ عَلى فاطِمَةَ الزَّهْراءِ سَيِّدةِ نِساءِ الْعالَمينَ،
segments.transliteration: wa alā fāţimataz-zahrā'i sayyidati nisā'il ālamīn,

translation.en: and (bless) Fatima, the radiant, and the chief of the ladies of the worlds,

segments.arabic: وَ عَلى سَيِّدَىْ شَبابِ أهْلِ الْجَنَّةِ الْحَسَنِ وَ الْحُسَيْنِ،
segments.transliteration: wa alā sayyiday shabābi 'ahlil jannah, al-ħasani wal ħusayn,

translation.en: and (bless) the two masters of the youth of Paradise, al-Hasan and al-Husain,
With an everlasting blessing,
equal to the number of drops of rains,
and the weight of the mountains and hills,
until peace and salutations is ever exchanged,
and brightness (of day) and darkness (of night) are replaced.

segments.arabic: وَ عَلى آلِهِ الطّاهِرينَ، الأئِمَّةِ الْمُهْتَدينَ،

translation.en: And (bless) his pure family, (the rest of) the guided Imams,
the defenders of the religion,

segments.arabic: عَلِيٍّ وَ مُحَمَّدٍ وَ جَعْفَرٍ وَ مُوسى وَ عَلِيٍّ وَ مُحَمَّدٍ وَ عَلِيٍّ وَ الْحَسَنِ وَ الْحُجَّةِ،
segments.transliteration: aliyyin wa muħammadin wa jafarin wa mūsa wa aliyyin wa muħammadin wa aliyyin wal ħasani wal ħujjah,

translation.en: (who are) Ali, Muhammad, Ja’far, Musa, Ali, Muhammad, Ali, al-Hasan, and al-Hujja,
the establishers of fairness, and the grandsons of the Prophet(PBUH&HF).

segments.arabic: اَللّهُمَّ إنّي أسْئَلُكَ بِحَقِّ هذَا الإمامِ فَرَجاً قَريباً،

translation.en: O Allah! I ask You by the rights of this Imam, a relief in the near future,
beautiful patience,
mighty triumph,
sufficiency from people,
steadfastness in (the path of) guidance,

segments.arabic: وَ التَّوْفيقَ لِما تُحِبُّ وَ تَرْضى،

translation.en: success in what pleases and satisfies You,
a sustenance that is vast, lawful, pure,
adequate, fruitful, pleasant,
abundant, superior, pouring forth,

segments.arabic: مِنْ غَيْرِ كَدّ وَ لا نَكَد، وَ لا مِنَّة مِنْ أحَد،
segments.transliteration: min ghayri kaddin wa lā nakad, wa lā minnatin min 'aħad,

translation.en: without any toil, trouble, and favor from anyone.

segments.arabic: وَ عافِيَةً مِنْ كُلِّ بَلاء وَ سُقْم وَ مَرَض،
segments.transliteration: wa āfiyatan min kulli balā'in wa suqmin wa marađh,

translation.en: Preserve my health against any affliction, ailment, and disease,

segments.arabic: وَ الشُّكْرَ عَلَى الْعافِيَةِ وَ النَّعْماءِ،

translation.en: Grant me appreciation of well-being and blessings,

segments.arabic: وَ إذا جاءَ الْمَوْتُ فَاقْبِضْنا عَلى أحْسَنِ ما يَكُونُ لَكَ طاعَةً،
segments.transliteration: wa 'idhā jā'al mawtu faqbidhnā alā 'aħsani mā yakūnu laka ţā'ah,

translation.en: And when the time of death arrives, take our soul while it is in the best condition of obedience to You,

segments.arabic: عَلى ما أمَرْتَنا مُحافِظينَ حَتّى تُؤَدِّيَنا إلى جَنّاتِ النَّعيمِ،
segments.transliteration: alā mā 'amartanā muħāfiđhīna ħattā ţu'addiyanā 'ilā jannātin-naīm,

translation.en: And while observing what You commanded, so that You lead us to the Garden of blessings.
By Your mercy, O the most merciful of the merciful!

segments.arabic: اَللّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ وَ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ،
segments.transliteration: allāhumma šalli alā muħammadin wa āli muħammad,

translation.en: My Allah! Bless Muhammad and the family of Muhammad,

segments.arabic: وَ أوْحِشْني مِنَ الدُّنْيا وَ آنِسْني بِالآخِرَةِ،
segments.transliteration: wa 'awħishnī minad-dunyā wa ānisnī bil ākhirah,

translation.en: and estrange me from (the attractions of) the world, and make me familiar with (the affairs of) the hereafter.

segments.arabic: فَإنَّهُ لا يُوحِشُ مِنَ الدُّنْيا إلاّ خَوْفُكَ،

translation.en: Certainly, nothing causes alienation from this world except fear of You,

segments.arabic: وَ لا يُؤْنِسُ بِالآخِرَةِ إلاّ رَجآؤُكَ،
segments.transliteration: wa lā yu'nisu bil ākhirati 'illā rajā'uk,

translation.en: and nothing causes to be accustomed to the hereafter except hope for Your (mercy).
O Allah! For You is the argument, not against You,
and to You is complained, not from You.
Thus, bless Muhammad and his family,

segments.arabic: وَ أعِنّي عَلى نَفْسِيَ الظّالِمَةِ الْعاصِيَةِ،

translation.en: and assist me against my unjust and disobedient soul,
and my dominant desire.
And conclude me with a safe and healthy end.

segments.arabic: اَللّهُمَّ إنَّ اسْتِغْفاري إيّاكَ وَ أنَا مُصِرٌّ عَلى مانَهَيْتَ قِلَّةُ حَيآء،
segments.transliteration: allāhumma 'innastighfārī 'iyyāka wa 'ana muširrun alā mā nahayta qillatu ħayā',

translation.en: O Allah! It is due to my shamelessness that I ask forgiveness while I insist on what You have forbidden.

segments.arabic: وَ تَرْكِيَ الاِسْتِغْفارَ مَعَ عِلْمي بِسِعَةِ حِلْمِكَ تَضْييعٌ لِحَقِّ الرَّجآءِ،
segments.transliteration: wa tarkiyal 'istighfāra maa ilmī bisiati ħilmika tađhyī'un liħaqqir-rajā'

translation.en: On the other hand, if I do not ask for forgiveness with my knowledge of Your abundant forbearance, it will be wasting the right of hope (to You).

segments.arabic: اَللّهُمَّ إنَّ ذُنُوبي تُؤْيِسُني أنْ أرْجُوَكَ،

translation.en: My Allah! (due to my weak faith) my sins discourage me from requesting You,

segments.arabic: وَ إنَّ عِلْمي بِسِعَةِ رَحْمَتِكَ يَمْنَعُني أنْ أخْشاكَ،
segments.transliteration: wa inna ilmi bisiati rahmatika yamnaunī 'an 'akhshāk,

translation.en: and my knowledge about Your vast mercy prevents me from fearing You.

segments.arabic: فَصَلِّ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ وَ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ،
segments.transliteration: fašalli alā muħammadin wa āli muħammad,

translation.en: Thus, bless Muhammad and the family of Muhammad,
and confirm my hope in You,
negate my fear of You,

segments.arabic: وَ كُنْ لي عِنْدَ أحْسَنِ ظَنّي بِكَ يا أكْرَمَ الأكْرَمينَ،
segments.transliteration: wa kun lī inda 'aħsani đanni bika yā 'akramal 'akramīn

translation.en: and be for me according to the best of what I expect and believe about You, O the most generous of the generous!

segments.arabic: اَللّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ وَ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ وَ أيِّدْني بِالْعِصْمَةِ،
segments.transliteration: allāhumma šalli alā muħammadin wa āli muħammadin, wa 'ayyidnī bil išmah,

translation.en: O Allah! Bless Muhammad and the family of Muhammad, and support me by protection (from sins),
Make my tongue speak wisdom,

segments.arabic: وَ اجْعَلْني مِمَّنْ يَنْدَمُ عَلى ما ضَيَّعَهُ في أمْسِهِ،
segments.transliteration: wajalnī mimman yandamu alā mā đhayyaahu fī 'amsih,

translation.en: Place me among those who regret what they wasted yesterday,

segments.arabic: وَ لا يَغْبَنُ حَظَّهُ في يَوْمِهِ،
segments.transliteration: wa lā yaghbanu ħađđahu fī yawmih,

translation.en: do not aggrieve their portion today,
and do not worry about the sustenance of tomorrow.

segments.arabic: اَللّهُمَّ إنَّ الْغَنِيَّ مَنِ اسْتَغْنى بِكَ وَ افْتَقَرَ إلَيْكَ،

translation.en: O Allah! Certainly, rich is he who suffices himself with You and considers himself in need of You,

segments.arabic: وَ الْفَقيرَ مَنِ اسْتَغْنى بِخَلْقِكَ عَنْكَ،

translation.en: and poor is he who feels that he can suffice himself through Your creation without You.

segments.arabic: فَصَلِّ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ وَ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ،
segments.transliteration: fašalli alā muħammadin wa āli muħammad,

translation.en: Thus, bless Muhammad and the family of Muhammad,
and suffice me from Your creation by Yourself,

segments.arabic: وَ اجْعَلْني مِمَّنْ لا يَبْسُطُ كَفّاً إلاّ إلَيْكَ،

translation.en: and put me amongst those who do not extend their hand except towards You.

segments.arabic: اَللّهُمَّ إنَّ الشَّقِيَّ مَنْ قَنَطَ وَ أمامَهُ التَّوْبَةُ وَ وَرآءَهُ الرَّحْمَةُ،
segments.transliteration: allāhumma 'innash-shaqiyya man qanaţa wa 'amāmahut-tawbatu wa warā'ahur-rahmah,

translation.en: My Allah! Certainly, wretched is he who despairs while repentance is placed before him and mercy is after him,

segments.arabic: وَ إنْ كُنْتُ ضَعيفَ الْعَمَلِ فَإ نّي في رَحْمَتِكَ قَوِيُّ الأمَلِ،
segments.transliteration: wa 'in kuntu đhaīfal amali fa 'innī fī raħmatika qawiyyal 'amal,

translation.en: Even though I have been weak in action, I have strong hope in Your mercy,

segments.arabic: فَهَبْ لي ضَعْفَ عَمَلي لِقُوَّةِ أمَلي،

translation.en: Thus, forgive the weakness of my action in light of the strength of my hope.

segments.arabic: اَللّهُمَّ إنْ كُنْتَ تَعْلَمُ أنْ ما في عِبادِكَ مَنْ هُوَ أقْسى قَلْباً مِنّي وَ أعْظَمُ مِنّي ذَنْباً،
segments.transliteration: allāhumma 'in kunta talamu 'an mā fī 'ibādika man huwa 'aqsā qalban minnī wa 'ađamu minnī dhanbā,

translation.en: O Allah! Even if You know there is no one amongst Your servants with harder heart and greater sins than mine,

segments.arabic: فَإ نّي أعْلَمُ أنَّهُ لا مَوْلى أعْظَمُ مِنْكَ طَوْلا،

translation.en: I certainly know that there is no master more generous than You,
with greater mercy and forgiveness.

segments.arabic: فَيا مَنْ هُوَ أوْحَدُ في رَحْمَتِهِ،

translation.en: Then, O He Who is unique in His mercy!

segments.arabic: إغْفِرْ لِمَنْ لَيْسَ بِأوْحَدَ في خَطيئَتِهِ،

translation.en: Forgive him, who is not unique in his mistakes.
O Allah! Certainly, You commanded us, but we have disobeyed,
forbade us, but we did not desist,
reminded us, but we remained unmindful,
enlightened us, but we behaved blindly,
and warned us, but we transgressed.

segments.arabic: وَ ما كانَ ذلِكَ جَزآءَ إحْسانِكَ إلَيْنا،

translation.en: This was not the repayment of Your kindness to us,

segments.arabic: وَ أنْتَ أعْلَمُ بِما أعْلَنّا وَ أخْفَيْنا،

translation.en: and You know what we have disclosed and have concealed,

segments.arabic: وَ أخْبَرُ بِما نَأْتي وَ ما أتَيْنا،

translation.en: and You are aware of what we will do and have done.

segments.arabic: فَصَلِّ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ وَ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ،
segments.transliteration: fašalli alā muħammadin wa āli muħammad,

translation.en: Thus, bless Muhammad and the family of Muhammad,

segments.arabic: وَ لا تُؤاخِذْنا بِما أخْطَأْنا وَ نَسينا،
segments.transliteration: wa lā tu'ākhidhnā bimā 'akhta'nā wa nasīnā,

translation.en: and pardon us for our mistakes and our forgetfulness,
forgive us the negligence of Your rights by us,
complete Your beneficence towards us,
and cause Your mercy to descend upon us.

segments.arabic: اَللّهُمَّ إنّا نَتَوَسَّلُ إلَيْكَ بِهذَا الصِّدّيقِ الإمامِ،

translation.en: My Allah! We seek nearness to You by means of this truthful Imam,

segments.arabic: وَ نَسْئَلُكَ بِالْحَقِّ الَّذي جَعَلْتَةُ لَهُ وَ لِجَدِّهِ رَسُولِكَ وَ لِأبَوَيْهِ عَلِيٍّ وَ فاطِمَةَ، أهْلِ بَيْتِ الرَّحْمَةِ،
segments.transliteration: wa nas'aluka bil ħaqqil-ladhi jaaltahu lah wa lijaddihi rasūlika wa li 'abawayhi aliyyin wa fāţimata 'ahli baytir-raħmah,

translation.en: and request You by the rights that You have placed for him, his grandfather, his parents, Ali and Fatima, the household of mercy,

segments.arabic: إدْرارَ الرِّزْقِ الَّذي بِهِ قِوامُ حَياتِنا وَ صَلاحُ أحْوالِ عِيالِنا،
segments.transliteration: 'idrārar-rizqil-lladhi bihī qiwāmu ħayātinā, wa šalāħu 'aħwāli iyālinā,

translation.en: an abundant flow of livelihood that maintains our life and the welfare of our dependents.

segments.arabic: فَأنْتَ الْكَريمُ الَّذي تُعْطي مِنْ سِعَةٍ،

translation.en: Certainly You are the generous one who grants abundantly,
and holds back having power (to fulfill).

segments.arabic: وَ نَحْنُ نَسْئَلُكَ مِنَ الرِّزْقِ مايَكُونُ صَلاحاً لِلدُّنْيا وَ بَلاغاً لِلآخِرَةِ،
segments.transliteration: wa naħnu nas'aluka minar-rizqi mā yakūnu šalāhan liddunyā wa balāghan lil ākhirah,

translation.en: We request from You a livelihood that brings the goodness of this world and the pleasure of the hereafter,

segments.arabic: اَللّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ وَ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ،
segments.transliteration: allāhumma šalli alā muħammadin wa āli muħammad,

translation.en: O Allah! Bless Muhammad and the family of Muhammad,
and forgive me, my parents,
all the believing men and women,
and the submissive men and women,
the living of them and the dead.

segments.arabic: وَ آتِنا فِي الدُّنْيا حَسَنَةً وَ فِي الآخِرَةِ حَسَنَةً وَ قِنا عَذابَ النّارِ.
segments.transliteration: wa ātinā fid-dunyā ħasanatan wa fil ākhirati ħasanatan wa qinā adhāban-nār.

translation.en: Give us good in this world and good in the hereafter, and shield us from the torment of the fire.[1]

text.en: Then, complete your prayer, and recite Tasbihat (of Lady Fatima(PBUH)), and then place the side of you face on the ground and say the following forty times:
text.ar: ثم تركع وتسجد وتجلس وتتشهد وتسلم فإذا سبحت فعفر خديك وقل أربعين مرة:

segments.arabic: سُبْحانَ اللهِ وَ الْحَمْدُ للهِِ وَ لا إلهَ إلاَّ اللهُ وَ اللهُ أكْبَرُ
segments.transliteration: subħānal-lāhi wal ħamdulil-lāhi wa lā 'ilāha 'illal-lāhu wal-lāhu akbar,

translation.en: Glory be to Allah! Praise be to Allah! There is no deity but Allah! Allah is greater (than being described).

text.en: Following that, ask Allah for protection, salvation, forgiveness, success in performing good deeds, and the acceptance of the actions that you do to seek His nearness and perform for His sake.
text.ar:
واسئل الله العصمة والنجاة والمغفرة والتوفيق بحسن العمل والقبول لما تتقرب به إليه وتبتغي به وجهه وقف عند الرأس ثم صل ركعتين على ما تقدم.
ثم انكب على القبر وقبله وقل:

segments.arabic: زادَ اللهُ في شَرَفِكُمْ، وَ السَّلامُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَ رَحْمَةُ اللهِ وَ بَرَكاتُهُ.
segments.transliteration: zādal-lāhu fī sharafikum, was-salāmu alaykum wa raħmatul-lāhi wa barakātuh.

translation.en: May Allah increase your honor. Peace be upon you, the mercy of Allah, and His blessings.

text.en: To be recited in Jannatul Baqi. Cemetry has Graves of Imam Hassan (as) Imam Zainul Abideen (as) Imam Baqer (as) Imam Sadiq (as) Fatima Bint Asad(mother of Imam ali (as), | Wives of Prophet (saws)| Hz Aqeel & Hz Abdullah | Hz Ibrahim(son) | Hurra martyrs | Paternal aunts | Haleemah Sadiya | Ummul Baneen mother of Hz Abbas as
text.ar: أئمة البقیع علیهم السلام أي الإمام الحسن المجتبى والإمام زين العابدين والإمام محمد الباقر والإمام جعفر الصادق عليهم‌السلام. إذا أردت زيارتهم فاعمل بما سبق من أداب الزيارة من الغسل والكون على الطهارة ولبس الثياب الطاهرة النظيفة والتطيب

text.en: Permission to Enter- Izn e Dukhool

segments.arabic: يَا مَوَالِيَّ يَا أَبْنَاءَ رَسُولِ ٱللَّهِ
segments.transliteration: ya mawaliyya ya abna'a rasuli allahi

translation.en: O my masters, sons of the Messenger of Allah,
I—your servant and the son of your maid
who stand submissively before you,
who is worth nothing in comparison with your elevated esteem,
and who admits your right (that is incumbent upon us)—
have come to you, seeking your shelter,
heading for your sanctuary,
seeking nearness to Your statuses,
and beseeching Allah in your names.
May I enter, O my masters?
May I enter, O intimate servants of Allah?

segments.arabic: اادْخُلُ يَا مَلاَئِكَةَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلْمُحْدِقينَ بِهٰذَا ٱلْحَرَمِ
segments.transliteration: a'adkhulu ya mala'ikata allahi almuhdiqina bihadha alharami

translation.en: May I enter, O Allah’s angels who surround this sanctuary
and reside in this shrine?

text.en: After you attain reverence, submission, and tenderheartedness, you may enter the shrine with your right foot, and say the following:

translation.en: Allah is greatly the Greatest.
All praise be to Allah abundantly.

segments.transliteration: wa subhana allahi bukratan wa asilan

translation.en: Glory be to Allah in morns and eves.
All praise be to Allah, the Single, the Absolute,
the Glorious, the One and Only,
the All-obliging, the All-benefactor,
the Donor, the All-compassionate,
Who conferred (upon us) with His bequests,
Who has made easy for me to visit my masters out of His beneficence,
Who has not included me with those who are prevented from visiting them;
rather, He has bestowed upon me and donated me (this favor).

text.en: You may then move toward their tombs, face them, turn the back to the kiblah direction, and say the following:

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O Leaders of true guidance.
Peace be upon you, O people of piety.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكُمْ ايُّهَا ٱلْحُجَجُ عَلَىٰ اهْلِ ٱلدُّنْيَا

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O Arguments against the inhabitants of this world.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكُمْ ايُّهَا ٱلْقُوَّامُ فِي ٱلْبَرِيَّةِ بِٱلْقِسْطِ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alaykum ayyuha alquwwamu fi albariyyati bilqisti

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O maintainers of justice on people.
Peace be upon you, O choicest people.
Peace be upon you, O members of the Household of Allah’s Messenger.
Peace be upon you, O people of confidential talks (of the angels).

segments.transliteration: ashhadu annakum qad ballaghtum wa nasahtum

translation.en: I bear witness that you did convey, offer advice,
endure for the sake of Allah,

segments.transliteration: wa kudhdhibtum wa usi'a ilaykum faghafartum

translation.en: and forgive when you were belied and maltreated.

segments.transliteration: wa ashhadu annakumu al-a'immatu alrrashiduna almuhtaduna

translation.en: I also bear witness that you are the orthodox, well-guided Leaders,
that the obedience to you is obligatory,
that your words are true,
that you had called (to Allah) but you were not answered,
that you had enjoined (the right) but you were not obeyed,

segments.transliteration: wa annakum da`a'imu alddini wa arkanu al-ardi

translation.en: and that you are the supports of the religion and the poses of the earth.
You were always under the sight of Allah
Who moved you from the loins of purified men
to the wombs of purified women.
The benighted ignorance could not stain you

segments.transliteration: wa lam tashrak fikum fitanu al-ahwa'i

translation.en: and the whimsical sedition could not attract you.
Pleased be you as your origin has been pleased.
The King of the religion has conferred upon us with the grace of our acknowledgment of you;
He therefore caused you to be in houses
which He (i.e. Allah) has permitted to be exalted
and that His Name may be mentioned therein,
and He has decided our invocations of blessings upon you
to be mercy on us and to be granting remission of our sins.
Allah has chosen you for us

segments.arabic: وَطَيَّبَ خَلْقَنَا بِمَا مَنَّ عَلَيْنَا مِنْ وِلاَيَتِكُمْ
segments.transliteration: wa tayyaba khalqana bima manna `alayna min wilayatikum

translation.en: and has purified our creation through the grace of our loyalty to your (Divinely commissioned) Leadership with which He has conferred upon us.
We have thus become nominated with Him due to our acknowledgement of you
and our giving credence to you.

segments.transliteration: wa hadha maqamu man asrafa wa akhta'a

translation.en: So, this is the situation of him who has committed mistakes and sins,
shown submission (to you), and confessed of what he had committed.
Hence, being in this situation, I hope for redemption

segments.arabic: وَانْ يَسْتَنْقِذَهُ بِكُمْ مُسْتَنْقِذُ ٱلْهَلْكَىٰ مِنَ ٱلرَّدَىٰ

translation.en: and I hope that the Savior of the perishing ones from perdition may save me in your names.
So, (please) be my interceders,
for I have come to you
when the people of this world abandoned you,
took the Signs of Allah for a jest,
and turned away from them haughtily.

text.en: You may then raise your head toward the sky and say the following:

segments.arabic: يَا مَنْ هُوَ قَائِمٌ لاَ يَسْهُو
segments.transliteration: ya man huwa qa'imun la yashu

translation.en: O He Who is Self-Subsisting and never forgets,
Who is Ever-living and never becomes heedless,
and Who encompasses all things!
To You is all gratitude for that You have guided me (to this)
and You have introduced to me that on which I rest,
when Your servants diverted from it,
ignored the recognition of it,
belittled its right,
and inclined to elsewhere.
Hence, You have conferred upon me with this favor

segments.arabic: مَعَ اقْوَامٍ خَصَصْتَهُمْ بِمَا خَصَصْتَنِي بِهِ
segments.transliteration: ma`a aqwamin khasastahum bima khasastani bihi

translation.en: as well as upon other people whom You have granted exclusively the same.
So, all praise be to You, for I have been
mentioned and written with You due to the situation in which I am now.
Hence, (please) do not deprive me of what I long for,

segments.transliteration: wa la tukhayyibni fi ma da`awtu

translation.en: and do not disappoint me as regards my supplication;
(please do so) in the name of the sanctity of Muhammad and his immaculate Household.

segments.arabic: وَصَلَّىٰ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: wa salla allahu `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin

translation.en: May Allah send blessings upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad.

text.en: You may now pray Almighty Allah for anything you want.
In Tahdhib al-Ahkam, Shaykh al-Tusi says: You may then offer the Ziyarah Prayer (salat al-ziyarah) in eight units; each two units are for one of the (four) Imams.
Shaykh al-Tusi and Sayyid Ibn Tawus say that if you want to bid farewell to the four Imams, you may say the following:

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكُمْ ائِمَّةَ ٱلْهُدَىٰ وَرَحْمَةُ ٱللَّهِ وَبَرَكَاتُهُ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alaykum a'immata alhuda wa rahmatu allahi wa barakatuhu

translation.en: Peace and Allah’s mercy and blessings be upon you, O leaders to the true guidance.
I entrust you with Allah([1]) and send salutations to you.
We believe in Allah and in the Messenger

segments.transliteration: wa bima ji'tum bihi wa dalaltum `alayhi

translation.en: and in that which you have conveyed and that to which you have guided.
O Allah, then write us down among those who bear witness.

text.en: You may then supplicate Almighty Allah as earnestly as possible and pray Him to grant you another opportunity to visit these holy tombs.

The holy shrine of the sublime and great lady Fatimah the daughter
of Imam Musa ibn Ja’far al-Kazim (‘a) lies in the blessed city of
Qumm. It is a very famous place with a towering dome, a tomb, many
courtyards, many custodians, and abundant endowments. The shrine is
the delight of the eyes of the people of Qumm and the shelter of all
people. Every year, great numbers of people undertake the troubles of
travel in order come for visiting this lady from distant regions, seeking
the rewards of visiting her tomb.
text.ar: مشهد السيدة الجليلة العظيمة فاطمة بنت موسى بن جعفر عليه‌السلام وقبرها الشريف في بلدة قم الطيّبة معروف مشهور وله قبّة شامخة وضريح وصحون وخدم كثيرون وأوقاف وافرة وهو قرة العين لأهالي قم وملاذ لعامّة الخلق يشدّ إليه الرحال في كل سنة خلق كثير من أقاصي البلاد فيتحمّلون متاعب السفر ابتغاء فضيلة زيارتها وفضلها وجلالها يعرف من كثير من الاخبار. روى الصدوق بسند كالصحيح عن سعد بن سعد قال : سألت الرضا عليه‌السلام عن فاطمة بنت موسى بن جعفر عليه‌السلام فقال : من زارها فله الجنة

text.en:
The merits and sublimity of Lady Fatimah al-Ma’sumah can be
derived from many traditions some of which are as follows:
Shaykh al-Saduq, through an authentic-like chain of authority, has
reported Sa’d ibn Sa’d to have asked Imam al-Rida (‘a) about Fatimah
the daughter of Imam Musa ibn Ja’far. Imam al-Rida, peace be upon
him, answered, “Whoever visits her (tomb) will be awarded Paradise.”
Through another valid chain of authority, Shaykh al-Saduq reports
Imam Muhammad al-Jawad the son of Imam al-Rida as saying,
“Whoever visits the tomb of my paternal aunt in Qumm will be
awarded Paradise.
text.ar: وروي بسند معتبر آخر عن محمد التقي بن الرضا عليه‌السلام قال : من زار قبر عمّتي بقم فله الجنة

text.en:
Allamah al-Majlisi has reported from some books on forms of
ziyarah that ‘Ali ibn Ibrahim reported on the authority of his father
that Imam al-Rida, Allah’s blessings be upon him, said to Sa’d al-
Ash’ari al-Qummi, “O Sa’d, we have a tomb in your city.”
“May Allah accept me as ransom for you!” answered Sa’d, “Do
you mean the tomb of Fatimah the daughter of Musa ibn Ja’far?”
The Imam (‘a) answered in the affirmative and added:
Whoever visits her with full recognition of her standing will be
awarded Paradise. When you come to her tomb, you should stop at the
side of her head, directing your face towards the kiblah direction, and
repeat the following word thirty-four times:
text.ar: وروى العلامة المجلسي (رض) عن بعض كتب الزيارات عن علي بن إبراهيم عن أبيه عن سعد الأشعري القمّي عن الرضا صلوات الله عليه قال : قال : يا سعد عندكم لنا قبر. قلت : جعلت فداك قبر فاطمة عليها‌السلام بنت موسى بن جعفر عليه‌السلام. قال : بلي ، من زارها عارفاً بحقّها فله الجنّة. فإذا أتيت القبر فقم عند رأسها مستقبلاً القبلة وقل : أربعاً وثلاثين مرة :

translation.en: Allah Is The Great

text.en: You should then repeat the following word thirty-three times:
text.ar: وثلاثاً وثلاثين مرة

translation.en: Glory Be To Allah

text.en: You should also repeat the following word thirty-three times:
text.ar: وثلاثاً وثلاثين مرّة

translation.en: Praise Be To Allah

text.en: You should then say the following words:
text.ar: وقل :

translation.en: Peace be upon Adam the choice of Allah.
Peace be upon Noah the prophet of Allah.
Peace be upon Abraham the friend of Allah.
Peace be upon Moses the spoken by Allah.
Peace be upon Jesus the spirit of Allah.
Peace be upon you, O Allah’s Messenger.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا خَيْرَ خَلْقِ ٱللَّهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O Allah’s best creature.
Peace be upon you, O elite of Allah.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا مُحَمَّدَ بْنَ عَبْدِ ٱللَّهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O Muhammad the son of `Abdullah
and the seal of the Prophets.
Peace be upon you, O Commander of the Faithful
`Ali the son of Abu-Talib
and the successor of Allah’s Messenger.
Peace be upon you, O Fatimah
the doyenne of the women of the worlds.
Peace be upon both of you, O grandsons of the Mercy
and two chiefs of the youth of Paradise.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا عَلِيُّ بْنَ ٱلْحُسَيْنِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O `Ali the son of al-Husayn,
the chief of the worshippers and the delight of the eye of all seers.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنَ عَلِيٍّ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O Muhammad the son of `Ali,
the splitter of knowledge after the Prophet.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا جَعْفَرُ بْنَ مُحَمَّدٍ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O Ja`far the son of Muhammad,
the veracious, dutiful, and honest.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا مُوسَىٰ بْنَ جَعْفَرٍ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O Musa the son of Ja`far,
the immaculate and ever-pure.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا عَلِيُّ بْنَ مُوسَىٰ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O `Ali the son of Musa,
the well-pleased and well-contented.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنَ عَلِيٍّ ٱلتَّقِيُّ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O Muhammad the son of `Ali, the pious.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا عَلِيُّ بْنَ مُحَمَّدٍ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O `Ali the son of Muhammad,
the immaculate, sincere, and honest.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا حَسَنُ بْنَ عَلِيٍّ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O Hasan the son of `Ali.
Peace be upon the next successor.
O Allah, send blessings upon Your light, Your lantern,
the representative of Your representative,
the successor of Your successor,
and the argument of You against Your creatures.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكِ يَا بِنْتَ رَسُولِ ٱللَّهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O daughter of Allah’s Messenger.

segments.arabic: السَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا بِنْتَ فَاطِمَةَ وَخَدِيـجَةَ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayki ya binta fatimata wa khadijata

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O daughter of Fatimah and Khadijah.

segments.arabic: السَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكِ يَا بِنْتَ أَمِيرِ ٱلْمُؤمِنينَ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O daughter of the Commander of the Faithful.

segments.arabic: السَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكِ يَا بِنْتَ ٱلْحَسَنِ وَٱلْحُسَيْنِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O daughter of al-Hasan and al-Husayn.

segments.arabic: السَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكِ يَا بِنْتَ وَلِيِّ ٱللَّهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O daughter of Allah’s representative.

segments.arabic: السَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكِ يَا أُخْتَ وَلِيِّ ٱللَّهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O sister of Allah’s representative.

segments.arabic: السَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكِ يَا عَمَّةَ وَلِيِّ ٱللَّهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O paternal aunt of Allah’s representative.

segments.arabic: السَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكِ يَا بِنْتَ مُوسَىٰ بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayki ya binta musa bni ja`farin

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O daughter of Musa the son of Ja`far.
Allah’s mercy and blessings, too, be upon you.
Peace be upon you.

segments.arabic: عَرَّفَ ٱللَّهُ بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَكُمْ فِي ٱلْجَنَّةِ
segments.transliteration: `arrafa allahu baynana wa baynakum fi aljannati

translation.en: May Allah introduce us to you all in Paradise,
include us with your group,
allow us to approach the pond of Your Prophet,
and serve us with water from the cup of your grandfather

segments.arabic: مِنْ يَدِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ

translation.en: and from the hand of `Ali the son of Abu-Talib.
Allah’s blessings be upon you all.

segments.arabic: أَسْأَلُ ٱللَّهَ أَنْ يُرِيَنَا فِيكُمُ ٱلسُّرُورَ وَٱلْفَرَجَ
segments.transliteration: as'alu allaha an yuriyana fikumu alssurura walfaraja

translation.en: I beseech Allah to show us delight and relief through you,

segments.arabic: وَأَنْ يَجْمَعَنَا وَإِِيَّاكُمْ فِي زُمْرَةِ جَدِّكُمْ
segments.transliteration: wa an yajma`ana wa iyyakum fi zumrati jaddikum

translation.en: to join us with you in the group of your grandfather
Muhammad, Allah’s blessings be upon him and his Household,
and never to deprive us of our recognition of you.
Verily, He is Guardian and All-powerful.
I seek nearness to Allah through my love for you,
repudiation of your enemies,
and submission to Allah,

segments.arabic: رَاضِياً بِهِ غَيْرَ مُنْكِرٍ وَلاَ مُسْتَكْبِرٍ
segments.transliteration: radiyan bihi ghayra munkirin wa la mustakbirin

translation.en: accepting Him without denial or arrogance,

segments.arabic: وَعَلَىٰ يَقيِنِ مَا أَتَىٰ بِهِ مُحَمَّدٌ
segments.transliteration: wa `ala yaqini ma ata bihi muhammadun

translation.en: being fully certain of what Muhammad has brought,
and showing full satisfaction with it.
We, through this, only seek Your Face,
O my Master Allah, and Your satisfaction
as well as the Next Abode.

segments.arabic: يَا فَاطِمَةُ ٱشْفَعِي لِي فِي ٱلْجَنَّةِ
segments.transliteration: ya fatimatu ishfa`i li fi aljannati

translation.en: O Fatimah, (please) intercede for me to win Paradise,

segments.arabic: فَإِنَّ لَكِ عِنْدَ ٱللَّهِ شَأْناً مِنَ ٱلشَّأْنِ
segments.transliteration: fa'inna laki `inda allahi sha'nan min alshsha'ni

translation.en: for you enjoy an outstanding status with Allah.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ أَنْ تَخْتِمَ لِي بِٱلسَّعَادَةِ
segments.transliteration: allahumma inni as'aluka an takhtima li bilssa`adati

translation.en: O Allah, I do beseech You to seal my life with happiness;

segments.arabic: فَلاَ تَسْلُبْ مِنِّي مَا أَنَا فِيهِ
segments.transliteration: fala taslub minni ma ana fihi

translation.en: so, (please) do not deprive me of that which I enjoy.

segments.arabic: وَلاَ حَوْلَ وَلاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاَّ بِٱللَّهِ ٱلْعَلِيِّ ٱلْعَظِيمِ
segments.transliteration: wa la hawla wa la quwwata illa billahi al`aliyyi al`azimi

translation.en: There is neither might nor power except with Allah the All-high and All-great.
O Allah, (please) respond to us
and accept this from us in the name of Your generosity, almightiness,
mercy, and bestowal of wellbeing.

segments.arabic: وَصَلَّىٰ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِهِ أَجْمَعِينَ
segments.transliteration: wa salla allahu `ala muhammadin wa alihi ajma`ina

translation.en: May Allah bless Muhammad and his entire Household

segments.transliteration: wa sallama tasliman ya arhama alrrahimina

translation.en: and pour down peace on them, O most merciful of all those who show mercy.

name: Ziyarat of Abd Al azim Al Hasani (as)

text.en:
SHORT BIOGRAPHY OF ‘ABD AL-’AZIM AL-HASANI
The lineage of ‘Abd al-’Azim al-Hasani (known as Shah ‘Abd al-
’Azim), the great personality, goes back through four lines to Imam
al-Hasan al-Mujtaba the grandson of the best of all creatures; namely,
the Holy Prophet, peace be upon him and his Household.
text.ar: عبد العظيم ـ شاه زاده عبد العظيم ـ اللازم التعظيم وينتهي نسبه الشريف بوسائط أربع إلى سبط خير الورى الإمام الحسن المجتبى عليه‌السلام فهو عبد العظيم بن عبد الله بن علي بن الحسن بن زيد بن الحسن بن علي بن أبي طالب عليه‌السلام ، ومرقده الشريف في الري معروف مشهور وملاذ ومعاذ لعامّة الخلق وعلو مقامه وجلالة شأنه أظهر من الشمس فإنّه من سلالة خاتم النبيّين وهو مع ذلك من أكابر المحدّثين ، وأعاظم العلماء والزهّاد والعبّاد وذوي الورع والتقوى. وهو من أصحاب الجواد والهادي عليه‌السلام وكان متوسّلا بهما أقصى درجات التوسّل ومنقطعاً إليهما غاية الانقطاع. وقد روى عنهما أحاديث كثيرة [١] وهو المؤلف لكتاب خطب أمير المؤمنين عليه‌السلام ، وكتاب اليوم والليلة

text.en:
Abd al-’Azim is thus the son of ‘Abdullah the son of ‘Ali the son
of al-Hasan the son of Zayn the son of al-Hasan the son of ‘Ali the son
of Abu-Talib. His holy tomb, which is in the city of Ray (southern
Tehran, Iran), is famous. It is a haven and a shelter for the publics.
The high standing and majestic position of ‘Abd al-’Azim is clearer
than sunlight. First of all, he is from the descendants of the Seal of the
Prophets. He is also one of the master traditionists, great scholars,
ascetics, and worshippers, and well-known for his distinguished
religiousness and piety. He was within the companions of Imam al-
Jawad and Imam al-Hadi whom he used to take as means to Almighty
Allah and to whom he adhered distinguishably. He also reported many
traditions from these two Holy Imams.
text.ar: وهو الذي عرض دينه على إمام زمانه الإمام الهادي عليه‌السلام فأقره وصدّقه وقال : يا أبا القاسم ، هذا والله دين الله الذي ارتضاه فأثبت عليه ثبتك الله بالقول الثابت في الدنيا والآخِرة ]. وقد ألّف الصاحب بن عبّاد رسالة وجيزة في أحواله ، وشيخنا ثقة الإسلام النوري قد أورد الوجيزة في خاتمة كتاب المستدرك

text.en:
He was the author of the books entitled Khutab Amir al-Mu’minin
(Sermons of the Commander of the Faithful) and al-Yawm wal-
Laylah (The Day and the Night).
His story of providing his beliefs before Imam al-Hadi (‘a) is
famous. The Imam (‘a) then said to him, “O Abu’l-Qasim, this is by
Allah the very religion that Allah has accepted for His servants;
therefore, you should keep on it. Allah may strengthen your faith by
the true Words in this world and in the life to come.” Al-Sahib ibn ‘Abbad wrote a brief thesis about the biography of
this man. This thesis has been quoted by our mentor Shaykh al-Nuri in
the epilogue of his book entitled al-Mustadrak.
text.ar: وروى هناك وفي كتاب الرجال للنجاشي أنه خاف من السلطان فطاف بالبلدان على أنّه فيج الرسول) ثم ورد الريّ وسكن بساربانان. وعلى رواية النجاشي سكن سربا في دار رجل من الشيعة في سكة المولى وكان يعبد الله في ذلك السرب ويصوم نهاره ويقوم ليله وكان يخرج مستتراً يزور القبر المقابل لقبره وبينهما الطريق ويقول هو رجل من ولد موسى بن جعفر عليه‌السلام.

text.en:
In this book, as well as the famous book of biography known as
Rijal al-Kashshi, it has been recorded that when ‘Abd al-’Azim feared
for himself the persecution of the ruling authorities, he had to leave
for several countries disguising. He then came to the city of Ray and
resided in Sarbanan. He confided himself in a vault in a house of a
Shi’ite man in a place called Sikkat al-Mawla. He used to worship
Almighty Allah in that vault by spending his days fasting and his
nights doing acts of worship. Disguising himself, he used to leave that
vault for visiting the tomb that would be opposite to his tomb at the
other side of the road. He used to say that the tomb was for one of the
descendants of Imam Musa ibn Ja’far al-Kazim, peace be upon him.
During his stay in that vault, the Shi’ah reported his manners to one
another until he was known by the majority of them. One night, one of
these Shi’ah saw in dream the Holy Prophet (‘a) saying to him, “One
of my descendants will be carried from Sikkat al-Mawla and buried
next to that apple tree in the orchard of ‘Abd al-Jabbar the son of ‘Abd
text.ar: فلم يزل يأوي إلى ذلك السرب ويقع خبره إلى الواحد بعد الواحد من شيعة آل محمد عليه وعليهم السلام حتى عرفه أكثرهم فرأى رجل من الشيعة في المنام رسول الله صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله قال له : إن رجلا من ولدي يحمل من سكة الموالي ويدفن عند شجرة التفاح في باغ (بستان) عبد الجبار بن عبد الوهاب وأشار إلى المكان الذي دفن فيه فذهب الرجل ليشتري الشجرة ومكانها من صاحبها فقال له : لأي شي تطلب الشجرة ومكانها فأخبره بالرؤيا فذكر صاحب الشجرة ، أنّه كان رأى مثل هذه الرؤيا وأنه جعل موضع الشجرة مع جميع الباغ (البستان) وقفاً على الشريف والشيعة يدفنون فيه. فمرض عبد العظيم ومات (رض) فلما جرّد ليغسّل وجد في جيبه رقعة فيها ذكر نسبه فإذا فيها : أنا أبو القاسم عبد العظيم بن عبد الله بن علي بن الحسن بن زيد بن الحسن بن علي بن أبي طالب عليه‌السلام

text.en:
al-Wahhab. The man therefore went to the owner of that orchard and
offered to buy that tree and its place. The owner of the orchard asked,
“What for are you asking me to sell you that very tree and its place?”
The man had to inform him about his dream. The owner then told him
that he had seen a similar dream and, as a result, he endowed that tree
and its place to the sayyids and the Shi’ah to bury their dead ones
there.
text.ar: وقال أيضاً الصاحب بن عبّاد في وصف علم عبد العظيم : أنه روى أبو تراب الروياني قال : سمعت أبا حماد الرَّازي يقول : دخلت على الإمام علي النقي عليه‌السلام في سر من رأى فسألته عن أشياء من حلالي وحرامي فأجابني فلما ودّعته قال لي : يا حمّاد إذا أشكل عليك شي من أُمور دينك بناحيتك أي في بلدة الري فسل عنه عبد العظيم بن عبد الله الحسني وأقرئه مني السلام. وقال المحقّق الدّاماد في كتاب الرواشح : إن في فضل زيارة عبد العظيم روايات متضافرة. وروي أن من زار قبره وجبت له الجنة

text.en:
After that, ‘Abd al-’Azim became ill and passed away. May Allah
have mercy upon him. When he was stripped of his clothes for
performing the ritual bathing for his dead body, a sheet of paper was
found in his pocket. In that sheet of paper, he had written down his
lineage as follows: “I am Abu’l-Qasim ‘Abd al-’Azim the son of
‘Abdullah the son of ‘Ali the son of al-Hasan the son of Zayd the son
of al-Hasan the son of ‘Ali the son of Abu-Talib.”
text.ar: وهذا الحديث رواه أيضاً الشهيد الثاني (رض) في حواشي الخلاصة عن بعض النسّابين

text.en:
Describing the knowledgeability of ‘Abd al-’Azim, al-Sahib ibn
‘Abbad says that Abu-Turab al-Rawayani has reported that he heard
Abu-Hammad al-Razi saying: One day, I visited Imam ‘Ali al-Naqi
(al-Hadi) in Samarra’ and asked him about some legal and illegal
issues of the religion. The Imam (‘a) answered all of my questions.
When I bid farewell to him, he said to me, “Hammad, if you face any
problem regarding your religious affairs while you are in your
hometown (i.e. Ray), you may ask ‘Abd al-’Azim ibn ‘Abdullah al-
Hasani. Once you do, convey my greetings to him.”
text.ar: وروى ابن بابويه وابن قولويه بسند معتبر عن رجل من أهل الرّي عن الإمام عليّ النقيّ صلوات الله عليه قال : دخلت عليه فقال : أين كنت؟

text.en:
In his book of al-Rawashih, al-Muhaqqiq al-Damad says that the
rewards of visiting the tomb of ‘Abd al-’Azim al-Hasani have been
mentioned in confirmative traditions. One of these traditions reads that
whoever visits his tomb will be awarded Paradise inevitably.
This very tradition has been also mentioned by al-Shahid al-Thani
in the margin of his book of al-Khulasah as reported from some
experts in lineages.
Ibn Babawayh and Ibn Qawlawayh have reported through a valid
chain of authority that a man from the people of Ray visited Imam
‘Ali al-Hadi (‘a) who asked him where from he had come. “I came
here after I had visited Imam al-Husayn,” answered the man.
The Imam (‘a) said, “Actually, if you had visited the tomb of ‘Abd
al-’Azim in your city, you would have been as if you have visited al-
Husayn ibn ‘Ali, peace be upon them.”
text.ar: فقلت : زرت الحسين عليه‌السلام قال : أما لو أنّك زرت قبر عبد العظيم عليه‌السلام عندكم لكنت كمن زار الحسين بن علي عليهما‌السلام

text.en:
METHOD OF ZIYARAH OF ‘ABD AL-’AZIM
Scholars have not mentioned a form of ziyarah dedicated to ‘Abd
al-’Azim al-Hasani; rather, Fakhr al-Muhaqqiqin (the pride of versed
scholars), Jamal al-Din states in his book of al-Mazar that it is most
appropriate to say the following form of ziyarah at the tomb of ‘Abd
al-’Azim al-Hasani:
text.ar: أقول : لم يذكر العلماء زيارة خاصّة وإنما قال فخر المحققين جمال الدين في مزاره : إنّ من المناسب أن يزار هكذا :

segments.arabic: السَّلامُ عَلَى آدَمَ صَفْوَةِ الله السَّلامُ عَلَى نُوحٍ نَبِيِّ الله السَّلامُ عَلَى إِبْراهِيمَ خَلِيلِ الله السَّلامُ عَلَى مُوسى كَلِيمِ الله السَّلامُ عَلَى عِيسى رُوحِ اللهِ ، السَّلامُ عَلَيْكَ يا رَسُولَ الله السَّلامُ عَلَيْكَ يا خَيْرَ خَلْقِ الله السَّلامُ عَلَيْكَ يا صَفِيَّ الله

السَّلامُ عَلَيْكَ يا مُحَمَّد بْنَ عَبْدِ الله خاتَمَ النَّبِيِّينَ ، السَّلامُ عَلَيْكَ يا أمِيرَ المُؤْمِنِينَ عَلِيَّ بْنَ أَبِي طالِبٍ وَصِيَّ رَسُولِ اللهِ ، السَّلامُ عَلَيْكِ يا فاطِمَةُ سَيِّدَةَ نِساءِ العالَمِينَ ، السَّلامُ عَلَيْكُما يا سِبْطَي الرَّحْمَةِ وَسَيِّدَيْ شَبابِ أَهْلِ الجَنَّةِ

السَّلامُ عَلَيْكَ يا عَلِيَّ بْنَ الحُسَيْنِ سَيِّدَ العابِدِينَ وَقُرَّةَ عَيْنِ النَّاظِرِينَ ، السَّلامُ عَلَيْكَ يا مُحَمَّدَ بْنَ عَلِيٍّ باقِرَ العِلْمِ بَعْدَ النَّبِيِّ ، السَّلامُ عَلَيْكَ يا جَعْفَرَ بْنَ مُحَمَّدٍ الصَّادِقَ البارَّ الاَمِينَ ، السَّلامُ عَلَيْكَ يا مُوسى بْنَ جَعْفَرٍ الطَّاهِرَ الطُّهْرَ ،

السَّلامُ عَلَيْكَ يا عَلِيَّ بْنَ مُوسى الرِّضا المُرْتَضى ، السَّلامُ عَلَيْكَ يا مُحَمَّدَ بْنَ عَلِيِّ التَّقِيَّ ، السَّلامُ عَلَيْكَ يا عَلِيَّ بْنَ مُحَمَّدٍ النَّقِيَّ النَّاصِحَ الاَمِينَ ، السَّلامُ عَلَيْكَ يا حَسَنَ بْنَ عَلِيٍّ ، السَّلامُ عَلى الوَصِيِّ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ. اللّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلى نُورِكَ وَسِراجِكَ وَوَلِيِّ وَلِيِّكَ وَوَصِيِّ وَصِيِّكَ وَحُجَّتِكَ عَلى خَلْقِكَ ، السَّلامُ عَلَيْكَ أَيُّها السَّيِّدُ الزَّكِيُّ وَالطَّاهِرُ الصَّفِيُّ ، السَّلامُ عَلَيْكَ يا بْنَ السَّادَةِ الاَطْهارِ السَّلامُ عَلَيْكَ يا بْنَ المُصْطَفَينَ الاَخْيارِ ،

السَّلامُ عَلى رَسُولِ الله وَعَلى ذُرِّيَّةِ رَسُولِ الله وَرَحْمَةُ الله وَبَرَكاتُهُ ، السَّلامُ عَلى العَبْدِ الصَّالِحِ المُطِيعِ للهِ رَبِّ العالَمِينَ وَلِرَسُولِهِ وَلأَمِيرِ المُؤْمِنِينَ ، السَّلامُ عَلَيْكَ يا أَبا القاسِمِ ابْنَ السِّبْطِ المُنْتَجَبِ المُجْتَبى السَّلامُ عَلَيْكَ يا مَنْ بِزِيارَتِهِ ثَوابُ زِيارَةِ سَيِّدِ الشُّهَداءِ يِرْتَجى

السَّلامُ عَلَيْكَ عَرَّفَ الله بَيْنَنا وَبَيْنَكُمْ فِي الجَنَّةِ وَحَشَرَنا فِي زُمْرَتِكُمْ وَأَوْرَدَنا حَوْضَ نَبِيِّكُمْ وَسَقانا بِكَأْسِ جَدِّكُمْ مِنْ يَدِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طالِبٍ صَلَواتُ الله عَلَيْكُمْ ؛ أَسْأَلُ الله أَنْ يُرِيَنا فِيكُمُ السُّرُورَ وَالفَرَجَ وَأَنْ يَجْمَعَنا وَإِيَّاكُمْ فِي زُمْرَةِ جَدِّكُمْ مُحَمَّدٍ صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله وَأَنْ لايَسْلُبَنا مَعْرِفَتَكُمْ إِنَّهُ وَلِيُّ قَدِيرٌ.

أَتَقَرَّبُ إِلى الله بِحُبِّكُمْ وَالبَرائةِ مِنْ أَعْدائِكُمْ وَالتَّسْلِيمِ إِلى الله راضِياً بِهِ غَيْرَ مُنْكِرٍ وَلامُسْتَكْبِرٍ وَعَلى يَقِينٍ ماأَتى بِهِ مُحَمَّدٌ ، نَطْلُبُ بِذلِكَ وَجْهَكَ يا سَيِّدِي اللّهُمَّ وَرِضاكَ وَالدَّارَ الآخِرةَ ، يا سَيِّدِي وَابْنَ سَيِّدِي اشْفَعْ لِي فِي الجَنَّةِ فَإِنَّ لَكَ عِنْدَ الله شَأْنا مِنَ الشَّأْنِ ، اللّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ أَنْ تَخْتِمَ لِي بِالسَّعادَةِ فَلا تَسْلُبَ مِنِّي ما أَنا فِيهِ وَلاحَوْلَ وَلاقُوَّةَ إِلاّ بِالله العَلِيِّ العَظِيمِ ، اللّهُمَّ اسْتَجِبْ لَنا وَتَقَبَّلْهُ بِكَرَمِكَ وَعِزَّتِكَ وَبِرَحْمَتِكَ وَعافِيَتِكَ وَصَلّى الله عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِهِ أَجْمَعِينَ وَسَلَّمَ تَسْلِيماً يا أرْحَمَ الرَّاحِمِينَ.

text.en:
Al-Muhaqqiq al-Damad added: It has been reported that ‘Abd al-
’Azim al-Hasani, disguising himself, used to leave the place of his
residence for visiting the tomb that would be opposite to his tomb at
the other side of the road. He used to say that the tomb was for one of
the descendants of Imam Musa ibn Ja’far al-Kazim, peace be upon
him.
In the present day, we can see at this place a tomb ascribed to
Hamzah the son of Imam Musa al-Kazim. Apparently, ‘Abd al-’Azim
al-Hasani used to visit this tomb; it is therefore imperative to visit this
tomb, too.
text.ar: ثم قال المحقق المذكور : ورد في الأحاديث أنّ عبد العظيم كان يخرج عند إقامته بالريّ مستتراً يزور القبر المقابل قبره وبينهما الطريق ويقول : هو رجل من ولد موسى بن جعفر عليه‌السلام

text.en:
At visiting this tomb, it is unobjectionable to say the same form of
ziyarah that is said at visiting the tomb of ‘Abd al-’Azim with some
changes, of course, in the statements in which the name of ‘Abd al-
’Azim is mentioned.
In the courtyard of the shrine of Hamzah the son of Imam al-
Kazim, there lies the tomb of the pleased and sublime master scholar
and the exemplar of exegetes of the Holy Qur’an, Jamal al-Din Abu’l-
Futuh Husayn ibn ‘Ali al-Khuza’i, the author of the famous book of
Tafsir. It is therefore important to visit his tomb.
The tomb of Shaykh al-Saduq, the head of traditionists, known as
Ibn Babawayh, lies in a place near the town in which ‘Abd al-’Azim
al-Hasani was buried. It is also important not to neglect visiting his
tomb.
text.ar: ونجد هناك في عصرنا قبراً ينسب إلى حمزة ابن الإمام موسى عليه‌السلام والظاهر أنّه القبر الذي كان يزوره عبد العظيم وينبغي زيارته أيضاً إن شاء اللهِ ، ولا بأس بأن يزار بهذه الزيارة إِلاّ أنه يحذف منها الجملة : السلام عليك يا أبا القاسم ، والجملة التي تليها ، انتهى. لا يخفى عليك أنّ قبر الشيخ الجليل السعيد قدوة المفسرين جمال الدين أبي الفتوح حسين بن علي الخزاعي (رض) صاحب التفسير المعروف واقع في صحن حمزة عليه‌السلام وينبغي زيارته ، والشيخ الصدوق رئيس المحدثين المعروف بابن بابويه قبره بقرب بلدة شاهزاده عبد العظيم فلا تغفل عن زيارته أيضا.

text.en: As is mentioned in the book of Misbah al-Za’ir and other books, the recommended devotional acts at Masjid al-Kufah are as follows:
text.ar: فهي على ما في (مصباح الزائر) وغيره كما يلي : قل حينما تدخل مدينة الكوفة :

text.en: You may begin with the following words when you enter the city of al-Kufah:

translation.en: In the Name of Allah and in Allah
on the way of Allah
and the course of Allah’s Messenger
peace be upon him and his Household

O Allah, (please do) enable me
to reside blessedly
and You are the best of those who enable to reside.

text.en: You may then walk toward the mosque, repeating the following statements
text.ar: ثم سر نحو المسجد وأنت تقول :

translation.en: Allah is the Most Great.
There is no god but Allah.
All praise be to Allah.
All glory be to Allah.

text.en: When you reach the gate of the mosque, you may stop there and say the following words
text.ar: حتى تأتي باب المسجد ، فإذا أتيته فقف على الباب وقل :

translation.en: Peace be upon our master the Messenger of Allah
Mu¦ammad son of `Abdullah
and upon his immaculate progeny.
Peace be upon the Commander of the Faithful
Ali the son of Abu Talib
Allah’s mercy and blessings be upon him
upon his sessions, upon his meeting-places

segments.arabic: وَ مَقَامِ حِكْمَتِهِ وَ آثَارِ آبَائِهِ

translation.en: upon the places that witnessed his wisdom, upon the traces of his forefathers

segments.arabic: آدَمَ وَ نُوحٍ وَ اِبْرَاهِيمَ وَ اِسْمَاعِيلَ

translation.en: Adam, Noah, Abraham, and Ishmael
and upon the places that witnessed the presentations of his evidences
Peace be upon the Imam: the wise, the just
the grandest veracious, and the fair distinguisher

segments.arabic: الَّذِي فَرَّقَ اللّٰهُ بِهِ بَيْنَ الْحَقِّ وَ الْبَاطِلِ

translation.en: by whom Allah distinguished the right from the wrong
atheism from (true) belief
and polytheism from monotheism
so that he who would perish might perish by clear proof

segments.arabic: وَ يَحْيَا مَنْ حَيَّ عَنْ بَيِّنَةٍ

translation.en: and he who would live might live by clear proof.
I bear witness that you are verily the commander of the faithful,
the elite of the selves of the select ones
the adornment of the veracious ones
and the most steadfast among the tried ones

segments.arabic: وَ اَنَّكَ حَكَمُ اللّٰهِ فِي اَرْضِهِ

translation.en: and that you are verily the judge of Allah on His lands
the decider according to His orders
the door to His wisdom
the maker of His pledge
the spokesman of His promise

segments.arabic: وَ الْحَبْلُ الْمَوْصُولُ بَيْنَهُ وَ بَيْنَ عِبَادِهِ

translation.en: the rope that is connected between His servants and Him
the haven of redemption
the course of piety
the most elevated rank
and the executor of the laws of the Most Exalted Judge
O Commander of the Faithful
through you do I seek close nearness to Allah
for you are my chief, master

segments.arabic: وَ وَسِيلَتِي فِي الدُّنْيَا وَ الْآخِرَةِ

translation.en: and means (to Allah) in this world as well as the Hereafter

text.en: You may then enter the mosque.
text.ar: ثم تدخل المسجد.

text.en: It is however more favorable to enter the mosque from the gate situated at the backside. This gate is known as bab al-fil (The Elephant Gate). You may then say the following words:
text.ar: أقول : والأفضل أن تدخل من الباب الواقع خلف المسجد المشهور بباب الفيل ثمّ تقول :

segments.arabic: اللّٰهُ اَكْبَرُ اللّٰهُ اَكْبَرُ اللّٰهُ اَكْبَرُ

translation.en: Allah is the Most Great
This is the situation of him who seeks refuge with Allah
with Mu¦ammad the most beloved by Allah
may Allah’s peace be upon him and his Household
with the (Divinely commissioned) leadership of the Commander of the Faithful
and with the Imams; the well- guided, the veracious,
the spokesmen (of Allah), the orthodox,
from whom Allah removed filth
and He purified them with thorough purification
I accept them as leaders
guides, and masters
I succumb to the acts of Allah
I associate none with Him

segments.arabic: وَ لا اَتَّخِذُ مَعَ اللّٰهِ وَلِيّا

translation.en: and I do not betake any master other than Allah.
Truly liars are those who set up equals with Allah
as they have certainly strayed off in remote error
Sufficient for me are Allah and the saints of Allah.

segments.arabic: اَشْهَدُ اَنْ لا اِلَهَ اِلا اللّٰهُ

translation.en: I bear witness that there is no god but Allah
alone without having any partner

segments.arabic: وَ اَشْهَدُ اَنَّ مُحَمَّدا عَبْدُهُ وَ رَسُولُهُ

translation.en: And I bear witness that Mu¦ammad is His servant and Messenger
may Allah’s blessings be upon him and his Household

segments.arabic: وَ اَنَّ عَلِيّا وَ الْاَئِمَّةَ الْمَهْدِيِّينَ مِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِهِ عَلَيْهِمُ السَّلامُ

translation.en: and that `Ali and the well- guided Imams from his progeny, peace be upon them all

segments.arabic: اَوْلِيَائِي وَ حُجَّةُ اللّٰهِ عَلَى خَلْقِهِ.

translation.en: are my masters and the arguments of Allah against His created beings.

text.en:
You may then walk to the fourth column of the mosque, which is situated next to bab al-anmat and opposite to the fifth column. This column is also called istiwanat ibrahim (the column of Prophet Abraham). You may then offer a four-unit prayer. In the first two units, you may recite Surah al-Fatihah and Surah al-Tawhid. In the other two units, you may recite Surah al-Fatihah and <ref=1:97>Surah Al-Qadr</ref>
. When you finish, you may say tasbih al-zahra’ litany and then say the following words:
text.ar: ثم سر إلى الأسطوانة الرابعة الواقعة إلى جانب باب الأنماط بحذاء الخامسة وهي أسطوانة إبراهيم عليه‌السلام فصل عندها أربع ركعات : ركعتان بالحمد والتوحيد (قُلْ هُوَ الله أَحَد) وركعتان بالحمد و <ref=1:97>القدر</ref> فإذا فرغت منها فسبح تسبيح الزهراء عليها‌السلام وقل :

segments.arabic: السَّلامُ عَلَى عِبَادِ اللّٰهِ الصَّالِحِينَ الرَّاشِدِينَ

translation.en: Peace be upon the righteous, orthodox servants of Allah
from whom Allah has removed filth and purified them a
thorough purification
He also made them Prophets and Messengers
and set them as arguments against all the created beings.
Peace be upon the Messengers
and all praise be to Allah the Lord of the worlds.
This is the arrangement of the Mighty, Knowing Lord

text.en: You may then repeat the following statement seven times:
text.ar: وقل سبع مرات :

translation.en: Peace and salutation to Noah among the nations.

text.en: You may then say the following words:
text.ar: ثم قل :

segments.arabic: نَحْنُ عَلَى وَصِيَّتِكَ يَا وَلِيَّ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ

translation.en: O master of the believers, we are abiding by your precept
that you provided to your offspring
the Messengers and the veracious ones
And we are among your adherents
and among the adherents of our Prophet Muhammad
peace of Allah be upon him and his Household

segments.arabic: وَ عَلَيْكَ وَ عَلَى جَمِيعِ الْمُرْسَلِينَ

translation.en: and upon you as well as all the Messengers,
Prophets, and veracious ones
And we are also following the doctrine of Abraham
the religion of Muhammad the Meccan Prophet
and the religion of the well- guided Imams

segments.arabic: وَ وِلايَةِ مَوْلانَا عَلِيٍّ اَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ

translation.en: and we are loyal to the leadership of `Ali, the Commander of the Faithful
Peace be upon the bearer of glad tidings (for the believers) and the warner
May Allah’s peace

segments.arabic: وَ رَحْمَتُهُ وَ رِضْوَانُهُ وَ بَرَكَاتُهُ

translation.en: mercy, pleasure, and blessings be upon him
and upon his successor and vicegerent

segments.arabic: الشَّاهِدِ لِلَّهِ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ عَلَى خَلْقِهِ

translation.en: the witness on Allah’s creatures after him (the Prophet)
`Ali, the Commander of the Faithful
the grandest veracious
and the clear distinguisher (between the right and the wrong)

segments.arabic: الَّذِي اَخَذْتَ بَيْعَتَهُ [اُخِذَتْ بَيْعَتُهُ‏] عَلَى الْعَالَمِينَ

translation.en: whom You have ordered the peoples to swear allegiance to

segments.arabic: رَضِيتُ بِهِمْ اَوْلِيَاءَ وَ مَوَالِيَّ وَ حُكَّاما

translation.en: I thus accept them as masters, leaders, and rulers
over myself, my progeny

segments.arabic: وَ اَهْلِي وَ مَالِي وَ قِسْمِي

translation.en: my family members, my property, whatever sustenance You decide for me,
whatever is legal or illegal for me to do
my being Muslim, my religion,
my worldly life, my Next Life
my being kept alive, and my death
You (the Infallible Imams) are indeed the Imams whom are mentioned in the (Holy) Book
and you are the clear leaders in all situations
the clear judges
the eyes of the Ever-living Lord Who never sleeps
and the judging representatives of Allah
Through you has Allah judged
and though you was the right of Allah recognized.
There is no god but Allah.
Mu¦ammad is the messenger of Allah

segments.arabic: أَنْتُمْ نُورُ الله مِنْ بَيْنِ أَيْدِينا وَمِنْ خَلْفِنا

translation.en: You are the Light of Allah that lies before and behind us

segments.arabic: أَنْتُمْ سُنَّةُ الله الَّتِي بِها سَبَقَ القَضاء

translation.en: You are the instructions of Allah that He has decided before all things.
O Commander of the Faithful,
I submit to you with thorough submission
I never associate with Allah anything [or anyone],
and I never betake a master other than Him
All praise be to Allah Who has guided me to this

segments.arabic: وَما كُنْتُ لاِ هْتَدِيَ لَوْلا أَنْ هَدانِي الله

translation.en: and I should never have been guided if Allah had not guided me
Allah is the Most Great. Allah is the Most Great. Allah is the Most Great
All praise be to Allah for that to which He has guided us

name: Aamal In The Seat Of Judgment

text.en: Aamal In The Seat Of Judgment (DAKKAT AL QAZA) And The Place Of The Wash-Tub (BAYT AL TASHT)
text.ar: أعمال دكة القضاء وبيت الطست

text.en: The seat of judgment (dakkat al-qaza’) was a shop-like structure inside Masjid al-Kufah in which there was a bench that was used by Imam ‘Ali (‘a) for judging. Next to this place, there was a short column on which the following holy verse was written:[Verily God has enjoined justice, the doing of good] انّ اللّه يامر بالعدل و الاحسان
text.ar: واعلم أن دكة القضاء كانت بناءً في جامع الكوفة يشبه الحانوت يجلس عليها أمير المؤمنين عليه‌السلام للقَضاء والحكم وكانت هنالك أسطوانة قصيرة كتب عليها الآية : (إنَّ الله يأْمُرُ بِالعَدْلِ والاحْسانِ)

text.en: The place of the washtub (bayt al-tast) is the place where one of the miracles of Imam ‘Ali (‘a) was shown: While an unmarried girl was swimming in a pool, a leech could penetrate into her body to settle in her womb. This leech fed on the blood of that girl and thus grew up. Hence, the girl’s belly began to grow bigger and bigger that her brothers though that she was illegally pregnant. As they intended to kill their sister, they first provided the case before Imam ‘Ali (‘a) to decide about her. The Imam (‘a) ordered that a thick curtain should be erected in one of the sides of the mosque (Masjid al-Kufah) behind which the girl would sit. The Imam (‘a) then asked a famous midwife in al-Kufah city to check the girl. The answer of the midwife came as follows: “O Amir al-Mu’minin, this girl is pregnant. She carries a fetus in her womb.” However, the Imam (‘a) ordered them to bring a washtub made of clay and he asked them to make that girl sit there. When the leech felt the stench of the clay, it sneaked out from where it had entered and it fell in the washtub. Thus, the innocence of the girl was proved.
text.ar: وبيت الطست هو المكان الذي برزت فيه معجزة لأمير المؤمنين عليه‌السلام في بنت عزباء كانت قد غاصت في ماء فيه العلق فولجت علقة في جوفها فنمت وكبرت ممّا امتصته من الدم فعلا بذلك بطن البنت فحسبها إخوتها حُبلى فراموا قتلها فأتوا أمير المؤمنين عليه‌السلام ليحكم بينهم فأمر عليه‌السلام بستار فضرب في جانب من المسجد وجعلت البنت خلفه وأمر بقابلة الكوفة ففحصتها وأعلنت رأيها فقالت : يا أمير المؤمنين إنها حبلى تحمل جنينا في جوفها فأمر عليه‌السلام بطست من الحمأة فأجلست البنت عليه فأحست العلقة بذفر الحمأة فانسلَّت من جوفها نحو الطست.

text.en: According to other narrations, Imam ‘Ali (‘a) extended his hand and brought a piece of ice from the mountains of Syria and put it in the washtub, causing the leech to come out.
text.ar: وفي بعض الروايات أنه عليه‌السلام مد يده فأتى بقطع من الثلج من جبال الشام وجعله عند الطست فانسلت العلقة

text.en: Be it known to you that the order of doing the recommended acts in Masjid al-Kufah according to the opinion of the majority of scholars is that the acts to be done in the center of the mosque come directly after the acts at the fourth column. Hence, the acts of the seat of judgment and the place of washtub should be the last of all, which means that they are done after the accomplishment of the acts at the seat of Imam al-Sadiq (‘a) (dakkat al-sadiq).
text.ar: واعلم أيضاً أن المشهور في ترتيب أعمال جامع الكوفة أن تتلو أعمال وسط المسجد أعمال الأسطوانة الرابعة فتؤخر أعمال دكة القضاء وبيت الطست عن جميع أعمال المسجد وتؤدي عند الفراغ من أعمال دكة الصادق عليه‌السلام ونحن نجاري في الترتيب السيد ابن طاووس في مصباح الزائر والعلامة المجلسي في البحار والشيخ خضر في المزار وأما من تابع المشهور فليؤخر أعمال دكة القضا وبيت الطست عن الكل وليأتها بعد أعمال دكة الصادق عليه‌السلام. وبالجملة نقول : ثم امضِ إلى دكة القضاء فصل عليها ركعتين تقرأ فيها بعد الحمد ما أردت من السور فإذا فرغت منها وسبحت تسبيح الزهراء عليها‌السلام فقل :

text.en: In this book, I imitate the order followed by Sayyid Ibn Tawus, in his book of Misbah al-Za’ir, ‘Allamah al-Majlisi, in his book of Bihar al-Anwar, and Shaykh Khazir, in his book of al-Mazar.

In conclusion, one who desires to follow the opinion of the majority of scholars may do the acts at the seat of judgment (dakkat al-qaza’) and the place of washtub (bayt al-tast) after accomplishing all the other acts of Masjid al-Kufah.

Back to the acts of dakkat al-qaza’ and bayt al-tast, after finishing from the fourth column, you may walk to dakkat al-qaza’ and offer there a two-unit prayer, reciting in each unit Surah al-Fatihah and any other Surah you like to recite. After accomplishment, you may say the famous Tasbih al-Zahra’ litany and then say the following supplicatory words:

translation.en: O He Who possesses me, He Who endows me with possessions
and He Who encompasses me with enormous bounties
albeit that I do not deserve them.
My face is humble to what is overtopped by feet
due to the majesty of Your Noble Face

segments.arabic: لا تَجْعَلْ هَذِهِ الشِّدَّةَ وَ لا هَذِهِ الْمِحْنَةَ

translation.en: (Please) do not make this hardship and this ordeal
to be continual until they lead to the extirpation of the root.
And (please) grant me from Your favor

segments.arabic: مَا لَمْ تَمْنَحْ بِهِ اَحَدا مِنْ غَيْرِ مَسْاَلَةٍ

translation.en: more than that which You have granted to any body who has not asked You to grant him.
You are verily the Eternal and the First Lord

segments.arabic: الَّذِي لَمْ تَزَلْ وَ لا تَزَالُ

translation.en: Who has neither beginning nor end.

segments.arabic: صَلِّ عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ وَ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ

translation.en: (please) send blessings upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad
forgive me, have mercy on me
admit my deeds
bless my lifetime,

segments.arabic: وَ اجْعَلْنِي مِنْ عُتَقَائِكَ وَ طُلَقَائِكَ مِنَ النَّارِ

translation.en: and include me with those whom You release and pardon from Hellfire
in the name of Your mercy, O most merciful of all those who show mercy.

text.en: At the place of the washtub, which is connected to the seat of judgment, you may offer a two-unit prayer. When you finish the prayer and say the Tasbih statements, you may say the following supplicatory words:
text.ar: أعمال بيت الطست المتصل بدكَّة القضاء : تصلي هناك ركعتين ، فإذا سلمت وسبحت فقل :

translation.en: O Allah, I save (the reward of) my submission to Your Oneness
my recognition of You
my sincerity to You
and my bearing witness of Your Godhead,

segments.arabic: وَ ذَخَرْتُ وِلايَةَ مَنْ اَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيَّ

translation.en: and I also save (the reward of) my loyalty to those
with the recognition of whom You have bestowed upon me namely

segments.arabic: مُحَمَّدٍ وَ عِتْرَتِهِ صَلَّى اللّٰهُ عَلَيْهِمْ

translation.en: Mu¦ammad and his Household, peace of Allah be upon them

segments.arabic: لِيَوْمِ فَزَعِي اِلَيْكَ عَاجِلا وَ آجِلا

translation.en: (I save all that) to the day of my taking refuge with You, whether in this world or the Hereafter

segments.arabic: وَقَدْ فَزِعْتُ إِلَيْكَ وَإِلَيْهِمْ يا مَوْلايَ

translation.en: Thus, I am now taking refuge with You, O my Master, and with them

segments.arabic: فِي هذا اليَوْمِ وَفِي مَوْقِفِي هذا

translation.en: on this day and on this very situation
and I thus beg You for Your increasing bounties,
for removing from me Your chastisement that I fear
for blessing all the sustenance which You grant me
and for immunizing my heart from receiving any grievous matter
any calamity, or any act of disobedience to You
regarding my religion, worldly life, and the Next Life.
O most merciful of all those who show mercy

text.en: It is narrated that Imam al-Sadiq (‘a) offered a two-unit prayer in this place; the place of the washtub (bayt al-tast)
text.ar: وروي أنّ الصادق عليه‌السلام قد صلى ركعتين في بيت الطست

text.en:
At the center of Masjid al-Kufah, you may offer a two-unit prayer, reciting in the first unit Surah al-Fatihah and Surah al-Tawhid, and in the second unit Surah al-Fatihah and <ref=1:109>Surah Al-Kafirun</ref>
. After you finish the prayer and say the Tasbih statements, you may say the following:
text.ar: ذكر الصلاة والدعاء في وسط المسجد : تصلي هناك ركعتين تقرأ في الأولى الحمد والتوحيد : (قُلْ هُوَ الله أَحَد) ، وفي الثانية الحمد و <ref=1:109>الکافرون</ref> . فإذا سلَّمت وسبحت فقل :

translation.en: O Allah, You are the Peace
You are the source of peace
To You does peace belong.
Your abode is the abode of peace
So, our Lord, greet us with peace from You.
O Allah, I have offered this prayer
seeking Your mercy, pleasure, forgiveness
and as sign of my esteeming Your mosque

segments.arabic: اللّهُمَّ فَصَلِّ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ

translation.en: O Allah, (please) send blessings upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad
raise my prayer to the most elevated place,
and accept it from me, O most merciful of all those who show mercy.

text.en: It is worth mentioning that the center of Masjid al-Kufah is also called dakkat al-mi’raj (Seat of the Ascension to the Heavens). Apparently, when the Holy Prophet (s), at the night of his Ascension, asked permission from Almighty Allah to visit this mosque, he offered his prayer at this place.
text.ar: أقول : قد دعي هذا المقام بدكة المعراج ووجه التسمية على ما يظهر أن رسول الله صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله استأذن الله تعالى ليلة المعراج فهبط إلى الأَرض في هذه البقعة فصلّى ركعتين.

text.en: Aamal At The Seventh Column (SUTOON). The seventh column is the place where Almighty Allah guided Prophet Adam to ask for repentance. Hence, you may walk to this place, stop there, face the kiblah direction, and say the following words:
text.ar: أعمال الأسطوانة السابعة : وهي مقام وفق الله تعالى آدم فيه للتوبة. ثم امض إلى الأسطوانة السابعة وقف عندها واستقبل القبلة وقل :

translation.en: In the Name of Allah (I begin), in Allah (I trust)
and the course of Allah’s Messenger
peace be upon him and his Household, (I follow)
There is no god but Allah
Mu¦ammad is Allah’s messenger

segments.arabic: السَّلامُ عَلى أَبِينا آدَمَ وَاُمِّنا حَوَّاءَ

translation.en: Peace be upon our father Adam and our mother Eve.

segments.arabic: السَّلامُ عَلى هابِيلَ المَقْتُولِ ظُلْما وَعُدْوانا

translation.en: Peace be upon Abel who was killed wrongly and aggressively,
because he was envied for Allah’s favors and pleasure.
Peace be upon Sheeth, the select of Allah,
the chosen and trustee

segments.arabic: وَعلى الصَّفْوَةِ الصَّادِقِينَ مِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِهِ الطَّيِّبِينَ

translation.en: and upon the veracious, select ones from his immaculate progeny,
from the first to the last
Peace be upon Abraham, Ishmael
Isaac, and Jacob
and upon their chosen descendants.
Peace be upon Moses the spoken by Allah
Peace be upon Jesus the spirit of Allah

segments.arabic: السَّلامُ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ بْنِ عَبْدِ الله خاتَمِ النَّبِيِّينَ

translation.en: Peace be upon Mu¦ammad the son of `Abdull¡h, the seal of the Prophets.

segments.arabic: السَّلامُ عَلى أَمِيرِ المُؤْمِنِينَ وَذُرِّيَّتِهِ الطَيِّبِينَ

translation.en: Peace be upon the Commander of the Faithful and upon his immaculate progeny.
Allah’s mercy and blessings be upon them.
Peace be upon you among the ancient generations.
Peace be upon you among the coming generations.
Peace be upon Fatimah al- Zahra (the Luminous Lady).
Peace be upon the guiding Imams
the witnesses of Allah upon His created beings.

segments.arabic: السَّلامُ عَلى الرَّقِيبِ الشَّاهِدِ عَلى الاُمَمِ للهِ رَبِّ العالَمِينَ

translation.en: Peace be upon the supervisor the witness on the nations for Allah the Lord of the worlds.

text.en: At the same place, you may offer a four unit prayer reciting in the first unit Surah al-Fatihah and <ref=1:97>Surah Al-Qadr</ref> and reciting in the second unit Surah al-Fatihah and Surah al-Tawhid and repeat the same in the third and fourth units. When you finish this prayer and say the Tasbih statements, you may then say the following supplicatory words:
text.ar: ثم تصلي عندها أربع ركعات تقرأ في الأولى الحمد و <ref=1:97>القدر</ref> ، وفي الثانية الحمد و (قُلْ هُوَ الله أَحَد) ، وفي الثالثة والرابعة مثل ذلك ، فإذا فرغت وسبحت بتسبيح الزهراء عليها‌السلام فقل :

translation.en: O Allah, if I have disobeyed you,

segments.arabic: فَإِنِّي قَدْ أَطَعْتُكَ فِي الإيمانِ مِنِّي بِكَ

translation.en: I have also obeyed you in my belief in You.

segments.arabic: مَنّاً مِنْكَ عَلَيَّ لا مَنّاً مِنِّي عَلَيكَ

translation.en: This is out of Your favoring upon me, not a favor that I do to You.
I have also obeyed You in the things that You most like
I have not claimed that You have child
and I have not associated any one with You.
Yet, I have disobeyed You in many things
on account of neither defiance
nor mutiny against my servitude to You,
nor denial of Your Godhead;
rather, I only followed my lusts,

segments.arabic: وَأَزَلَّنِي الشَّيْطانُ بَعْدَ الحُجَّةِ عَلَيَّ وَالبَيانِ

translation.en: and Satan caused me to slip although You have had clear argument against me.

segments.arabic: فَإِنْ تُعَذِّبْنِي فَبِذُنُوبِي غَيْرَ ظالِمٍ لِي

translation.en: Thus, if You chastise me, then it is on account of my sins and You are never unjust to me.

And if You pardon me and have
mercy upon me,
then this is out of Your magnanimity and generosity, O All-generous.

segments.arabic: اللّهُمَّ إِنَّ ذُنُوبِي لَمْ يَبْقَ لَها إِلاّ رَجاءُ عَفْوِكَ

translation.en: O Allah, nothing can stand for my sins except my hope in Your pardon,
although I have done things due to which I deserve deprivation of Your pardon.
Therefore, I beseech You, O Allah, for that which I do not deserve
and I seek from You that which I do not merit.

segments.arabic: اللّهُمَّ إِنْ تُعَذِّبْنِي فَبِذُنُوبِي وَلَمْ تَظْلِمْنِي شَيْئاً

translation.en: O Allah, if you chastise me, then this will be on account of my sins and You shall never be unjust to me.

segments.arabic: وَإِنْ تَغْفِر لِي فَخَيْرُ راحِمٍ أَنْتَ يا سَيِّدِي

translation.en: But if You forgive me, then You are already the best of all those who show mercy, O my Master.
O Allah, You are what You are and I am what I am
You are always returning with forgiveness
and I always return with sinning.
You always confer with forbearance
and I always return with impatience

segments.arabic: اللّهُمَّ فَإِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ يا كَنْزَ الضُّعَفاءِ

translation.en: O Allah, I do beseech You, O  treasure of the weak,
O He in Whom great hope is put,
O rescuer of the drowned,
O savior of those about to perish
O He Who causes the alive to die,
O He Who causes the dead to live again

segments.arabic: أَنْتَ الله لا إِلهَ إِلاّ أَنْتَ

translation.en: You are Allah; there is no god but You.

segments.arabic: أَنْتَ الَّذِي سَجَدَ لَكَ شُعاعُ الشَّمْسِ

translation.en: It is You before Whom has prostrated the ray of the sun,
the sound of water
the rustling of trees
and the moonlight,
the murk of night,
the light of daytime,
and the flapping of birds.
I thus beseech You, O All-great
in the name of the duty of Muhammad and his veracious Household towards You,
in the name of Your duty towards Muhammad and his veracious Household,
in the name of the duty of `Ali towards You,
in the name of Your duty towards `Ali
in the name of the duty of Fatimah towards You,
in the name of Your duty towards Fatimah
in the name of the duty of al- Hasan towards You
in the name of Your duty towards al-Hasan
in the name of the duty of al- Husayn towards You,
and in the name of Your duty towards al-Husayn.

segments.arabic: فَإِنَّ حُقُوقَهُمْ عَلَيكَ مِنْ أَفْضَلِ إِنْعامِكَ عَلَيْهِمْ

translation.en: Verily, Your duty towards them is one of the greatest blessings that You conferred upon them
And (I beseech You) in the name of the position that You enjoy with them
and in the name of the position that they enjoy with You

segments.arabic: صَلِّ عَلَيْهِمْ يا رَبِّ صَلاةً دائِمَةً مُنْتَهى رِضاكَ

translation.en: to (please) bless them, O Lord, with blessings that are as endless until they attain Your pleasure,

segments.arabic: وَاغْفِرْ لِي بِهِمْ الذُّنُوبَ الَّتِي بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَكَ

translation.en: to forgive me, in their names, the sins that I committed concerning my duties towards You
to make Your creatures to be satisfied with me,
to complete Your favors upon me

segments.arabic: كَما أَتْمَمْتَها عَلى آبائِي مِنْ قَبْلُ

translation.en: in the same way as You had completed them upon my forefathers

segments.arabic: وَلاتَجْعَلْ لاَحَدٍ مِنَ المَخْلُوقِينَ عَلَيَّ فِيها امْتِناناً

translation.en: not to cause any of Your creatures to have any role in these favors

segments.arabic: وَامْنُنْ عَلَيَّ كَما مَنَنْتَ عَلى آبائِي مِنْ قَبْلُ

translation.en: to confer upon me with graces in the same way as You had already done with my forefathers
O kaf-ha’-ya’-`ayn-sad

segments.arabic: اللّهُمَّ كَما صَلَّيْتَ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِهِ

translation.en: O Allah, in the same way as You have poured blessings upon Mu¦ammad and his Household,
(please) respond to my entreaty as regards that which I have asked from You,

segments.arabic: يا كَرِيمُ يا كَرِيمُ يا كَرِيمُ.

translation.en: O All-generous! O All-generous! O All-generous!

text.en: You may then prostrate yourself and say (while prostrating) the following supplicatory prayer:
text.ar: ثم اسجد وقل في سجودك :

segments.arabic: يا مَنْ يَقْدِرُ عَلى حَوائِج السَّائِلينَ

translation.en: O He Who has full power over settling requests of beseechers
Who knows what is hidden in the inner selves of the silent
O He Who does not require explanation.

segments.arabic: يا مَنْ يَعْلَمُ خائِنَةَ الاَعْيُنِ وَماتُخْفِي الصُّدُورُ

translation.en: O He Who knows the stealthy looks and that which the breasts conceal.

segments.arabic: يا مَنْ أَنْزَلَ العَذابَ عَلى قَوْمِ يُونُسَ

translation.en: O He Who decided chastisement to befall the people of (Prophet) Jonah
and as He decided to chastise them
they prayed Him and besought Him;

segments.arabic: فَكَشَفَ عَنْهُمْ العَذابَ وَمَتَّعَهُمْ إِلى حِينٍ

translation.en: so, He removed from them the chastisement and He gave them provision until a time.
You do see where I am
You do hear my prayer,
You do know what I conceal, what I declare, and in what situation I am
(please) send blessings upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad
and relieve me from all that which has aggrieved me
from the affairs of my religion, my worldly life, and my Next Life

text.en: You may then repeat the following statement seventy times:
text.ar: ثم قل سبعين مرة :

translation.en: O my Master

text.en: You may then raise your head from the state of prostration and say the following supplicatory prayer:
text.ar: ثم ارفع رأسك من السجود وقل :

segments.arabic: يا رَبِّ أَسْأَلُكَ بَرَكَةَ هذا المَوْضِعِ

translation.en: O my Lord, I pray You to grant me the blessing of this place
and the blessing of its people
And I pray You to grant me sustenance from Your sustenance
sustenance that is legal and pleasant
and that You bring it to me by Your might and power
while I experience good health
O most merciful of all those who show mercy

text.en: In the book of al-Mazar al-Qadim, it is mentioned that the following supplication may be said immediately after the abovementioned supplication that ends with ya karimu ya karimu ya karimu:
text.ar: أقول : قد ورد في كتاب (المزار القديم) في الدعاء في هذا المقام بعد كلمة : يا كَرِيمُ يا كَرِيمُ يا كَرِيمُ ، وقبل السجود دعاء : <ref=4:7>اللّهم يا من تحلّ به عقد المكاره</ref>

text.en: This supplication, which has been mentioned in the first section of this book under the title of du’a’ al-amn (Supplication for Security), is one of the supplications of al-Sahifah al-Sajjadiyyah. <ref=4:3954>The seventh prayer of Sahifa Sajjadieh</ref>. The writer of al-Mazar al-Qadim has added that the following supplication may be said:
text.ar: ثم قال صاحب المزار ثم قل :

translation.en: O Allah, You verily know while I do not
You have power (over all things) while I do not
and You are the Knower of all unseen things.

segments.arabic: صَلِّ اللّهُمَّ عَلى مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ

translation.en: (Please) send blessings upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad
forgive me, have mercy upon me
overlook my sins
and confer upon me with that which is befitting You
O most merciful of all those who show mercy

text.en: You may then prostrate yourself and say (while prostrating) the following supplicatory prayer:
text.ar: ثم اسجد وقل :

segments.arabic: يا مَنْ يَقْدِرُ عَلى حَوائِج السَّائِلينَ

translation.en: O He Who has full power over settling requests of beseechers
Who knows what is hidden in the inner selves of the silent
O He Who does not require explanation.

segments.arabic: يا مَنْ يَعْلَمُ خائِنَةَ الاَعْيُنِ وَماتُخْفِي الصُّدُورُ

translation.en: O He Who knows the stealthy looks and that which the breasts conceal.

segments.arabic: يا مَنْ أَنْزَلَ العَذابَ عَلى قَوْمِ يُونُسَ

translation.en: O He Who decided chastisement to befall the people of (Prophet) Jonah
and as He decided to chastise them
they prayed Him and besought Him;

segments.arabic: فَكَشَفَ عَنْهُمْ العَذابَ وَمَتَّعَهُمْ إِلى حِينٍ

translation.en: so, He removed from them the chastisement and He gave them provision until a time.
You do see where I am
You do hear my prayer,
You do know what I conceal, what I declare, and in what situation I am
(please) send blessings upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad
and relieve me from all that which has aggrieved me
from the affairs of my religion, my worldly life, and my Next Life

text.en: Be it known to you that the narrations carrying the merits of the seventh column in Masjid al-Kufah are numerous.
text.ar: واعلم أيضاً أن ما ورد من الروايات في فضل الأسطوانة السابعة كثير.

text.en: For instance, al-Kulayni has reported through an authoritative chain of authority that Imam ‘Ali Amir al-Mu’minin (‘a) used to offer prayers next to the seventh column and the distance between him and this column was so close that one could hardly pass from there.
text.ar: وقد روى الكليني بسند معتبر أنه كان أمير المؤمنين عليه‌السلام يصلي إلى الأسطوانة السابعة بينه وبين السابعة ممرُّ عنزٍ . وفي رواية معتبرة أخرى أنه ينزل في كل ليلة ستون ألف ملك فيصلون عند الأسطوانة السابعة فلا يعود منهم ملك إلى يوم القيامة. وفي حديث معتبر عن الصادق عليه‌السلام أن الأسطوانة السابعة هي مقام إبراهيم عليه‌السلام.

text.en: According to another validly reported narration, each night sixty thousand angels descend from the heavens to offer prayer at the seventh column and none of them returns until the Resurrection Day.
text.ar: وروى الكليني أيضاً في الكافي بسند صحيح عن أبي إسماعيل السراج قال : قال معاوية بن وهب وأخذ بيدي وقال : قال لي أبو حمزة الثمالي وأخذ بيدي ، وقال : قال لي أصبغ بن نباتة وأخذ بيدي فأراني الأسطوانة السابعة فقال : هذا مقام أمير المؤمنين عليه‌السلام قال : وكان الحسن عليه‌السلام يصلي عند الأسطوانة الخامسة فإذا غاب أمير المؤمنين عليه‌السلام صلّى فيها الحسن عليه‌السلام وهي من باب كندة. وبالاجمال فالرِّوايات في فضلها جمّة ونحن نبغي الاختصار.

text.en: According to another validly reported tradition, Imam al-Sadiq (‘a) has said, “The seventh column is the standing-place of Prophet Abraham (‘a).”

In his book of al-Kafi, Shaykh al-Kulayni has also narrated that Abu-Isma’il al-Sarraj reported that Mu’awiyah ibn Wahab took him from the arm to the seventh column and said that Abu-Hamzah al-Thumali had taken him from the arm to this seventh column and said that al-Asbagh ibn Nubatah had taken him from the arm to this seventh column and said, “This is the place where Imam ‘Ali Amir al-Mu’minin (‘a) used to be. Imam al-Hasan (‘a) used to offer prayers at the fifth column, but when Imam ‘Ali (‘a) would be absent, al-Hasan (‘a) would take his place at the seventh column, which is at bab kindah (the Gate of Kindah).”
In conclusion, the narrations reporting the merits of the seventh column at Masjid al-Kufah are numerous; but because my course in this book is to sum up as much as possible, I will mention no more.
Aamal At The Fifth Column (SUTOON)
text.ar: أعمال الأسطوانة الخامسة :

text.en: The fifth column in al-Kufah Mosque is one of the peculiar places; therefore, it is advised not to miss offering a prayer there and not to miss supplicating Almighty Allah for granting the personal requests. According to an authoritative narration, Prophet Abraham (‘a) offered a prayer at this place.
text.ar: إعلم أنّ من المقامات ذوات المزيّة في جامع الكوفة الأسطوانة الخامسة ينبغي أن تصلى عندها وتطلب المسألات ، ففي رواية معتبرة أنّها بقعة صلى فيها إبراهيم خليل الرحمن عليه‌السلام ولا ينافي هذا ما في سائر الروايات فلعله عليه‌السلام كان قد صلى في مختلف هذه المواضع الواردة في مختلف الروايات. وفي رواية معتبرة عن الصادق عليه‌السلام قال : الأسطوانة الخامسة هي مقام جبرائيل عليه‌السلام
