translation.en: Peace be upon the one who caused a ruined well in the desert to spring forth with water.
Peace be upon the one who moved a rock
after strong men could not even move it.
Peace be upon the one who spoke to a serpent
from the minbar (pulpit) of al-Kufah Mosque with an eloquent language.
Peace be upon the one who spoke to a wolf
and spoke to a skull
whose bones were decayed in al-Nahrawan.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَىٰ صَاحِبِ ٱلشَّفَاعَةِ فِي يَوْمِ ٱلْوَرَىٰ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `ala sahibi alshshafa`ati fi yawmi alwara

translation.en: Peace be upon the one granted the right of intercession on the Assembly Day.
Allah’s mercy and blessings be upon him, too.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَىٰ ٱلإِمَامِ ٱلزَّكِيِّ حَلِيفِ ٱلْمِحْرَابِ

translation.en: Peace be upon the pure leader who stuck to the mihrab (prayer niche).
Peace be upon the owner of astounding miracles
and who speaks with wisdom and sagacity.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَىٰ مَنْ عِنْدَهُ تَأْوِيلُ ٱلْمُحْكَمِ وَٱلْمُتَشَابِهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon the one who holds the interpretation of the decisive and the allegorical [verses of the Holy Qur'an]
and holds the Basis of the [Holy] Book.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَىٰ مَنْ رُدَّتْ عَلَيْهِ ٱلشَّمْسُ

translation.en: Peace be upon the one to whom the sun was returned
after it had disappeared beyond the veils of night.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَىٰ مُحْيِي ٱللَّيْلِ ٱلْبَهِيمِ بِٱلتَّهَجُّدِ وَٱلِٱكْتِئَابِ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `ala muhyi allayli albahimi bilttahajjudi wal'ikti'abi

translation.en: Peace be upon the one who spent the dark nights with acts of worship and ponderation.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَىٰ مَنْ خَاطَبَهُ جَبْرَئِيلُ بِإِمْرَةِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ بِغَيْرِ ٱرْتِيَابٍ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `ala man khatabahu jabra'ilu bi'imrati almu'minina bighayri irtiyabin

translation.en: Peace be upon the one who was addressed by Archangel Gabriel as the Commander of the Faithful, without any doubt.
Allah’s mercy and blessings be upon him, too.
Peace be upon the master of masters.
Peace be upon the owner of miracles.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَىٰ مَنْ عَجِبَ مِنْ حَمَلاتِهِ فِي ٱلْحُرُوبِ مَلائِكَةُ سَبْعِ سَمَاوَاتٍ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `ala man `ajiba min hamalatihi fi alhurubi mala'ikatu sab`i samawatin

translation.en: Peace be upon the one whose campaigns in the battles were a source of astonishment for the angels of seven heavens.
Peace be upon the one who, when he consulted the Holy Messenger,
offered something in charity before his consultation.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَىٰ أَمِيرِ ٱلْجُيُوشِ وَصَاحِبِ ٱلْغَزَوَاتِ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `ala amiri aljuyushi wa sahibi alghazawati

translation.en: Peace be upon the commander of the armies and the leader of the battles.
Peace be upon the one who spoke to a wolf in the desert.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَىٰ نُورِ ٱللَّهِ فِي ٱلظُّلُمَاتِ

translation.en: Peace be upon the light of Allah in murk.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَىٰ مَنْ رُدَّتْ لَهُ ٱلشَّمْسُ

translation.en: Peace be upon the one to whom the sun was returned
so that he could made up the prayer that he had missed.
Allah’s mercy and blessings be upon him, too.
Peace be upon the Commander of the Faithful.
Peace be upon the chief of the Prophets’ successors.
Peace be upon the example of the pious ones.
Peace be upon the inheritor of the Prophets’ knowledge.
Peace be upon the head of the religion.
Peace be upon the shelter of the believers.
Peace be upon the pattern of the veracious ones.
Allah’s mercy and blessings be upon him, too.
Peace be upon the argument of the upright ones.
Peace be upon the father of the Immaculate Imams.
Peace be upon the one whom was given [the sword of] Dhu’l-Faqar exclusively.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَىٰ سَاقِي أَوْلِيَائِهِ مِنْ حَوْضِ ٱلنَّبِيِّ ٱلْمُخْتَارِ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `ala saqi awliya'ihi min hawdi alnnabiyyi almukhtari

translation.en: Peace be upon the one who shall water his adherents from the Pond of the Chosen Prophet,

segments.arabic: صَلَّىٰ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ مَا ٱطَّرَدَ ٱللَّيْلُ وَٱلنَّهَارُ
segments.transliteration: salla allahu `alayhi wa alihi ma ittarada allaylu walnnaharu

translation.en: may Allah’s blessings be upon him and his Household as long as there is night and day coming after one another.
Peace be upon the Great News.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَىٰ مَنْ أَنْزَلَ ٱللَّهُ فِيهِ:

translation.en: Peace be upon the one about whom the following verse was revealed by Almighty Allah:

segments.arabic: ”وَإِنَّهُ فِي أُمِّ ٱلْكِتَابِ لَدَيْنَا لَعَلِيٌّ حَكِيمٌ.“
segments.transliteration: wa innahu fi ummi alkitabi ladayna la`aliyyun hakimun

translation.en: “And, indeed, in the origin of the Book with Us, it is decided that `Ali is wise.”
Peace be upon the straight path of Allah.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَىٰ ٱلْمَنْعُوتِ فِي ٱلتَّوْرَاةِ وَٱلإِنْجِيلِ وَٱلْقُرْآنِ ٱلْحَكِيمِ
segments.transliteration: alssalamu `ala alman`uti fi alttawrati wal-injili walqur'ani alhakimi

translation.en: Peace be upon the one whose characteristics are mentioned in the Torah, the Bible, and the Wise Qur'an.
Allah’s mercy and blessings be upon him, too.

text.en: You may then throw yourself on the tomb, kiss it, and say the following:
text.ar: ثُمَّ انكب عَلى الضريح وَقبّله وَقل :

translation.en: O trustee of Allah!
O argument of Allah!
O saint of Allah!
O path to Allah!
I, your servant and adherent, have visited you,
resorted to you tomb,
resided in your courtyard,
sought nearness to Allah the Almighty and All-majestic in the name of you,
and sought your intercession for me before Allah,
I am visiting you so sincerely that I have deserted my friends for so
and I have relied upon you after Almighty Allah.
I bear witness that you are the Mountain Tur (mount of revelation),
the Written Book,
the outstretched fine parchment,
and the swollen ocean of knowledge.
O saint of Allah!

segments.arabic: إِنَّ لِكُلِّ مَزُورٍ عِنَايَةً فِي مَنْ زَارَهُ وَقَصَدَهُ وَأَتَاهُ
segments.transliteration: inna likulli mazurin `inayatan fi man zarahu wa qasadahu wa atahu

translation.en: Indeed, each visited person is expected to pay special attention to the one who visits and comes to him.

segments.arabic: وَأَنَا وَلِيُّكَ وَقَدْ حَطَطْتُ رَحْلِي بِفِنَائِكَ
segments.transliteration: wa ana waliyyuka wa qad hatattu rahli bifina'ika

translation.en: I, your adherent, have resided in your courtyard,
resorted to your holy precinct,
and had recourse to your tomb,

segments.transliteration: li`ilmi bi`azimi manzilatika wa sharafi hadratika

translation.en: because I have full acquaintance with the great standing that you enjoy and the gear honor that you hold.
My sins have overburdened my back
and deprived me of sleep.

segments.transliteration: fama ajidu hirzan wa la ma`qilan

translation.en: Therefore, I can find no shelter, no haven,

segments.arabic: وَلاَ مَلْجَأَ أَلْجَأُ إِلَيْهِ إِلاَّ ٱللَّهُ تَعَالَىٰ
segments.transliteration: wa la malja'an alja'u ilayhi illa allahu ta`ala

translation.en: and no refuge to which I resort save Almighty Allah,
my begging you to be my mediator to Him,
and my seeking your intercession for me before Him.
I am thus residing in your courtyard
and you enjoy a great standing with Almighty Allah
as well as an honorable position;

segments.arabic: فَٱشْفَعْ لِي عِنْدَ ٱللَّهِ رَبِّكَ يَا مَوْلاَيَ
segments.transliteration: fashfa` li `inda allahi rabbika ya mawlaya

translation.en: therefore, (please) intercede for me before your Lord, O master!

text.en: You may then kiss the tomb, turn your face towards the kiblah direction, and say the following supplicatory words:
text.ar: ثم قبّل الضريح واستقبل القبلة وقل :

translation.en: O Allah, I do seek nearness to You,
O most Hearing of all those who can hear!
O most Seeing of all those who can see!
O quickest in reckon!
O most Generous of all those who are generous,
(I seek nearness to You) in the name of Muhammad, the seal of the Prophets
and Your Messengers to all created beings,
and in the name of his brother and cousin,
the hero and erudite,
the manifestly knowledgeable,
`Ali, the Commander of the Faithful,
and (in the names of) al-Hasan and al-Husayn, the two martyred Imams,

segments.transliteration: wa bi`aliyyi bni alhusayni zayni al`abidina

translation.en: and (in the name of) `Ali ibn al-Husayn, the adornment of the worshippers,

segments.arabic: وَبِمُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ بَاقِرِ عِلْمِ ٱلأَوَّلِينَ

translation.en: and (in the name of) Muhammad ibn `Ali, the one who split the knowledge of the old ages,

segments.transliteration: wa bija`fari bni muhammadin zakiyyi alssiddiqina

translation.en: and (in the name of) Ja`far ibn Muhammad, the purest among the veracious ones,

segments.arabic: وَبِمُوسَىٰ بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ ٱلْكَاظِمِ ٱلْمُبِينِ وَحَبِيسِ ٱلظَّالِمِينَ
segments.transliteration: wa bimusa ibni ja`farin alkazimi almubini wa habisi alzzalimina

translation.en: and (in the name of) Musa ibn Ja`far, the manifestly suppressor (of rage) and the imprisoned by the oppressors,

segments.transliteration: wa bi`aliyyi bni musa alrrida al-amini

translation.en: and (in the name of) `Ali ibn Musa, the pleased and trusted,

segments.arabic: وَبِمُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ ٱلْجَوَادِ عَلَمِ ٱلْمُهْتَدِينَ

translation.en: and (in the name of) Muhammad ibn `Ali, the magnanimous and the epitome of the guided ones (to the true path),

segments.arabic: وَبِعَلِيِّ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ ٱلْبَرِّ ٱلصَّادِقِ سَيِّدِ ٱلْعَابِدِينَ
segments.transliteration: wa bi`aliyyi bni muhammadin albarri alssadiqi sayyidi al`abidina

translation.en: and (in the name of) `Ali ibn Muhammad, the upright, the veracious, and the master of worshippers,

segments.arabic: وَبِٱلْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ ٱلْعَسْكَرِيِّ وَلِيِّ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
segments.transliteration: wa bilhasani bni `aliyyin al`askariyyi waliyyi almu'minina

translation.en: and (in the name of) al-Hasan ibn `Ali al-`Askari, the chief of the believers,

segments.arabic: وَبِٱلْخَلَفِ ٱلْحُجَّةِ صَاحِبِ ٱلأَمْرِ مُظْهِرِ ٱلْبَرَاهِينِ
segments.transliteration: wa bilkhalafi alhujjati sahibi al-amri muzhiri albarahini

translation.en: and (in the name of) the Successor, the Argument, the Patron of the Affair, and the Demonstrator of Proofs,

segments.arabic: أَنْ تَكْشِفَ مَا بِي مِنَ ٱلْهُمُومِ
segments.transliteration: an takshifa ma bi min alhumumi

translation.en: (I beseech You in the name of all these) to relieve the grievances that I experience,
to save me from the evils of predetermined ordeals,

segments.transliteration: wa tujirani min alnnari dhati alssamumi

translation.en: and to release me from the hot-winded fire,
(I beseech You) in the name of Your mercy, O most Merciful of all those who show mercy!

text.en: Then, you may pray Almighty Allah to grant you your personal requests, bid farewell to the Imam (‘a), and leave.
text.ar: ثم ادع بما شئت وودعه وانصرف .

text.en: One who intends to leave the holy shrine of Imam `Ali (‘a) may say the following form of bidding farewell, which has been mentioned by the scholars after the previously cited fifth form of ziyarah:
text.ar: فإذا شئت وداعه فودعه بهذا الوداع الذي أورده العلماء تلو ما ذكروه من الزيارة الخامسة :

segments.transliteration: alssalamu `alayka wa rahmatu allahi wa barakatuhu

translation.en: Peace and Allah’s mercy and blessings be upon you.

segments.arabic: أَسْتَوْدِعُكَ ٱللَّهَ وَأَسْتَرْعِيكَ وَأَقْرَأُ عَلَيْكَ ٱلسَّلاَمَ
segments.transliteration: astawdi`uka allaha wa astar`ika wa aqra'u `alayka alssalama

translation.en: I entrust you with Allah and ask Him to keep you under His custody and to send blessings upon you.

segments.arabic: آمَنَّا بِٱللَّهِ وَبِٱلرُّسُلِ وَبِمَا جَاءَتْ بِهِ
segments.transliteration: amanna billahi wa bilrrusuli wa bima ja'at bihi

translation.en: We believe in Allah, in the Messengers, and in what they have conveyed to us,
in that to which they have called, and in that to which they have guided.
So, (please, our Lord,) include us with the witnesses.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ لاَ تَجْعَلْهُ آخِرَ الْعَهْدِ مِنْ زِيَارَتِي إِيَّاهُ
segments.transliteration: allahumma la taj`alhu akhira al`ahdi min ziyarati iyyahu

translation.en: O Allah, (please) do not decide this visit of mine to him (i.e. the Imam) to be the last,
and if you cause me to die before that,
then I bear witness in my death

segments.arabic: عَلَىٰ مَا شَهِدْتُ عَلَيْهِ فِي حَيَاتِي

translation.en: to the same things to which I have born witness in my lifetime:
I bear witness that `Ali the Commander of the Faithful,
and al-Hasan, al-Husayn,
`Ali ibn al-Husayn,
Muhammad ibn `Ali,
Ja`far ibn Muhammad,
Musa ibn Ja`far,
`Ali ibn Musa,
Muhammad ibn `Ali,
`Ali ibn Muhammad,
al-Hasan ibn `Ali,
and al-Hujjah (the Argument of Allah) ibn al-Hasan—
may Your blessings be upon all of them—are my Imams.

segments.arabic: وَأَشْهَدُ أَنَّ مَنْ قَتَلَهُمْ وَحَارَبَهُمْ مُشْرِكُونَ
segments.transliteration: wa ashhadu anna man qatalahum wa harabahum mushrikuna

translation.en: I also bear witness that all of those who have slain and fought against them are polytheists

segments.arabic: وَمَنْ رَدَّ عَلَيْهِمْ فِي أَسْفَلِ دَرَكٍ مِنَ ٱلْجَحِيمِ
segments.transliteration: wa man radda `alayhim fi asfali darakin min aljahimi

translation.en: and that all of those who reject them shall be in the lowest class of Hellfire.

segments.arabic: وَأَشْهَدُ أَنَّ مَنْ حَارَبَهُمْ لَنَا أَعْدَاءٌ
segments.transliteration: wa ashhadu anna man harabahum lana a`da'un

translation.en: I also bear witness that those who have fought against the Imams are our enemies
and we disavow them,
because they are indeed the party of Satan.

segments.arabic: وَعَلَىٰ مَنْ قَتَلَهُمْ لَعْنَةُ ٱللَّهِ وَٱلْمَلائِكَةِ وَٱلنَّاسِ أَجْمَعِينَ
segments.transliteration: wa `ala man qatalahum la`natu allahi walmala'ikati walnnasi ajma`ina

translation.en: The curse of Allah, His angels and all the peoples be upon those who killed the Imams,

segments.arabic: وَمَنْ شَرِكَ فِيهِمْ وَمَنْ سَرَّهُ قَتْلُهُمْ
segments.transliteration: wa man sharika fihim wa man sarrahu qatluhum

translation.en: and upon those who had any role in killing them and those who were pleased for their having been killed.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ بَعْدَ ٱلصَّلاَةِ وَٱلتَّسْلِيمِ
segments.transliteration: allahumma inni as'aluka ba`da alssalati walttaslimi

translation.en: O Allah, as I begin with invoking Your peace and blessings upon them,
I pray you to bless Muhammad, `Ali,
Fatimah, al-Hasan, al-Husayn,

segments.transliteration: wa `aliyyin wa muhammadin wa ja`farin wa musa

translation.en: `Ali, Muhammad, Ja`far, Musa,

segments.transliteration: wa `aliyyin wa muhammadin wa `aliyyin walhasani walhujjati

translation.en: `Ali, Muhammad, `Ali, al-Hasan, and al-Hujjah,

segments.arabic: وَلاَ تَجْعَلْهُ آخِرَ ٱلْعَهْدِ مِنْ زِيَارَتِهِ
segments.transliteration: wa la taj`alhu akhira al`ahdi min ziyaratihi

translation.en: and not to decide this visit of me to his tomb to be the last,

segments.arabic: فَإِنْ جَعَلْتَهُ فَٱحْشُرْنِي مَعَ هٰؤُلاَءِ ٱلْمُسَمَّيْنَ ٱلأَئِمَّةِ
segments.transliteration: fa'in ja`altahu fahshurni ma`a ha'ula'i almusammayna al-a'immati

translation.en: and if you decide so, then (please) include me with these Imams to whom I have referred.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ وَذَلِّلْ قُلُوبَنَا لَهُمْ بِٱلطَّاعَةِ وَٱلْمُنَاصَحَةِ
segments.transliteration: allahumma wa dhallil qulubana lahum biltta`ati walmunasahati

translation.en: O Allah, (please) cause our hearts to be subservient to them by showing obedience to them, well-wishing,
love for them, support, and submission.

name: Asking Permission for Entering holy shrines

text.en: Asking Permission for Entering the Holy Shrines (Izn Dukhool)
text.ar: في ذكر الاستئذان للدخول في كل من الروضات الشريفة

text.en: Let us mention only two forms of asking permission for entering the holy shrines (isti’dhan). First: In the word of Shaykh al-Kaf’ami, when you intend to enter the Holy Prophet’s Mosque in al-Madinah or any of the holy shrines of the Imams (‘a), you may say the following:
text.ar: وهنا نثبت استئذانين : الأول : قال الكفعمي : إذا أردت دخول مسجد النبي صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله أو أحد المشاهد الشريفة لاحد الأئمة عليهم‌السلام فقل :

translation.en: O Allah, I am standing at one of the doors
of Your Prophet’s Houses—
may Your blessings be upon him and his Household.

segments.arabic: وَقَدْ مَنَعْتَ ٱلنَّاسَ انْ يَدْخُلُوٱ إِلاَّ بِإِذْنِهِ
segments.transliteration: wa qad mana`ta alnnasa an yadkhulu illa bi'idhnihi

translation.en: And You have prevented people to enter there before they obtain his permission.
You have thus said, “O ye who believe!

segments.arabic: لاََ تَدْخُلُوٱ بُيُوتَ ٱلنَّبِيِّ إِلاَّ انْ يُؤْذَنَ لَكُمْ“
segments.transliteration: la tadkhulu buyuta alnnabiyyi illa an yu'dhana lakum

translation.en: Enter not the Prophet's houses until leave is given you.”

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي اعْتَقِدُ حُرْمَةَ صَاحِبِ هٰذَا ٱلْمَشْهَدِ ٱلشَّريفِ فِي غَيْبَتِهِ
segments.transliteration: allahumma inni a`taqidu hurmata sahibi hadha almashhadi alshsharifi fi ghaybatihi

translation.en: O Allah, I believe in the sanctity of the owner of this holy shrine in his absence
as same as I believe in it in his presence.

segments.arabic: وَاعْلَمُ انَّ رَسُولَكَ وَخُلَفَاءَكَ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلسَّلاَمُ
segments.transliteration: wa a`lamu anna rasulaka wa khulafa'aka `alayhimu alssalamu

translation.en: I also know for sure that Your Messenger and Your Representatives—peace be upon them—
are live, finding their sustenance in the presence of You;
they can see my place,
hear my words,
and respond to my greetings,
but You have prevented my hearing from receiving their words

segments.transliteration: wa fatahta baba fahmi biladhidhi munajatihim

translation.en: and You have opened the door of my understanding to taste the fine flavor of my confidential speech with

segments.transliteration: wa inni asta'dhinuka ya rabbi awwalan

translation.en: I thus ask Your permission—O my Lord—first of all,

segments.arabic: وَاسْتَاذِنُ رَسُولَكَ صَلَّىٰ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ ثَانِياً
segments.transliteration: wa asta'dhinu rasulaka salla allahu `alayhi wa alihi thaniyan

translation.en: the permission of Your Prophet—peace be upon him and his Household—secondly,
the permission of Your Representative, the Imam
the obedience to whom is incumbent upon me…

text.en: You may now mention the name of the Imam that you are visiting his holy shrine and also mention the name of his father. For instance, if you are visiting the holy shrine of Imam al-Husayn (‘a), you may say, “al-Husayn ibn ‘Ali - peace be upon him.” And if you are visiting the holy shrine of Imam al-Rida (‘a), you may say, “‘Ali ibn Musa al-Rida - peace be upon him” and so on. You may then say:
text.ar: واذكر اسم الإمام الذي تزوره واسم أبيه. فقل في زيارة الحسين عليه‌السلام مثلاً : الحُسَيْنَ بْنَ عَلِيٍّ عليه‌السلام. وفي زيارة الإمام الرضا عليه‌السلام : عَلِيّ بْنَ مُوسى الرِّضا عليه‌السلام وهكذا. ثم قل :

segments.arabic: وَٱلْمَلاَئِكَةَ ٱلْمُوَكَّلينَ بِهٰذِهِ ٱلْبُقْعَةِ ٱلْمُبَارَكَةِ ثَالِثاً
segments.transliteration: walmala'ikata almuwakkalina bihadhihi albuq`ati almubarakati thalithan

translation.en: …and the permission of the angels whom are commissioned to supervise this blessed area, thirdly.
May I enter, O Messenger of Allah?
May I enter, O Argument of Allah?
May I enter, O angels of Allah—the intimate,
the residing in this shrine?

segments.arabic: فَاذَنْ لِي يَا مَوْلاَيَ فِي ٱلدُّخُولِ
segments.transliteration: fa'dhan li ya mawlaya fi alddukhuli

translation.en: So, (please do) permit me to enter, O my Master,

segments.arabic: افْضَلَ مَا اذِنْتَ لاِحَدٍ مِنْ اوْلِيَائِكَ
segments.transliteration: afdala ma adhinta li'ahadin min awliya'ika

translation.en: in the best way of permission that you have ever conferred upon any of your intimate adherents.
If I do not deserve such permission,
then you are worthy of conferring it upon me.

text.en: You may then kiss the holy doorstep (of the shrine) and enter, saying:
text.ar: ثم قبل العتبة الشريفة وادخل وقل :

translation.en: In the Name of Allah (I begin), in Allah (I trust),
on the way of Allah (I proceed),
and on the norm of the Messenger of Allah,
may Allah bless him and his Household.
O Allah, (please do) forgive me,
have mercy upon me, and accept my repentance,
for You are verily the Oft-Returning, the All-Merciful.

text.en: Second: Following is the form of asking permission that is reported by `Allamah al-Majlisi—may Allah sanctify his soul—from an old copy of a book written by one of our old scholars who had mentioned that it is a form of asking permission to enter the Holy Vault (Sirdab) and the illuminative shrines of the Holy Imams (‘a):
text.ar: الثاني : الاستئذان الذي رواه المجلسي قدّس الله سرّه عن نسخة قديمة من مؤلفات الأصحاب للدخول في السرداب المقدس وفي البقاع المنورة للأئمة عليهم‌السلام وهو هذا تقول :

translation.en: O Allah, this is verily an area that You have purified,
a courtyard that You have honored,
and landmarks that You have ennobled
when You have shown therein the proofs on Your Oneness
and the ghosts of the Glorious Throne
whom You have selected as kings maintaining the System (of the universe),
You have chosen them as chiefs of all beings,
and You have sent them for standing out firmly for justice

segments.arabic: فِي ٱبْتِدَاءِ ٱلْوُجُودِ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَامَةِ
segments.transliteration: fi ibtida'i alwujudi ila yawmi alqiyamati

translation.en: since the beginning of existence and up to the Resurrection Day.
You have then conferred upon them with the grace of their representing Your Prophets
in the matters of maintaining Your rules and laws.
You have thus perfected, through assigning them as successors of Your Prophets, the Mission of the warning Prophets

segments.arabic: كَمَا اوْجَبْتَ رِيَاسَتَهُمْ فِي فِطَرِ ٱلْمُكَلَّفِينَ
segments.transliteration: kama awjabta ri'asatahum fi fitari almukallafina

translation.en: in the same way as You have made their leadership incumbent since the first creation of all peoples who are responsible for carrying out Your duties.
Glory be to You; what a Compassionate Lord Your are!

segments.arabic: وَلاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ انْتَ مِنْ مَلِكٍ مَا اعْدَلَكَ
segments.transliteration: wa la ilaha illa anta min malikin ma a`dalaka

translation.en: There is no god save You; what a Just King You are!

segments.arabic: حَيْثُ طَابَقَ صُنْعُكَ مَا فَطَرْتَ عَلَيْهِ ٱلْعُقُولَ
segments.transliteration: haythu tabaqa sun`uka ma fatarta `alayhi al`uqula

translation.en: Your making has corresponded with all that on which You have created the intellects,

segments.arabic: وَوَافَقَ حُكْمُكَ مَا قَرَّرْتَهُ فِي ٱلْمَعْقُولِ وَٱلْمَنْقُولِ
segments.transliteration: wa wafaqa hukmuka ma qarrartahu fi alma`quli walmanquli

translation.en: and Your laws have concurred with that which You have decided, including the logic and the narrated.

segments.arabic: فَلَكَ ٱلْحَمْدُ عَلَىٰ تَقْدِيرِكَ ٱلْحَسَنِ ٱلْجَمِيلِ

translation.en: Praise be to You for Your arrangement—the fine and excellent.

segments.arabic: وَلَكَ ٱلشُّكْرُ عَلَىٰ قَضَائِكَ ٱلْمُعَلَّلِ بِاكْمَلِ ٱلتَّعْلِيلِ
segments.transliteration: wa laka alshshukru `ala qada'ika almu`allali bi'akmali altta`lili

translation.en: Thanks be to You for Your ordain that is outstandingly founded.

segments.arabic: فَسُبْحَانَ مَنْ لاََ يُسْالُ عَنْ فِعْلِهِ

translation.en: Glory be to Him Whom is not asked about whatever He does,
and Whom is not disputed about whatever He decides.

segments.arabic: وَسُبْحَانَ مَنْ كَتَبَ عَلَىٰ نَفْسِهِ ٱلرَّحْمَةَ
segments.transliteration: wa subhana man kataba `ala nafsihi alrrahmata

translation.en: Glory be to Him Who has inscribed mercy for Himself
before He started creation.
Praise be to Allah Who has conferred upon us
with the favor of assigning rulers who represent Him
as if He is present therein.

segments.arabic: وَلاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِي شَرَّفَنَا بِاوْصِيَاءَ
segments.transliteration: wa la ilaha illa allahu alladhi sharrafana bi'awsiya'a

translation.en: There is no god save Allah Who has honored us with Successors
maintaining the (Divine) laws in all ages.
Allah is the Most Great for He has demonstrated them before us
through miracles that neither men nor can the jinn do.

segments.arabic: لاََ حَوْلَ وَلاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاَّ بِٱللَّهِ ٱلْعَلِيِّ ٱلْعَظيمِ
segments.transliteration: la hawla wa la quwwata illa billahi al`aliyyi al`azimi

translation.en: There is no power and no might save with Allah, the Most High, the All-great,

segments.arabic: ٱلَّذِي اجْرَانَا عَلَىٰ عَوَائِدِهِ ٱلْجَمِيلَةِ فِي ٱلامَمِ ٱلسَّالِفينَ
segments.transliteration: alladhi ajrana `ala `awa'idihi aljamilati fi al'umami alssalifina

translation.en: Who has accustomed us to receive His favorable donations that He did to the ancient nations.
O Allah, all praise be to You and elevated thanks be to You
as eternal existence has been confirmed for Your Face,

segments.transliteration: wa kama ja`alta nabiyyana khayra alnnabiyyina

translation.en: and as You have chosen our Prophet to be the best of Prophets
and our kings to be the best of the creatures,
and You have chosen them aforetime above the nations knowingly.

segments.arabic: وَفِّقْنَا للِسَّعْيِ إِلَىٰ ابْوَابِهِمُ ٱلْعَامِرَةِ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلدِّينِ
segments.transliteration: waffiqna lilssa`yi ila abwabihim al`amirati ila yawmi alddini

translation.en: (Please do) grant us prosperity to stand at the doorsteps of their flourishing shrines up to the Resurrection Day,

segments.arabic: وَٱجْعَلْ ارْوَاحَنَا تَحِنُّ إِلَىٰ مَوْطِئِ اقْدَامِهِمْ
segments.transliteration: waj`al arwahana tahinnu ila mawti'i aqdamihim

translation.en: make our souls long for the places of their feet,

segments.arabic: وَنُفُوسَنَا تَهْوَىٰ النَّظَرَ إِلَىٰ مَجَالِسِهِمْ وَعَرَصَاتِهِمْ
segments.transliteration: wa nufusana tahwa alnnazara ila majalisihim wa `arasatihim

translation.en: and make our spirits desire for looking at their sessions and courtyards

segments.arabic: حَتَّىٰ كَانَّنَا نُخَاطِبُهُمْ فِي حُضُورِ اشْخَاصِهِمْ
segments.transliteration: hatta ka'nnana nukhatibuhum fi huduri ashkhasihim

translation.en: as if we can talk to them in their presence.

segments.arabic: فَصَلَّىٰ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ مِنْ سَادَةٍ غَائِبينَ

translation.en: May Allah bless them; they are absent masters,
immaculate progeny,
and infallible leaders.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ فَاذَنْ لَنَا بِدُخُولِ هٰذِهِ ٱلْعَرَصَاتِ
segments.transliteration: allahumma fa'dhan lana bidukhuli hadhihi al`arasati

translation.en: O Allah, (please do) permit us to enter these courtyards

segments.arabic: ٱلَّتِي ٱسْتَعْبَدْتَ بِزِيَارَتِهَا اهْلَ ٱلارَضِينَ وَٱلسَّمَاوَاتِ
segments.transliteration: allati ista`badta biziyaratiha ahla al-aradina walssamawati

translation.en: that You have ordered the inhabitants of the heavens and the earth to visit them as sign of worship to You,
make our eyes shed tears of reverence for their dignity,

segments.arabic: وَذَلِّلْ جَوَارِحَنَا بِذُلِّ ٱلْعُبُودِيِّةِ وَفَرْضِ ٱلطَّاعَةِ
segments.transliteration: wa dhallil jawarihana bidhulli al`ubudiyyati wa fardi altta`ati

translation.en: and make our organs show humility of servitude and of obligatory obedience (to You),

segments.arabic: حَتَّىٰ نُقِرَّ بِمَا يَجِبُ لَهُمْ مِنَ ٱلاوْصَافِ
segments.transliteration: hatta nuqirra bima yajibu lahum min al-awsafi

translation.en: so that we will confess of the qualities that are fixed for them
and to admit that they are the interceders for the creatures

segments.arabic: إِذَا نُصِبَتِ ٱلْمَوَازِينُ فِي يَوْمِ ٱلاعْرَافِ
segments.transliteration: idha nusibat almawazinu fi yawmi al-a`rafi

translation.en: when the Scale shall be set up on the Heights Day.

segments.arabic: وَٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ وَسَلاَمٌ عَلَىٰ عِبَادِهِ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱصْطَفَىٰ
segments.transliteration: walhamdu lillahi wa salamun `ala `ibadihi alladhina istafa

translation.en: All praise be to Allah and peace be upon His servants that He has chosen;
Muhammad and His Immaculate Household.

text.en: You may then kiss the doorstep with submission and teary eyes, for this is the sign that you have been permitted by the Infallibles—peace be upon them—to enter.
text.ar: ثم قبل العتبة وادخل وأنت خاشع باكٍ فذلك إذن منهم صَلوات الله عَلَيْهم أجمَعين.

text.en: Allamah al-Majlisi, in his book of Tuhfat al-Za’ir, has listed the following comprehensive form of ziyarah as the eighth, saying: This form of ziyarah has been reported by Sayyid Ibn Tawus within the supplicatory prayers of the ‘Arafah Day from Imam al-Sadiq, peace be upon him. It can be said at visiting any shrine at any time, especially on the ‘Arafah Day. It is as follows:
text.ar: ما جعلها العلامة المجلسي (رض) الثامنة من الزيارات الجامعة في كتابه (تحفة الزائر) وقال : هذه زيارة رواها السيد ابن طاووس في خلال أدعية عرفة عن الصادق صلوات الله عليه ، ويزار بها في كل مكان وزمان لا سيّما في عرفة وهي هذه الزيارة

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O Allah’s Messenger.
Peace be upon you, O Allah’s Prophet.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا خِيَرَةَ ٱللَّهِ مِنْ خَلْقِهِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O Allah’s best choice among His creatures
and His trustee on His revelations.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ يَا مَوْلاَيَ يَا أَمِيرَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O my master, O Commander of the Faithful.
Peace be upon you, O my master.
You are Allah’s argument against His creatures,
the door to His knowledge,
the successor of His Prophet,
and the vicegerent after him among his nation.
May Allah curse the people who usurped your right
and took your place.
I repudiate them
and their partisans in your presence.
Peace be upon you, O Fatimah the chaste.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكِ يَا زَيْنَ نِسَاءِ ٱلْعَالَمِينَ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O adornment of the women of the worlds.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكِ يَا بِنْتَ رَسُولَ ٱللَّهِ رَبِّ ٱلْعَالَمِينَ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O daughter of the Messenger of Allah the Lord of the worlds.
May Allah send blessings upon you and him.

segments.arabic: اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكِ يَا أُمَّ ٱلْحَسَنِ وَٱلْحُسَيْنِ

translation.en: Peace be upon you, O mother of al-Hasan and al-Husayn.
May Allah curse the people who usurped your right

segments.arabic: وَمَنَعَتْكِ مَا جَعَلَهُ ٱللَّهُ لَكِ حَلاَلاًَ

translation.en: and deprived you of that which Allah has deemed lawful to you.

segments.arabic: أَنَا بَريءٌ إِِلَيْكِ مِنْهُمْ وَمِنْ شِيعَتِهِمْ

translation.en: I repudiate them and their partisans in your presence.
Peace be upon you, O my master
Abu-Muhammad al-Hasan the bright.
Peace be upon you, O my master.
May Allah curse the people who killed you,
swore allegiance to each other against you, and helped each other against you.

segments.arabic: أَنَا بَرِيءٌ إِِلَيْكَ مِنْهُمْ وَمِنْ شِيعَتِهِمْ

translation.en: I repudiate them and their partisans in your presence.
Peace be upon you, O my master

segments.arabic: يَا أَبَا عَبْدِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلْحُسَيْنُ بْنَ عَلِيٍّ

translation.en: O Abu-`Abdullah al-Husayn the son of `Ali.
Allah’s blessings be upon you,
your father, and your grandfather Muhammad,
may Allah send blessings upon him and his Household.
May Allah curse the people who violated the shedding of your blood.
May Allah curse the people who slew you
and violated the sacredness of your women.
May Allah curse their partisans and followers.
May Allah curse those who paved the way to them
to fight against you.

segments.arabic: أَنَا بَرِيءٌ إِِلَىٰ ٱللَّهِ وَإِِلَيْكَ مِنْهُمْ

translation.en: I repudiate them in the presence of Allah and you.
Peace be upon you, O my master

segments.arabic: يَا أَبَا مُحَمَّدٍ عَلِيُّ بْنَ ٱلْحُسَيْنِ

translation.en: Abu-Muhammad `Ali the son of al-Husayn.
Peace be upon you, O my master

segments.arabic: يَا أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ مُحَمَّدُ بْنَ عَلِيٍّ

translation.en: Abu-Ja`far Muhammad the son of `Ali.
Peace be upon you, O my master

segments.arabic: يَا أَبَا عَبْدِ ٱللَّهِ جَعْفَرُ بْنَ مُحَمَّدٍ

translation.en: Abu-`Abdullah Ja`far the son of Muhammad.
Peace be upon you, O my master

segments.arabic: يَا أَبَا ٱلْحَسَنِ مُوسَىٰ بْنَ جَعْفَرٍ

translation.en: Abu’l-Hasan Musa ibn Ja`far.
Peace be upon you, O my master

segments.arabic: يَا أَبَا ٱلْحَسَنِ عَلِيُّ بْنَ موُسَىٰ

translation.en: Abu’l-Hasan `Ali ibn Musa.
Peace be upon you, O my master

segments.arabic: يَا أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ مُحَمَّدُ بْنَ عَلِيٍّ

translation.en: Abu-Ja`far Muhammad the son of `Ali.
Peace be upon you, O my master

segments.arabic: يَا أَبَا ٱلْحَسَنِ عَلِيُّ بْنَ مُحَمَّدٍ

translation.en: Abu’l-Hasan `Ali the son of Muhammad.
Peace be upon you, O my master

segments.arabic: يَا أَبَا مُحَمَّدٍ ٱلْحَسَنُ بْنَ عَلِيٍّ

translation.en: Abu-Muhammad al-Hasan the son of `Ali.
Peace be upon you, O my master

segments.arabic: يَا أَبَا ٱلْقَاسِمِ مُحَمَّدُ بْنَ ٱلْحَسَنِ

translation.en: Abu’l-Qasim Muhammad the son of al-Hasan
the patron of the age.
May Allah send blessings upon you
and upon your family, the immaculate and pure.
O my masters, be my intercessors
in the forgiveness of my burdens and sins.
I believe in Allah in what has been revealed to you.

segments.arabic: وَ أَتَوَالَىٰ آخِرَكُمْ بِمَا أَتَوَالَىٰ أَوَّلَكُمْ

translation.en: I swear to the last of you the same loyalty that I swear to the first.
I repudiate all idols, false deities,
Idol Lat, and Idol al-`Uzza.

segments.arabic: يَا مَوَالِيَّ أَنَا سِلْمٌ لِمَنْ سَالَمَكُمْ

translation.en: O my masters, I am at peace with those who are at peace with you,
I am at war against those who are at war against you,
I am the enemy of those who show enmity towards you,
and I am loyal to those who are loyalists to you
up to the Resurrection Day.
May Allah curse those who have wronged you and usurped your rights.

segments.arabic: وَلَعَنَ ٱللَّهُ أَشْيَاعَهُمْ وَ أَتْبَاعَهُمْ وَ أَهْلَ مَذْهَبِهِمْ

translation.en: May Allah curse their partisans, followers, and people of their sects.

segments.arabic: وَ أَبْرَاءُ إِِلَىٰ ٱللَّهِ وَإِِلَيْكُمْ مِنْهُمْ

translation.en: I repudiate them in the presence of Allah and you.

name: Supplications After the Ziyarat of imams

text.en: Sayyid Ibn Tawus has stated that it is recommended to say the following supplicatory prayer immediately after performing the rites of visitation to the Holy Imams (‘a):
text.ar: قال السيد ابن طاووس يستحب أن يدعى بهذا الدعاء عقيب زيارات الأئمة عليهم‌السلام ، :

segments.arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ اَنْ كَانَتْ ذُنُوْبِيْ قَدْ اَخْلَقَتْ وَجْهِيْ عِنْدَكَ وَ حَجَبَتْ دُعَآئِيْ عَنْكَ وَ حَالَتْ بَيْنِيْ وَ بَيْنَكَ فَاَسْاَلُكَ اَنْ تُقْبِلَ عَلَيَّ بِوَجْهِكَ الْكَرِيْمِ وَ تَنْشُرَ عَلَيَّ رَحْمَتَكَ وَ تُنَزِّلَ عَلَيَّ بَرَكَاتِكَ. وَ اِنْ كَانَتْ قَدْ مَنَعَتْ اَنْ تَرْفَعَ لِيْ اِلَيْكَ صَوْتًا اَوْ تَغْفِرَ لِيْ ذَنْبًا اَوْ تَتَجَاوَزَ عَنْ خَطِيْئَةٍ مُهْلِكَةٍ فَهَا اَنَا ذَا مُسْتَجِيْرٌ بِكَرَمِ وَجْهِكَ وَ عِزِّ جَلَالِكَ مُتَوَسِّلٌ اِلَيْكَ مُتَقَرِّبٌ اِلَيْكَ بِاَحَبِّ خَلْقِكَ اِلَيْكَ وَ اَكْرَمِهِمْ عَلَيْكَ وَ اَوْلَاهُمْ بِكَ وَ اَطْوَعِهِمْ لَكَ وَ اَعْظَمِهِمْ مَنْزِلَةً وَ مَكَانًا عِنْدَكَ مُحَمَّدٍ وَ عِتْرَتِهِ الطَّاهِرِيْنَ الْاَئِمَّةِ الْهُدَاةِ الْمَهْدِيِّيْنَ الَّذِيْنَ فَرَضْتَ عَلٰى خَلْقِكَ طَاعَتَهُمْ وَ اَمَرْتَ بِمَوَدَّتِهِمْ وَ جَعَلْتَهُمْ وُلَاةَ الْاَمْرِ مِنْ بَعْدِ رَسُوْلِكَ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَاٰلِهٖ يَا مُذِلَّ كُلِّ جَبَّارٍ عَنِيْدٍ وَ يَا مُعِزَّ الْمُؤْمِنِيْنَ بَلَغَ مَجْهُوْدِيْ فَهَبْ لِيْ نَفْسِيَ السَّاعَةَ وَ رَحْمَةً مِنْكَ تَمُنُّ بِهَا عَلَيَّ يَا اَرْحَمَ الرَّاحِمِيْنَ.
segments.transliteration: allaahumma in kaanat zonoobee qad akhlaqat wajhee i’ndaka wa hajabat do-a’aa-ee a’nka wa haalat baynee wa baynaka fa-as-aloka an tuqbela a’layya be-wajhekal kareeme wa tanshora a’layya rahmataka wa tonazzela a’layya barakaatek. wa in kaanat qad mana’ta an tarfa-a’ lee elayka sawtan aw taghfera lee zanban aw tata-jaa-waza a’n khatee-atin mohlekatin fahaa anaa zaa mustajeerun bekarame wajheka wa i’zze jalaaleka motawasselun elayka motaqarrebun elayka be-ahabbe khalqeka elayka wa akramehim a’layka wa awlaahum beka wa atwa-e’him laka a-a'zamehim manzelatan wa makaanan i'ndaka mohammadin wa i'tratehit taahereenal aimmatil hodaatil mahdiyyeenal lazeena farazta a'laa khalqeka taa-a'tahum wa amarta bemawaddatehim wa ja-a’ltahum wolaatal amre min ba’de rasooleka sallal laaho a’layhe wa aalehi yaa mozilla kulle jabbaarin a’needin wa yaa mo-i’zzal moameneena balagha majhoodee fahab lee nafseyas saa-a’te wa rahmatan minka tamunno behaa a’layya yaa arhamar raahemeen.

translation.en: O Allah! If my sins have caused my face to be tattered in Your view, my prayers to be screened from You, and put a barrier between You and me, then I beseech You to come to me with Your noble Face, to spread Your mercy upon me, and to pour down Your benedictions on me. And if my sins have impeded from raising my voice to You, impeded from forgiving any of my sins, or overlooking any of my destructive fault, then, here I am seeking shelter with the nobleness of Your Face and the dignity of Your majesty; and (here I am) begging You and seeking nearness to You through the most beloved of Your creatures to You, the most honorable with You, the nearest to You, the most obedient to You, and the owners of the greatest standing and position with You; namely, Muhammad and his immaculate descendants, the Imams, the guide and well-guided, the obedience to whom has been imposed by You on Your creatures, the love for whom has been ordered by You, and whom You have made the men in authority after Your Messenger, Allah's blessings be upon him and his Household. O He Who humiliates every obstinate tyrant! O He Who grants dignity to the faithful believers! I have exerted all my efforts; so, (please) release me at this very moment and grant me mercy from You that You may endow me, O Most Merciful of all.

text.en: You may now kiss the holy tomb, put your two cheeks on it, and say the following words:
text.ar: ثُمَّ قبل الضريح وضع خدّيك عليه وقل :

segments.arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ اِنَّ هٰذَا مَشْهَدٌ لَا يَرْجُوْ مَنْ فَاتَتْهُ فِيْهِ رَحْمَتُكَ اَنْ يَنَالَهَا فِيْ غَيْرِهٖ وَ لَا اَحَدٌ اَشْقٰى مِنِ امْرِئٍ قَصَدَهٗ مُؤَمِّلًا فَاٰبَ عَنْهُ خَآئِبًا. اَللّٰهُمَّ اِنِّيْ اَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ الْاِيَابِ وَخَيْبَةِ الْمُنْقَلَبِ وَ الْمُنَاقَشَةِ عِنْدَ الْحِسَابِ وَ حَاشَاكَ يَا رَبِّ اَنْ تُقْرِنَ طَاعَةَ وَلِيِّكَ بِطَاعَتِكَ وَ مُوَالَاتِهٖ بِـمُوَالَاتِكَ وَ مَعْصِيَتِهٖ بِمَعْصِيَتِكَ ثُمَّ تُؤْيِسَ زَآئِرَهٗ وَ الْمُتَحَمِّلَ مِنْ بُعْدِ الْبِلَادِ اِلٰى قَبْرِهٖ وَ عِزَّتِكَ يَا رَبِّ لَا يَنْعَقِدُ عَلٰى ذٰلِكَ ضَمِيْرِي اِذْ كَانَتِ الْقُلُوْبُ اِلَيْكَ بِالْجَمِيْلِ تُشِيْرُ.
segments.transliteration: allaahumma inna haazaa mash-hadun laa yarjoo man faatatho feehe rahmatoka an yanaalahaa fee ghayrehi wa laa ahadun ashqaa menim re-in qasadahu mo-ammelan fa-aaba a’nho khaaa-ebaa. allaahumma innee a-o’ozo beka min sharril eyaabe wa khaybatil munqalabe wal monaaqashate i’ndal hesaabe wa haashaaka yaa rabbe an tuqrena taa-a’ta waliyyeka be-taa-a’teka wa mowaalaatehi be-mowaalaateka wa ma’seyatehi be-ma’seyateka summa toa-yesa zaaa-erahu wal motahammela min bo’dil belaade elaa qabrehi wa i’zzateka yaa rabbe laa yan-a’qedo a’laa zaaleka zameeree iz kaanatil qoloobo elayka bil-jameele tosheero.

translation.en: O Allah! This is verily a place wherein one who misses Your mercy must never expect to find it elsewhere. None can be more miserable that one who visits this place, bearing hope, but leaves it with disappointment. O Allah! I do seek Your protection against evil return, hopeless retirement, and hard interrogation when I am called to account. It is too far from You, O my Lord! That after You matched the obedience to Your (visited) representative to the obedience to You, the loyalty to him to the loyalty to You, and the disobedience to him to the disobedience to You, You may then shock with despair one who has visited him and who has undergone remoteness from homeland to come to his grave! By Your Honor, O my Lord! I swear; my conscience can never even think of so, because all hearts always have excellent ideas about You.

text.en: You may now offer the prayer of ziyarah. If you intend to bid farewell to the visited holy person and leave, you may say the following words:
text.ar: ثُمَّ صل للزيارة فإذا شئت أن تودع وتنصرف فقل :

segments.arabic: السَّلامُ عَلَيْكُمْ يا أهْلَ بَيْتِ النُّبُوَّةِ وَمَعْدِنَ الرِّسالَةِ سَلامَ مُوَدِّعٍ لا سَئِمٍ وَلا قالٍ وَرَحْمَةُ الله وَبَرَكاتُهُ

text.en: Shaykh al-Mufid has also mentioned these words, but he added: After the statement, you may say the following words:
text.ar: والشيخ المفيد (رض) أيضاً قد ذكر هذا الدعاء ولكنه بعد كلمة (بالجميل تشير) قال : ثُمَّ قل :

segments.arabic: يَا وَلِيَّ اللهِ اِنَّ بَيْنِي وَ بَيْنَ اللهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ ذُنُوْبًا لَا يَاتِيْ عَلَيْهَا اِلَّا رِضَاكَ فَبِحَقِّ مَنِ ائْتَمَنَكَ عَلٰى سِرِّهٖ وَ اسْتَرْعَاكَ اَمْرَ خَلْقِهٖ وَ قَرَنَ طَاعَتَكَ بِطَاعَتِهٖ وَ مُوَالَاتَكَ بِـمُوَالَاتِهٖ تَوَلَّ صَلَاحَ حَالِيْ مَعَ اللهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ وَ اجْعَلْ حَظِّيْ مِنْ زِيَارَتِكَ تَخْلِيْطِيْ بِخَالِصِيْ زُوَّارِكَ الَّذِيْنَ تَسْاَلُ اللهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فِيْ عِتْقِ رِقَابِهِمْ وَ تَرْغَبُ اِلَيْهِ فِيْ حُسْنِ ثَوَابِهِمْ. فَهَا اَنَا الْيَوْمَ بِقَبْرِكَ لَآئِذٌ وَ بِحُسْنِ دِفَاعِكَ عَنِّيْ عَآئِذٌ فَتَلَافِنِيْ يَا مَوْلَايَ وَ اَدْرِكْنِيْ وَ اَسْاَلِ اللهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فِيْ اَمْرِيْ فَاِنَّ لَكَ عِنْدَ اللهِ مَقَامًا كَرِيْـمًا (وَ جَاهًا عَظِيْمًا) صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْكَ وَ سَلَّمَ تَسْلِيْـمًا.
segments.transliteration: yaa waliyyal laahe inna baynee wa baynal laahe a’zza wa jalla zonooban laa yaatee a’layhaa illaa rezaaka fa-be-haqqe manea tamanaka a’laa sirrehi was tar-a’aka amra khalqehi wa qarana taa-a’teka be-taa-a’tehi wa mowaalaataka be-mowaalaatehi tawalla salaaha haalee ma-a’l laahe a’zza wa jalla waj a’l hazzee min zeyaarateka takhleetee be-khaalesee zuwwaarekal lazeena tas-alul laaha a’zza wa jalla fee i’tqe reqaabehim wa tar-ghabo elayhe fee husne sawaabehim. fahaa anal yawma be-qabreka laaa-ezun wa be-husne de-faa-e’ka a’nnee a’aa-ezun fatalaatenee yaa mawlaaya wa adriknee wa as-alil laaha a’zza wa jalla fee amree fa-inna laka i’ndal laahe maqaaman kareeman (wa jaahan a’zeeman) sallal laaho a’layka wa sallama tasleemaa.

translation.en: O representative of Allah! There are sins that I have committed against Allah and nothing can mend them except winning your pleasure. So, I ask you in the name of Him Who has entrusted you with His secret, Who has commended you the affairs of His creation, and Who has matched the obedience to you to the obedience to Him and the loyalty to you to the loyalty to Him, to be in charge of setting aright my relationship with Allah the Almighty and All-majestic, to decide my share of my visit to you to be that you link me to the elite visitors of whom you ask Allah the Almighty and All-majestic to set free (from punishment) and whom you beg Him to grant excellent rewards. Here I am now seeking refuge in your tomb and seeking the protection of your excellent defense of me. So, O my master! Succor me, help me, and ask Allah the Almighty and All-majestic about my condition. Verily, you enjoy an honorable standing with Allah (and a great esteem). May Allah bless you and endue you with thorough benediction.

text.en: Actually, I could not put my hand on an authentically reported form of ziyarah that is dedicated to the holy prophets, except for two forms of ziyarah of Prophet Adam and Prophet Noah, which have been previously cited within the forms of ziyarah of Imam ‘Ali Amir al-Mu’minin (‘a).
text.ar: إعلم أن تكريم الأنبياء عليهم‌السلام وتعظيمهم واجب عقلا وشرعاً لا نفرق بين أحد من رسله ، وزيارتهم راجحة مستحسنة ، والعلماء قد صرّحوا باستحباب زيارتهم. وليس في الأنبياء عليهم‌السلام وإن كثروا من يعرف موضع قبره إِلاّ القليلون وهم على ما أعهد آدم عليه‌السلام ونوح عليه‌السلام وهما مدفونان عند مرقد أمير المؤمنين عليه‌السلام ، وإبراهيم عليه‌السلام وقبره في القدس الخليل قرب بيت المقدس ، وبجواره مراقد سارة زوجته واسحق ويعقوب ويوسف عليه‌السلام ، وإسماعيل عليه‌السلام وأُمه هاجر مدفونان في الحجر في المسجد الحرام وفيه قبور الأنبياء عليهم‌السلام. وعن الباقر (صلوات الله عليه) قال : ما بين الركن والمقام مكتظ بقبور الأنبياء عليهم‌السلام.

text.en: Anyhow, the first comprehensive form of ziyarah (al-Ziyarah al-Jami’ah al-Saghirah) can be said at visiting the tombs of the holy prophets, too, as is inferred from its report. Confirming this fact, the sublime Shaykh Muhammad ibn al-Mashhadi and the magnificent Sayyid Ibn Tawus, in the book of Misbah al-Za’ir as well as other books, have mentioned this form of ziyarah to be said at visiting the shrine of Prophet Jonah within the etiquettes of entering the city of al-Kufah. Most probably, these scholars have mentioned this comprehensive form of ziyarah for visiting this shrine is only because of the generality that is understood from the narration of this form of ziyarah. In other words, the report of this form of ziyarah suggests that it can be said at all shrines.
text.ar: وعن الصادق عليه‌السلام قال : ما بين الركن اليماني والحجر الأسود مراقد سبعين نبيا من الأنبياء عليهم‌السلام .

text.en: At any rate, it is appropriate to say al-Ziyarah al-Jami’ah al-Saghirah, which has been previously cited in this book, when visiting the shrines of the holy prophets.
text.ar: وفي بيت المقدس قبور عدّة من الأنبياء كداود عليه‌السلام وسليمان وغيرهما من الأنبياء المعروفين هناك سلام الله عليهم أجمعين ، وقبر زكريا عليه‌السلام معروف في حلب ، وليونس عليه‌السلام على شريعة الكوفة بقعة ذات قبّة معروفة ، وقبراً هود عليه‌السلام وصالح عليه‌السلام في النجف الأشرف مشهوران ، ومرقد ذي الكفل على شاطئ الفرات مشهور وهو يبعد عن الكوفة ، والنبي جرجيس قبره مدينة الموصل ، وفي خارج المدينة قبر شيت هبة الله وقبر النبي دانيال في شوش ، وقبر يوشع مقابل مسجد براثا وغيرهم سلام الله عليهم أجمعين. وأما كيفية زيارتهم عليهم‌السلام فلم أظفر بزيارة مأثورة تخصهم عداً ما سلف في باب زيارة أمير المؤمنين عليه‌السلام من زيارة آدم ونوح عليه‌السلام ولكن ماجعلناها الاُولى من الزيارات الجامعة يزار بها الأنبياء أيضاً عليه‌السلام كما يبدو من روايتها ، ويشهد لذلك أن الشيخ الجليل محمد ابن المشهدي والسيد الاجل عليّ بن طاووس في مصباح الزائر وغيرهما (رضوان الله عليهم) قد أوردوا هذه الزيارة لمشهد يونس عليه‌السلام عند بيانهم آداب دخول مدينة الكوفة والمظنون أنّ ذكرهم هذه الزيارة لهذا المشهد ليس إِلاّ لما يبدو من العموم من روايتها. وكيف كان فمن المناسب الزيارة بها في المراقد الشريفة للأنبياء عليهم‌السلام. وقد أثبتنا الزيارة فيما سلف فلا حاجة إلى إعادتها هنا فمن شاء فليرجع إلى زيارة الجامعة الأولى وينتفع بفضلها العظيم.

text.en: The descendants of the Holy Imams are the sons of the true kings. Their tombs are sources of divine abundance and benedictions and places where the mercy and care of Almighty Allah are sent down. Scholars have advised of visiting their tombs, which are scattered in the majority of the countries of the Shi’ah and even in small villages, deserts, mountains, and valleys. At all times, these tombs are taken by people as refuge for the distressed, havens for the miserable, shelters for the oppressed, and source of entertainment for withering hearts. They will keep acting this role up to the Resurrection Day. In many of such holy shrines, people have noticed many extraordinary matters, as signs of Almighty Allah’s honoring the one entombed there.
text.ar: وهم أبناء الملوك بالحق وقبورهم منابع الفيض والبركة ومهابط الرحمة والعناية الإلهية والعلماء قد صرّحوا باستحباب زيارة قبورهم وهي والحمد للهِ منتشرة في غالب بلاد الشيعة بل وفي القرى والبراري وأطراف الجبال والأودية وهي دائماً ملاذ المضطرين وملجأ البائسين وغياث المظلومين وتسلية للقلوب الذابلة وستظّل كذلك إلى يوم القيامة وقد برز في كثير من هذه المراقد الشريفة كرامات وخوارق للعادات. ولكن لا يخفى أنّ الزائر إذا شاء أن يشدّ الرحل إلى شي من هذه المراقد موقنا ببلوغه فيض رحمة الله وبكشف كروبه فينبغي أن يحرز فيه شرطين :

text.en: All the same, an individual who intends to visit such holy places, trusting that he will attain items of Almighty Allah’s mercy and will have his agonies relieved, must first of all be sure of two matters that act as conditions of visiting such places:
text.ar: الشرط الأول : جلالة صاحب ذلك المرقد وعظمة شأنه إضافة إلى ماحازه من شرافة النسب وتعرف هذه من كتب الأحاديث والأنساب والتواريخ.

text.en: First Condition: A visitor is required to acquaint himself with the sublimity, great importance, and authentic lineage of the visited person. These matters can be obtained from the books of traditions, lineages, and history.
text.ar: الشرط الثاني : التأكد من صحة نسبة هذا المرقد إليه وما حاز كلا الشرطين من المشاهد قليل جداً ونحن قد أشرنا في كتاب هدية الزائر إلى عدّة مراقد قد اجتمع فيها الشرطان وأشرنا في كتاب نفثة المصدور وكتاب منتهى الآمال إلى مرقد محسن بن الحسين عليه‌السلام.

text.en: Second Condition: A visitor must make sure that the visited tomb is validly ascribed to the person.

Unfortunately, these two conditions cannot be found except in a very few number of places of pilgrimage. In my book of Hadiyyat al-Za’ir, I have referred to a number of shrines that meet these two conditions. In my books of Nafthat al-Masdur and Muntaha al-Amal, I have mentioned something about the tomb of Muhsin ibn al-Husayn.
